General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.
   - When running commands, do not set a timeout yourself — let them run as long as needed (default timeout - 2 minutes is more than enough), and once they finish, review the logs in the file.
   - When running sudo commands (especially package installations like apt-get, yum, npm install, etc.), always run them in the background to avoid timeout issues and permission errors when the process needs to be killed. Use the run_in_background parameter or append & to the command.
   - When CI is failing or user reports failures, consider adding a detailed investigation protocol to your todo list with these steps:
      Step 1: List recent runs with timestamps using: gh run list --repo link-foundation/browser-commander --branch issue-21-dee364614118 --limit 5 --json databaseId,conclusion,createdAt,headSha
      Step 2: Verify runs are after the latest commit by checking timestamps and SHA
      Step 3: For each non-passing run, download logs to preserve them: gh run view {run-id} --repo link-foundation/browser-commander --log > ci-logs/{workflow}-{run-id}.log
      Step 4: Read each downloaded log file using Read tool to understand the actual failures
      Step 5: Report findings with specific errors and line numbers from logs
      This detailed investigation is especially helpful when user mentions CI failures, asks to investigate logs, you see non-passing status, or when finalizing a PR.
      Note: If user says \"failing\" but tools show \"passing\", this might indicate stale data - consider downloading fresh logs and checking timestamps to resolve the discrepancy.
   - When a code or log file has more than 1500 lines, read it in chunks of 1500 lines.
   - When facing a complex problem, do as much tracing as possible and turn on all verbose modes.
   - When you create debug, test, or example/experiment scripts for fixing, always keep them in an examples and/or experiments folders so you can reuse them later.
   - When testing your assumptions, use the experiment scripts, and add it to experiments folder.
   - When your experiments can show real world use case of the software, add it to examples folder.
   - When you face something extremely hard, use divide and conquer — it always helps.

Initial research.
   - When you start, make sure you create detailed plan for yourself and follow your todo list step by step, make sure that as many points from these guidelines are added to your todo list to keep track of everything that can help you solve the issue with highest possible quality.
   - When user mentions CI failures or asks to investigate logs, consider adding these todos to track the investigation: (1) List recent CI runs with timestamps, (2) Download logs from failed runs to ci-logs/ directory, (3) Analyze error messages and identify root cause, (4) Implement fix, (5) Verify fix resolves the specific errors found in logs.
   - When you read issue, read all details and comments thoroughly.
   - When you see screenshots or images in issue descriptions, pull request descriptions, comments, or discussions, use WebFetch tool (or fetch tool) to download the image first, then use Read tool to view and analyze it. IMPORTANT: Before reading downloaded images with the Read tool, verify the file is a valid image (not HTML). Use a CLI tool like 'file' command to check the actual file format. Reading corrupted or non-image files (like GitHub's HTML 404 pages saved as .png) can cause \"Could not process image\" errors and may crash the AI solver process. If the file command shows \"HTML\" or \"text\", the download failed and you should retry or skip the image.
   - When you need issue details, use gh issue view https://github.com/link-foundation/browser-commander/issues/21.
   - When you need related code, use gh search code --owner link-foundation [keywords].
   - When you need repo context, read files in your working directory.
   - When you study related work, study the most recent related pull requests.
   - When issue is not defined enough, write a comment to ask clarifying questions.
   - When accessing GitHub Gists (especially private ones), use gh gist view command instead of direct URL fetching to ensure proper authentication.
   - When you are fixing a bug, please make sure you first find the actual root cause, do as many experiments as needed.
   - When you are fixing a bug and code does not have enough tracing/logs, add them and make sure they stay in the code, but are switched off by default.
   - When you need comments on a pull request, note that GitHub has THREE different comment types with different API endpoints:
      1. PR review comments (inline code comments): gh api repos/link-foundation/browser-commander/pulls/22/comments --paginate
      2. PR conversation comments (general discussion): gh api repos/link-foundation/browser-commander/issues/22/comments --paginate
      3. PR reviews (approve/request changes): gh api repos/link-foundation/browser-commander/pulls/22/reviews --paginate
      IMPORTANT: The command \"gh pr view --json comments\" ONLY returns conversation comments and misses review comments!
   - When you need latest comments on issue, use gh api repos/link-foundation/browser-commander/issues/21/comments --paginate.

Solution development and testing.
   - When issue is solvable, implement code with tests.
   - When implementing features, search for similar existing implementations in the codebase and use them as examples instead of implementing everything from scratch.
   - When coding, each atomic step that can be useful by itself should be commited to the pull request's branch, meaning if work will be interrupted by any reason parts of solution will still be kept intact and safe in pull request.
   - When you test:
      start from testing of small functions using separate scripts;
      write unit tests with mocks for easy and quick start.
   - When you test integrations, use existing framework.
   - When you test solution draft, include automated checks in pr.
   - When issue is unclear, write comment on issue asking questions.
   - When you encounter any problems that you unable to solve yourself (any human feedback or help), write a comment to the pull request asking for help.
   - When you need human help, use gh pr comment 22 --body \"your message\" to comment on existing PR.

   - When you code, follow contributing guidelines.
   - When you commit, write clear message.
   - When you need examples of style, use gh pr list --repo link-foundation/browser-commander --state merged --search [keywords].

General guidelines.
   - When you execute commands, always save their logs to files for easier reading if the output becomes large.

        "content": "     1→# Browser Commander\n     2→\n     3→A universal browser automation library with a unified API across multiple browser engines and programming languages. The key focus is on **stoppable page triggers** - ensuring automation logic is properly mounted/unmounted during page navigation.\n     4→\n     5→## Available Implementations\n     6→\n     7→| Language | Package | Status |\n     8→|----------|---------|--------|\n     9→| JavaScript/TypeScript | [browser-commander](https://www.npmjs.com/package/browser-commander) | [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/browser-commander)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/browser-commander) |\n    10→| Rust | [browser-commander](https://crates.io/crates/browser-commander) | [![crates.io](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/browser-commander)](https://crates.io/crates/browser-commander) |\n    11→\n    12→## Core Concept: Page State Machine\n    13→\n    14→Browser Commander manages the browser as a state machine with two states:\n    15→\n    16→```\n    17→+------------------+                      +------------------+\n    18→|                  |   navigation start   |                  |\n    19→|  WORKING STATE   | -------------------> |  LOADING STATE   |\n    20→|  (action runs)   |                      |  (wait only)     |\n    21→|                  |   <-----------------  |                  |\n    22→+------------------+     page ready       +------------------+\n    23→```\n    24→\n    25→**LOADING STATE**: Page is loading. Only waiting/tracking operations are allowed. No automation logic runs.\n    26→\n    27→**WORKING STATE**: Page is fully loaded (30 seconds of network idle). Page triggers can safely interact with DOM.\n    28→\n    29→## Page Trigger Lifecycle\n    30→\n    31→The library provides a guarantee when navigation is detected:\n    32→\n    33→1. **Action is signaled to stop** (AbortController.abort())\n    34→2. **Wait for action to finish** (up to 10 seconds for graceful cleanup)\n    35→3. **Only then start waiting for page load**\n    36→\n    37→This ensures:\n    38→\n    39→- No DOM operations on stale/loading pages\n    40→- Actions can do proper cleanup (clear intervals, save state)\n    41→- No race conditions between action and navigation\n    42→\n    43→## Getting Started\n    44→\n    45→For installation and usage instructions, see the documentation for your preferred language:\n    46→\n    47→- **JavaScript/TypeScript**: See [js/README.md](js/README.md)\n    48→- **Rust**: See [rust/README.md](rust/README.md)\n    49→\n    50→## Architecture\n    51→\n    52→See [js/src/ARCHITECTURE.md](js/src/ARCHITECTURE.md) for detailed architecture documentation.\n    53→\n    54→## License\n    55→\n    56→[UNLICENSE](LICENSE)\n    57→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.\n</system-reminder>\n"

      "filePath": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1768327375795/README.md",
      "content": "# Browser Commander\n\nA universal browser automation library with a unified API across multiple browser engines and programming languages. The key focus is on **stoppable page triggers** - ensuring automation logic is properly mounted/unmounted during page navigation.\n\n## Available Implementations\n\n| Language | Package | Status |\n|----------|---------|--------|\n| JavaScript/TypeScript | [browser-commander](https://www.npmjs.com/package/browser-commander) | [![npm](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/browser-commander)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/browser-commander) |\n| Rust | [browser-commander](https://crates.io/crates/browser-commander) | [![crates.io](https://img.shields.io/crates/v/browser-commander)](https://crates.io/crates/browser-commander) |\n\n## Core Concept: Page State Machine\n\nBrowser Commander manages the browser as a state machine with two states:\n\n```\n+------------------+                      +------------------+\n|                  |   navigation start   |                  |\n|  WORKING STATE   | -------------------> |  LOADING STATE   |\n|  (action runs)   |                      |  (wait only)     |\n|                  |   <-----------------  |                  |\n+------------------+     page ready       +------------------+\n```\n\n**LOADING STATE**: Page is loading. Only waiting/tracking operations are allowed. No automation logic runs.\n\n**WORKING STATE**: Page is fully loaded (30 seconds of network idle). Page triggers can safely interact with DOM.\n\n## Page Trigger Lifecycle\n\nThe library provides a guarantee when navigation is detected:\n\n1. **Action is signaled to stop** (AbortController.abort())\n2. **Wait for action to finish** (up to 10 seconds for graceful cleanup)\n3. **Only then start waiting for page load**\n\nThis ensures:\n\n- No DOM operations on stale/loading pages\n- Actions can do proper cleanup (clear intervals, save state)\n- No race conditions between action and navigation\n\n## Getting Started\n\nFor installation and usage instructions, see the documentation for your preferred language:\n\n- **JavaScript/TypeScript**: See [js/README.md](js/README.md)\n- **Rust**: See [rust/README.md](rust/README.md)\n\n## Architecture\n\nSee [js/src/ARCHITECTURE.md](js/src/ARCHITECTURE.md) for detailed architecture documentation.\n\n## License\n\n[UNLICENSE](LICENSE)\n",
      "numLines": 57,
      "startLine": 1,
      "totalLines": 57
    }
  }
}

        "content": "     1→# Browser Commander\n     2→\n     3→A universal browser automation library for JavaScript/TypeScript that supports both Playwright and Puppeteer with a unified API. The key focus is on **stoppable page triggers** - ensuring automation logic is properly mounted/unmounted during page navigation.\n     4→\n     5→## Installation\n     6→\n     7→```bash\n     8→npm install browser-commander\n     9→```\n    10→\n    11→You'll also need either Playwright or Puppeteer:\n    12→\n    13→```bash\n    14→# With Playwright\n    15→npm install playwright\n    16→\n    17→# Or with Puppeteer\n    18→npm install puppeteer\n    19→```\n    20→\n    21→## Core Concept: Page State Machine\n    22→\n    23→Browser Commander manages the browser as a state machine with two states:\n    24→\n    25→```\n    26→+------------------+                      +------------------+\n    27→|                  |   navigation start   |                  |\n    28→|  WORKING STATE   | -------------------> |  LOADING STATE   |\n    29→|  (action runs)   |                      |  (wait only)     |\n    30→|                  |   <-----------------  |                  |\n    31→+------------------+     page ready       +------------------+\n    32→```\n    33→\n    34→**LOADING STATE**: Page is loading. Only waiting/tracking operations are allowed. No automation logic runs.\n    35→\n    36→**WORKING STATE**: Page is fully loaded (30 seconds of network idle). Page triggers can safely interact with DOM.\n    37→\n    38→## Quick Start\n    39→\n    40→```javascript\n    41→import {\n    42→  launchBrowser,\n    43→  makeBrowserCommander,\n    44→  makeUrlCondition,\n    45→} from 'browser-commander';\n    46→\n    47→// 1. Launch browser\n    48→const { browser, page } = await launchBrowser({ engine: 'playwright' });\n    49→\n    50→// 2. Create commander\n    51→const commander = makeBrowserCommander({ page, verbose: true });\n    52→\n    53→// 3. Register page trigger with condition and action\n    54→commander.pageTrigger({\n    55→  name: 'example-trigger',\n    56→  condition: makeUrlCondition('*example.com*'), // matches URLs containing 'example.com'\n    57→  action: async (ctx) => {\n    58→    // ctx.commander has all methods, but they throw ActionStoppedError if navigation happens\n    59→    // ctx.checkStopped() - call in loops to check if should stop\n    60→    // ctx.abortSignal - use with fetch() for cancellation\n    61→    // ctx.onCleanup(fn) - register cleanup when action stops\n    62→\n    63→    console.log(`Processing: ${ctx.url}`);\n    64→\n    65→    // Safe iteration - stops if navigation detected\n    66→    await ctx.forEach(['item1', 'item2'], async (item) => {\n    67→      await ctx.commander.clickButton({ selector: `[data-id=\"${item}\"]` });\n    68→    });\n    69→  },\n    70→});\n    71→\n    72→// 4. Navigate - action auto-starts when page is ready\n    73→await commander.goto({ url: 'https://example.com' });\n    74→\n    75→// 5. Cleanup\n    76→await commander.destroy();\n    77→await browser.close();\n    78→```\n    79→\n    80→## URL Condition Helpers\n    81→\n    82→The `makeUrlCondition` helper makes it easy to create URL matching conditions:\n    83→\n    84→```javascript\n    85→import {\n    86→  makeUrlCondition,\n    87→  allConditions,\n    88→  anyCondition,\n    89→  notCondition,\n    90→} from 'browser-commander';\n    91→\n    92→// Exact URL match\n    93→makeUrlCondition('https://example.com/page');\n    94→\n    95→// Contains substring (use * wildcards)\n    96→makeUrlCondition('*checkout*'); // URL contains 'checkout'\n    97→makeUrlCondition('*example.com*'); // URL contains 'example.com'\n    98→\n    99→// Starts with / ends with\n   100→makeUrlCondition('/api/*'); // starts with '/api/'\n   101→makeUrlCondition('*.json'); // ends with '.json'\n   102→\n   103→// Express-style route patterns\n   104→makeUrlCondition('/vacancy/:id'); // matches /vacancy/123\n   105→makeUrlCondition('https://hh.ru/vacancy/:vacancyId'); // matches specific domain + path\n   106→makeUrlCondition('/user/:userId/profile'); // multiple segments\n   107→\n   108→// RegExp\n   109→makeUrlCondition(/\\/product\\/\\d+/);\n   110→\n   111→// Custom function (receives full context)\n   112→makeUrlCondition((url, ctx) => {\n   113→  const parsed = new URL(url);\n   114→  return (\n   115→    parsed.pathname.startsWith('/admin') && parsed.searchParams.has('edit')\n   116→  );\n   117→});\n   118→\n   119→// Combine conditions\n   120→allConditions(\n   121→  makeUrlCondition('*example.com*'),\n   122→  makeUrlCondition('*/checkout*')\n   123→); // Both must match\n   124→\n   125→anyCondition(makeUrlCondition('*/cart*'), makeUrlCondition('*/checkout*')); // Either matches\n   126→\n   127→notCondition(makeUrlCondition('*/admin*')); // Negation\n   128→```\n   129→\n   130→## Page Trigger Lifecycle\n   131→\n   132→### The Guarantee\n   133→\n   134→When navigation is detected:\n   135→\n   136→1. **Action is signaled to stop** (AbortController.abort())\n   137→2. **Wait for action to finish** (up to 10 seconds for graceful cleanup)\n   138→3. **Only then start waiting for page load**\n   139→\n   140→This ensures:\n   141→\n   142→- No DOM operations on stale/loading pages\n   143→- Actions can do proper cleanup (clear intervals, save state)\n   144→- No race conditions between action and navigation\n   145→\n   146→## Action Context API\n   147→\n   148→When your action is called, it receives a context object with these properties:\n   149→\n   150→```javascript\n   151→commander.pageTrigger({\n   152→  name: 'my-trigger',\n   153→  condition: makeUrlCondition('*/checkout*'),\n   154→  action: async (ctx) => {\n   155→    // Current URL\n   156→    ctx.url; // 'https://example.com/checkout'\n   157→\n   158→    // Trigger name (for debugging)\n   159→    ctx.triggerName; // 'my-trigger'\n   160→\n   161→    // Check if action should stop\n   162→    ctx.isStopped(); // Returns true if navigation detected\n   163→\n   164→    // Throw ActionStoppedError if stopped (use in manual loops)\n   165→    ctx.checkStopped();\n   166→\n   167→    // AbortSignal - use with fetch() or other cancellable APIs\n   168→    ctx.abortSignal;\n   169→\n   170→    // Safe wait (throws if stopped during wait)\n   171→    await ctx.wait(1000);\n   172→\n   173→    // Safe iteration (checks stopped between items)\n   174→    await ctx.forEach(items, async (item) => {\n   175→      await ctx.commander.clickButton({ selector: item.selector });\n   176→    });\n   177→\n   178→    // Register cleanup (runs when action stops)\n   179→    ctx.onCleanup(() => {\n   180→      console.log('Cleaning up...');\n   181→    });\n   182→\n   183→    // Commander with all methods wrapped to throw on stop\n   184→    await ctx.commander.fillTextArea({ selector: 'input', text: 'hello' });\n   185→\n   186→    // Raw commander (use carefully - does not auto-throw)\n   187→    ctx.rawCommander;\n   188→  },\n   189→});\n   190→```\n   191→\n   192→## API Reference\n   193→\n   194→### launchBrowser(options)\n   195→\n   196→```javascript\n   197→const { browser, page } = await launchBrowser({\n   198→  engine: 'playwright', // 'playwright' or 'puppeteer'\n   199→  headless: false, // Run in headless mode\n   200→  userDataDir: '~/.hh-apply/playwright-data', // Browser profile directory\n   201→  slowMo: 150, // Slow down operations (ms)\n   202→  verbose: false, // Enable debug logging\n   203→  args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox'], // Custom Chrome args to append\n   204→});\n   205→```\n   206→\n   207→The `args` option allows passing custom Chrome arguments, which is useful for headless server environments (Docker, CI/CD) that require flags like `--no-sandbox`.\n   208→\n   209→### makeBrowserCommander(options)\n   210→\n   211→```javascript\n   212→const commander = makeBrowserCommander({\n   213→  page, // Required: Playwright/Puppeteer page\n   214→  verbose: false, // Enable debug logging\n   215→  enableNetworkTracking: true, // Track HTTP requests\n   216→  enableNavigationManager: true, // Enable navigation events\n   217→});\n   218→```\n   219→\n   220→### commander.pageTrigger(config)\n   221→\n   222→```javascript\n   223→const unregister = commander.pageTrigger({\n   224→  name: 'trigger-name',                    // For debugging\n   225→  condition: (ctx) => boolean,             // When to run (receives {url, commander})\n   226→  action: async (ctx) => void,             // What to do\n   227→  priority: 0,                             // Higher runs first\n   228→});\n   229→```\n   230→\n   231→### commander.goto(options)\n   232→\n   233→```javascript\n   234→await commander.goto({\n   235→  url: 'https://example.com',\n   236→  waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', // Playwright/Puppeteer option\n   237→  timeout: 60000,\n   238→});\n   239→```\n   240→\n   241→### commander.clickButton(options)\n   242→\n   243→```javascript\n   244→await commander.clickButton({\n   245→  selector: 'button.submit',\n   246→  scrollIntoView: true,\n   247→  waitForNavigation: true,\n   248→});\n   249→```\n   250→\n   251→### commander.fillTextArea(options)\n   252→\n   253→```javascript\n   254→await commander.fillTextArea({\n   255→  selector: 'textarea.message',\n   256→  text: 'Hello world',\n   257→  checkEmpty: true,\n   258→});\n   259→```\n   260→\n   261→### commander.destroy()\n   262→\n   263→```javascript\n   264→await commander.destroy(); // Stop actions, cleanup\n   265→```\n   266→\n   267→## Best Practices\n   268→\n   269→### 1. Use ctx.forEach for Loops\n   270→\n   271→```javascript\n   272→// BAD: Won't stop on navigation\n   273→for (const item of items) {\n   274→  await ctx.commander.click({ selector: item });\n   275→}\n   276→\n   277→// GOOD: Stops immediately on navigation\n   278→await ctx.forEach(items, async (item) => {\n   279→  await ctx.commander.click({ selector: item });\n   280→});\n   281→```\n   282→\n   283→### 2. Use ctx.checkStopped for Complex Logic\n   284→\n   285→```javascript\n   286→action: async (ctx) => {\n   287→  while (hasMorePages) {\n   288→    ctx.checkStopped(); // Throws if navigation detected\n   289→\n   290→    await processPage(ctx);\n   291→    hasMorePages = await ctx.commander.isVisible({ selector: '.next' });\n   292→  }\n   293→};\n   294→```\n   295→\n   296→### 3. Register Cleanup for Resources\n   297→\n   298→```javascript\n   299→action: async (ctx) => {\n   300→  const intervalId = setInterval(updateStatus, 1000);\n   301→\n   302→  ctx.onCleanup(() => {\n   303→    clearInterval(intervalId);\n   304→    console.log('Interval cleared');\n   305→  });\n   306→\n   307→  // ... rest of action\n   308→};\n   309→```\n   310→\n   311→### 4. Use ctx.abortSignal with Fetch\n   312→\n   313→```javascript\n   314→action: async (ctx) => {\n   315→  const response = await fetch(url, {\n   316→    signal: ctx.abortSignal, // Cancels on navigation\n   317→  });\n   318→};\n   319→```\n   320→\n   321→## Debugging\n   322→\n   323→Enable verbose mode for detailed logs:\n   324→\n   325→```javascript\n   326→const commander = makeBrowserCommander({ page, verbose: true });\n   327→```\n   328→\n   329→## Architecture\n   330→\n   331→See [src/ARCHITECTURE.md](src/ARCHITECTURE.md) for detailed architecture documentation.\n   332→\n   333→## License\n   334→\n   335→[UNLICENSE](../LICENSE)\n   336→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.     "type": "text",
    "file": {
      "filePath": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1768327375795/js/README.md",
      "content": "# Browser Commander\n\nA universal browser automation library for JavaScript/TypeScript that supports both Playwright and Puppeteer with a unified API. The key focus is on **stoppable page triggers** - ensuring automation logic is properly mounted/unmounted during page navigation.\n\n## Installation\n\n```bash\nnpm install browser-commander\n```\n\nYou'll also need either Playwright or Puppeteer:\n\n```bash\n# With Playwright\nnpm install playwright\n\n# Or with Puppeteer\nnpm install puppeteer\n```\n\n## Core Concept: Page State Machine\n\nBrowser Commander manages the browser as a state machine with two states:\n\n```\n+------------------+                      +------------------+\n|                  |   navigation start   |                  |\n|  WORKING STATE   | -------------------> |  LOADING STATE   |\n|  (action runs)   |                      |  (wait only)     |\n|                  |   <-----------------  |                  |\n+------------------+     page ready       +------------------+\n```\n\n**LOADING STATE**: Page is loading. Only waiting/tracking operations are allowed. No automation logic runs.\n\n**WORKING STATE**: Page is fully loaded (30 seconds of network idle). Page triggers can safely interact with DOM.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```javascript\nimport {\n  launchBrowser,\n  makeBrowserCommander,\n  makeUrlCondition,\n} from 'browser-commander';\n\n// 1. Launch browser\nconst { browser, page } = await launchBrowser({ engine: 'playwright' });\n\n// 2. Create commander\nconst commander = makeBrowserCommander({ page, verbose: true });\n\n// 3. Register page trigger with condition and action\ncommander.pageTrigger({\n  name: 'example-trigger',\n  condition: makeUrlCondition('*example.com*'), // matches URLs containing 'example.com'\n  action: async (ctx) => {\n    // ctx.commander has all methods, but they throw ActionStoppedError if navigation happens\n    // ctx.checkStopped() - call in loops to check if should stop\n    // ctx.abortSignal - use with fetch() for cancellation\n    // ctx.onCleanup(fn) - register cleanup when action stops\n\n    console.log(`Processing: ${ctx.url}`);\n\n    // Safe iteration - stops if navigation detected\n    await ctx.forEach(['item1', 'item2'], async (item) => {\n      await ctx.commander.clickButton({ selector: `[data-id=\"${item}\"]` });\n    });\n  },\n});\n\n// 4. Navigate - action auto-starts when page is ready\nawait commander.goto({ url: 'https://example.com' });\n\n// 5. Cleanup\nawait commander.destroy();\nawait browser.close();\n```\n\n## URL Condition Helpers\n\nThe `makeUrlCondition` helper makes it easy to create URL matching conditions:\n\n```javascript\nimport {\n  makeUrlCondition,\n  allConditions,\n  anyCondition,\n  notCondition,\n} from 'browser-commander';\n\n// Exact URL match\nmakeUrlCondition('https://example.com/page');\n\n// Contains substring (use * wildcards)\nmakeUrlCondition('*checkout*'); // URL contains 'checkout'\nmakeUrlCondition('*example.com*'); // URL contains 'example.com'\n\n// Starts with / ends with\nmakeUrlCondition('/api/*'); // starts with '/api/'\nmakeUrlCondition('*.json'); // ends with '.json'\n\n// Express-style route patterns\nmakeUrlCondition('/vacancy/:id'); // matches /vacancy/123\nmakeUrlCondition('https://hh.ru/vacancy/:vacancyId'); // matches specific domain + path\nmakeUrlCondition('/user/:userId/profile'); // multiple segments\n\n// RegExp\nmakeUrlCondition(/\\/product\\/\\d+/);\n\n// Custom function (receives full context)\nmakeUrlCondition((url, ctx) => {\n  const parsed = new URL(url);\n  return (\n    parsed.pathname.startsWith('/admin') && parsed.searchParams.has('edit')\n  );\n});\n\n// Combine conditions\nallConditions(\n  makeUrlCondition('*example.com*'),\n  makeUrlCondition('*/checkout*')\n); // Both must match\n\nanyCondition(makeUrlCondition('*/cart*'), makeUrlCondition('*/checkout*')); // Either matches\n\nnotCondition(makeUrlCondition('*/admin*')); // Negation\n```\n\n## Page Trigger Lifecycle\n\n### The Guarantee\n\nWhen navigation is detected:\n\n1. **Action is signaled to stop** (AbortController.abort())\n2. **Wait for action to finish** (up to 10 seconds for graceful cleanup)\n3. **Only then start waiting for page load**\n\nThis ensures:\n\n- No DOM operations on stale/loading pages\n- Actions can do proper cleanup (clear intervals, save state)\n- No race conditions between action and navigation\n\n## Action Context API\n\nWhen your action is called, it receives a context object with these properties:\n\n```javascript\ncommander.pageTrigger({\n  name: 'my-trigger',\n  condition: makeUrlCondition('*/checkout*'),\n  action: async (ctx) => {\n    // Current URL\n    ctx.url; // 'https://example.com/checkout'\n\n    // Trigger name (for debugging)\n    ctx.triggerName; // 'my-trigger'\n\n    // Check if action should stop\n    ctx.isStopped(); // Returns true if navigation detected\n\n    // Throw ActionStoppedError if stopped (use in manual loops)\n    ctx.checkStopped();\n\n    // AbortSignal - use with fetch() or other cancellable APIs\n    ctx.abortSignal;\n\n    // Safe wait (throws if stopped during wait)\n    await ctx.wait(1000);\n\n    // Safe iteration (checks stopped between items)\n    await ctx.forEach(items, async (item) => {\n      await ctx.commander.clickButton({ selector: item.selector });\n    });\n\n    // Register cleanup (runs when action stops)\n    ctx.onCleanup(() => {\n      console.log('Cleaning up...');\n    });\n\n    // Commander with all methods wrapped to throw on stop\n    await ctx.commander.fillTextArea({ selector: 'input', text: 'hello' });\n\n    // Raw commander (use carefully - does not auto-throw)\n    ctx.rawCommander;\n  },\n});\n```\n\n## API Reference\n\n### launchBrowser(options)\n\n```javascript\nconst { browser, page } = await launchBrowser({\n  engine: 'playwright', // 'playwright' or 'puppeteer'\n  headless: false, // Run in headless mode\n  userDataDir: '~/.hh-apply/playwright-data', // Browser profile directory\n  slowMo: 150, // Slow down operations (ms)\n  verbose: false, // Enable debug logging\n  args: ['--no-sandbox', '--disable-setuid-sandbox'], // Custom Chrome args to append\n});\n```\n\nThe `args` option allows passing custom Chrome arguments, which is useful for headless server environments (Docker, CI/CD) that require flags like `--no-sandbox`.\n\n### makeBrowserCommander(options)\n\n```javascript\nconst commander = makeBrowserCommander({\n  page, // Required: Playwright/Puppeteer page\n  verbose: false, // Enable debug logging\n  enableNetworkTracking: true, // Track HTTP requests\n  enableNavigationManager: true, // Enable navigation events\n});\n```\n\n### commander.pageTrigger(config)\n\n```javascript\nconst unregister = commander.pageTrigger({\n  name: 'trigger-name',                    // For debugging\n  condition: (ctx) => boolean,             // When to run (receives {url, commander})\n  action: async (ctx) => void,             // What to do\n  priority: 0,                             // Higher runs first\n});\n```\n\n### commander.goto(options)\n\n```javascript\nawait commander.goto({\n  url: 'https://example.com',\n  waitUntil: 'domcontentloaded', // Playwright/Puppeteer option\n  timeout: 60000,\n});\n```\n\n### commander.clickButton(options)\n\n```javascript\nawait commander.clickButton({\n  selector: 'button.submit',\n  scrollIntoView: true,\n  waitForNavigation: true,\n});\n```\n\n### commander.fillTextArea(options)\n\n```javascript\nawait commander.fillTextArea({\n  selector: 'textarea.message',\n  text: 'Hello world',\n  checkEmpty: true,\n});\n```\n\n### commander.destroy()\n\n```javascript\nawait commander.destroy(); // Stop actions, cleanup\n```\n\n## Best Practices\n\n### 1. Use ctx.forEach for Loops\n\n```javascript\n// BAD: Won't stop on navigation\nfor (const item of items) {\n  await ctx.commander.click({ selector: item });\n}\n\n// GOOD: Stops immediately on navigation\nawait ctx.forEach(items, async (item) => {\n  await ctx.commander.click({ selector: item });\n});\n```\n\n### 2. Use ctx.checkStopped for Complex Logic\n\n```javascript\naction: async (ctx) => {\n  while (hasMorePages) {\n    ctx.checkStopped(); // Throws if navigation detected\n\n    await processPage(ctx);\n    hasMorePages = await ctx.commander.isVisible({ selector: '.next' });\n  }\n};\n```\n\n### 3. Register Cleanup for Resources\n\n```javascript\naction: async (ctx) => {\n  const intervalId = setInterval(updateStatus, 1000);\n\n  ctx.onCleanup(() => {\n    clearInterval(intervalId);\n    console.log('Interval cleared');\n  });\n\n  // ... rest of action\n};\n```\n\n### 4. Use ctx.abortSignal with Fetch\n\n```javascript\naction: async (ctx) => {\n  const response = await fetch(url, {\n    signal: ctx.abortSignal, // Cancels on navigation\n  });\n};\n```\n\n## Debugging\n\nEnable verbose mode for detailed logs:\n\n```javascript\nconst commander = makeBrowserCommander({ page, verbose: true });\n```\n\n## Architecture\n\nSee [src/ARCHITECTURE.md](src/ARCHITECTURE.md) for detailed architecture documentation.\n\n## License\n\n[UNLICENSE](../LICENSE)\n",

      "startLine": 1,
      "totalLines": 336
    }
  }

Handle completion or errors\n   168→\n   169→## Network Idle Detection\n   170→\n   171→The library waits for **30 seconds** of zero pending HTTP requests:\n   172→\n   173→```javascript\n   174→// Ensures:\n   175→// - normal behavior\n   206→}\n   207→```\n   208→\n   209→## URL Condition Helpers\n   210→\n   211→Multiple ways to define URL conditions:\n   212→\n   213→```javascript\n   214→// Exact match\n   215→makeUrlCondition('https://example.com/page');\n   216→\n   217→// Wildcards\n   218→makeUrlCondition('*checkout*');\n   219→\n   220→// Route patterns\n   221→makeUrlCondition('/vacancy/:id');\n   222→\n   223→// Regex\n   224→makeUrlCondition(/\\/product\\/\\d+/);\n   225→\n   226→// Custom function\n   227→makeUrlCondition((url, ctx) => url.includes('/admin'));\n   228→\n   229→// Combine\n   230→allConditions(condition1, condition2);\n   231→anyCondition(condition1, condition2);\n   232→notCondition(condition);\n   233→```\n   234→\n   235→## Design Principles Applied\n   236→\n   237→### Modularity\n   238→\n   239→- Each module has single responsibility\n   240→- Clear boundaries between components\n   241→- Easy to test in isolation\n   242→\n   243→### Abstraction\n   244→\n   245→- Unified API hides engine differences\n   246→- High-level patterns for common tasks\n   247→\n   248→### Stable Contracts\n   249→\n   250→- Options object pattern prevents breaking changes\n   251→- Adding new options is backward compatible\n   252→\n   253→### Separation of Concerns\n   254→\n   255→- Navigation tracking separate from page operations\n   256→- Network tracking separate from element interactions\n   257→- Trigger management separate from action execution\n   258→\n   259→### Composition Over Complexity\n   260→\n   261→- Complex operations built from simple primitives\n   262→- `clickButton` uses `scrollIntoView`, `click`, verification\n   263→\n   264→## Future Improvements\n   265→\n   266→See GitHub issues:\n   267→\n   268→- [#94](https://github.com/konard/hh-job-application-automation/issues/94) - Page Trigger Pattern\n\nDeclarative page handlers that automatically manage lifecycle:\n\n```javascript\ncommander.pageTrigger({\n  name: 'checkout-handler',\n  condition: makeUrlCondition('*/checkout*'),\n  action: async (ctx) => {\n    // ctx.checkStopped() - check if should stop\n    // ctx.commander - wrapped commander (throws on navigation)\n    // ctx.onCleanup(fn) - register cleanup\n    await ctx.commander.fillTextArea({ selector: 'input', text: 'value' });\n  },\n});\n```\n\n### 3. Safe Iteration Pattern\n\n`ctx.forEach()` automatically checks for navigation between items:\n\n```javascript\nawait ctx.forEach(items, async (item) => {\n  await ctx.commander.click({ selector: item.selector });\n});\n```\n\n### 4. Wait for network idle (30s no HTTP requests)\n\n### WORKING STATE\n\nWhen page is ready:\n\n1. Find matching trigger (condition check)\n2. Create action context with wrapped commander\n3. Execute action\n4. Handle completion or errors\n\n## Network Idle Detection\n\nThe library waits for **30 seconds** of zero pending HTTP requests:\n\n```javascript\n// Ensures:\n// - All lazy-loaded content fetched\n// - All analytics complete\n// - All async JavaScript executed\n// - SPAs fully hydrated\n```\n\n## Error Handling\n\n### ActionStoppedError\n\nThrown when navigation interrupts an action:\n\n```javascript\ntry {\n  await ctx.commander.clickButton({ selector: 'button' });\n} catch (error) {\n  if (commander.isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n    // Navigation happened - action was stopped\n    return;\n  }\n  throw error;\n}\n```\n\n### Navigation Errors\n\nHandled gracefully with automatic recovery:\n\n```javascript\nif (isNavigationError(error)) {\n  // Page navigated during operation - normal behavior\n}\n```\n\n## URL Condition Helpers\n\nMultiple ways to define URL conditions:\n\n```javascript\n// Exact match\nmakeUrlCondition('https://example.com/page');\n\n// Wildcards\nmakeUrlCondition('*checkout*');\n\n// Route patterns\nmakeUrlCondition('/vacancy/:id');\n\n// Regex\nmakeUrlCondition(/\\/product\\/\\d+/);\n\n// Custom function\nmakeUrlCondition((url, ctx) => url.includes('/admin'));\n\n// Combine\nallConditions(condition1, condition2);\nanyCondition(condition1, condition2);\nnotCondition(condition);\n```\n\n## Design Principles Applied\n\n### Modularity\n\n- Each module has single responsibility\n- Clear boundaries between components\n- Easy to test in isolation\n\n### Abstraction\n\n- Unified API hides engine differences\n- High-level patterns for common tasks\n\n### Stable Contracts\n\n- Options object pattern prevents breaking changes\n- Adding new options is backward compatible\n\n### Separation of Concerns\n\n- Navigation tracking separate from page operations\n- Network tracking separate from element interactions\n- Trigger management separate from action execution\n\n### Composition Over Complexity\n\n- Complex operations built from simple primitives\n- `clickButton` uses `scrollIntoView`, `click`, verification\n\n## Future Improvements\n\nSee GitHub issues:\n\n- [#94](https://github.com/konard/hh-job-application-automation/issues/94) -         "content": "     1→# Browser Commander\n     2→\n     3→A Rust library for universal browser automation that provides a unified API for different browser automation engines. The key focus is on **stoppable page triggers** - ensuring automation logic is properly mounted/unmounted during page navigation.\n     4→\n     5→## Installation\n     6→\n     7→Add this to your `Cargo.toml`:\n     8→\n     9→```toml\n    10→[dependencies]\n    11→browser-commander = \"0.4\"\n    12→tokio = { version = \"1.0\", features = [\"full\"] }\n    13→```\n    14→\n    15→## Core Concept: Page State Machine\n    16→\n    17→Browser Commander manages the browser as a state machine with two states:\n    18→\n    19→```\n    20→+------------------+                      +------------------+\n    21→|                  |   navigation start   |                  |\n    22→|  WORKING STATE   | -------------------> |  LOADING STATE   |\n    23→|  (action runs)   |                      |  (wait only)     |\n    24→|                  |   <-----------------  |                  |\n    25→+------------------+     page ready       +------------------+\n    26→```\n    27→\n    28→**LOADING STATE**: Page is loading. Only waiting/tracking operations are allowed. No automation logic runs.\n    29→\n    30→**WORKING STATE**: Page is fully loaded (30 seconds of network idle). Page triggers can safely interact with DOM.\n    31→\n    32→## Quick Start\n    33→\n    34→```rust\n    35→use browser_commander::prelude::*;\n    36→\n    37→#[tokio::main]\n    38→async fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    39→    // Launch a browser with chromiumoxide engine\n    40→    let options = LaunchOptions::chromiumoxide()\n    41→        .headless(true);\n    42→\n    43→    let result = launch_browser(options).await?;\n    44→    println!(\"Browser launched: {:?}\", result.browser.engine);\n    45→\n    46→    // Navigate to a URL\n    47→    let page = &result.page;\n    48→    goto(page, \"https://example.com\", None).await?;\n    49→\n    50→    // Click a button\n    51→    click_button(page, \"button.submit\", None).await?;\n    52→\n    53→    // Fill a text field\n    54→    fill_text_area(page, \"input[name='email']\", \"test@example.com\", None).await?;\n    55→\n    56→    Ok(())\n    57→}\n    58→```\n    59→\n    60→## Features\n    61→\n    62→- **Unified API** across multiple browser engines\n    63→- **Built-in navigation safety handling**\n    64→- **Element visibility and scroll management**\n    65→- **Click, fill, and other interaction support with verification**\n    66→- **Async/await support with Tokio**\n    67→\n    68→## API Reference\n    69→\n    70→### Browser Launch\n    71→\n    72→```rust\n    73→use browser_commander::prelude::*;\n    74→\n    75→// Launch with chromiumoxide (CDP-based)\n    76→let options = LaunchOptions::chromiumoxide()\n    77→    .headless(true)\n    78→    .user_data_dir(\"~/.browser-data\");\n    79→\n    80→let result = launch_browser(options).await?;\n    81→```\n    82→\n    83→### Navigation\n    84→\n    85→```rust\n    86→// Navigate to URL\n    87→goto(&page, \"https://example.com\", None).await?;\n    88→\n    89→// Navigate with options\n    90→let nav_options = NavigationOptions {\n    91→    wait_until: WaitUntil::NetworkIdle,\n    92→    timeout: Some(30000),\n    93→};\n    94→goto(&page, \"https://example.com\", Some(nav_options)).await?;\n    95→\n    96→// Wait for URL to match condition\n    97→wait_for_url_condition(&page, |url| url.contains(\"success\")).await?;\n    98→```\n    99→\n   100→### Element Interactions\n   101→\n   102→```rust\n   103→// Click a button\n   104→click_button(&page, \"button.submit\", None).await?;\n   105→\n   106→// Click with options\n   107→let click_options = ClickOptions {\n   108→    scroll_into_view: true,\n   109→    wait_for_navigation: true,\n   110→    ..Default::default()\n   111→};\n   112→click_button(&page, \"button.submit\", Some(click_options)).await?;\n   113→\n   114→// Fill text area\n   115→fill_text_area(&page, \"textarea.message\", \"Hello world\", None).await?;\n   116→\n   117→// Scroll element into view\n   118→scroll_into_view(&page, \".target-element\", None).await?;\n   119→```\n   120→\n   121→### Element Queries\n   122→\n   123→```rust\n   124→// Check visibility\n   125→let visible = is_visible(&page, \".element\").await?;\n   126→\n   127→// Check if enabled\n   128→let enabled = is_enabled(&page, \"button.submit\").await?;\n   129→\n   130→// Get text content\n   131→let text = text_content(&page, \".message\").await?;\n   132→\n   133→// Get attribute value\n   134→let href = get_attribute(&page, \"a.link\", \"href\").await?;\n   135→\n   136→// Count matching elements\n   137→let count = count(&page, \".item\").await?;\n   138→```\n   139→\n   140→### Utilities\n   141→\n   142→```rust\n   143→// Wait for a duration\n   144→wait(1000).await;\n   145→\n   146→// Get current URL\n   147→let url = get_url(&page).await?;\n   148→\n   149→// Parse URL\n   150→let parsed = parse_url(\"https://example.com/path?query=value\")?;\n   151→\n   152→// Evaluate JavaScript\n   153→let result: String = evaluate(&page, \"document.title\").await?;\n   154→```\n   155→\n   156→## Modules\n   157→\n   158→- `core` - Core types and traits (constants, engine adapter, logger)\n   159→- `elements` - Element operations (selectors, visibility, content)\n   160→- `interactions` - User interactions (click, scroll, fill)\n   161→- `browser` - Browser management (launcher, navigation)\n   162→- `utilities` - General utilities (URL handling, wait operations)\n   163→- `high_level` -       "filePath": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1768327375795/rust/README.md",
      "content": "# Browser Commander\n\nA Rust library for universal browser automation that provides a unified API for different browser automation engines. The key focus is on **stoppable page triggers** - ensuring automation logic is properly mounted/unmounted during page navigation.\n\n## Installation\n\nAdd this to your `Cargo.toml`:\n\n```toml\n[dependencies]\nbrowser-commander = \"0.4\"\ntokio = { version = \"1.0\", features = [\"full\"] }\n```\n\n## Core Concept: Page State Machine\n\nBrowser Commander manages the browser as a state machine with two states:\n\n```\n+------------------+                      +------------------+\n|                  |   navigation start   |                  |\n|  WORKING STATE   | -------------------> |  LOADING STATE   |\n|  (action runs)   |                      |  (wait only)     |\n|                  |   <-----------------  |                  |\n+------------------+     page ready       +------------------+\n```\n\n**LOADING STATE**: Page is loading. Only waiting/tracking operations are allowed. No automation logic runs.\n\n**WORKING STATE**: Page is fully loaded (30 seconds of network idle). Page triggers can safely interact with DOM.\n\n## Quick Start\n\n```rust\nuse browser_commander::prelude::*;\n\n#[tokio::main]\nasync fn main() -> anyhow::Result<()> {\n    // Launch a browser with chromiumoxide engine\n    let options = LaunchOptions::chromiumoxide()\n        .headless(true);\n\n    let result = launch_browser(options).await?;\n    println!(\"Browser launched: {:?}\", result.browser.engine);\n\n    // Navigate to a URL\n    let page = &result.page;\n    goto(page, \"https://example.com\", None).await?;\n\n    // Click a button\n    click_button(page, \"button.submit\", None).await?;\n\n    // Fill a text field\n    fill_text_area(page, \"input[name='email']\", \"test@example.com\", None).await?;\n\n    Ok(())\n}\n```\n\n## Features\n\n- **Unified API** across multiple browser engines\n- **Built-in navigation safety handling**\n- **Element visibility and scroll management**\n- **Click, fill, and other interaction support with verification**\n- **Async/await support with Tokio**\n\n## API Reference\n\n### Browser Launch\n\n```rust\nuse browser_commander::prelude::*;\n\n// Launch with chromiumoxide (CDP-based)\nlet options = LaunchOptions::chromiumoxide()\n    .headless(true)\n    .user_data_dir(\"~/.browser-data\");\n\nlet result = launch_browser(options).await?;\n```\n\n### Navigation\n\n```rust\n// Navigate to URL\ngoto(&page, \"https://example.com\", None).await?;\n\n// Navigate with options\nlet nav_options = NavigationOptions {\n    wait_until: WaitUntil::NetworkIdle,\n    timeout: Some(30000),\n};\ngoto(&page, \"https://example.com\", Some(nav_options)).await?;\n\n// Wait for URL to match condition\nwait_for_url_condition(&page, |url| url.contains(\"success\")).await?;\n```\n\n### Element Interactions\n\n```rust\n// Click a button\nclick_button(&page, \"button.submit\", None).await?;\n\n// Click with options\nlet click_options = ClickOptions {\n    scroll_into_view: true,\n    wait_for_navigation: true,\n    ..Default::default()\n};\nclick_button(&page, \"button.submit\", Some(click_options)).await?;\n\n// Fill text area\nfill_text_area(&page, \"textarea.message\", \"Hello world\", None).await?;\n\n// Scroll element into view\nscroll_into_view(&page, \".target-element\", None).await?;\n```\n\n### Element Queries\n\n```rust\n// Check visibility\nlet visible = is_visible(&page, \".element\").await?;\n\n// Check if enabled\nlet enabled = is_enabled(&page, \"button.submit\").await?;\n\n// Get text content\nlet text = text_content(&page, \".message\").await?;\n\n// Get attribute value\nlet href = get_attribute(&page, \"a.link\", \"href\").await?;\n\n// Count matching elements\nlet count = count(&page, \".item\").await?;\n```\n\n### Utilities\n\n```rust\n// Wait for a duration\nwait(1000).await;\n\n// Get current URL\nlet url = get_url(&page).await?;\n\n// Parse URL\nlet parsed = parse_url(\"https://example.com/path?query=value\")?;\n\n// Evaluate JavaScript\nlet result: String = evaluate(&page, \"document.title\").await?;\n```\n\n## Modules\n\n- `core` - Core types and traits (constants, engine adapter, logger)\n- `elements` - Element operations (selectors, visibility, content)\n- `interactions` - User interactions (click, scroll, fill)\n- `browser` - Browser management (launcher, navigation)\n- `utilities` - General utilities (URL handling, wait operations)\n- `high_level` - High-level DRY utilities\n\n## Prelude\n\nFor convenience, import everything commonly needed with:\n\n```rust\nuse browser_commander::prelude::*;\n```\n\n## License\n\n[UNLICENSE](../LICENSE)\n",

Action is fully stopped before page loading starts\n * 3. Action can gracefully cleanup on stop\n * 4. All commander operations throw ActionStoppedError when stopped\n *\n * Terminology:\n * - Trigger: A condition + action pair that fires when condition is met\n * - Condition: A function that returns true/false to determine if trigger should fire\n * - Action: The async function that runs when condition is true\n */\n\n/**\n * Error thrown when action is stopped (navigation detected)\n * Actions can catch this to do cleanup, but should re-throw or return\n */\nexport class ActionStoppedError extends Error {\n  constructor(message = 'Action stopped due to navigation') {\n    super(message);\n    this.name = 'ActionStoppedError';\n    this.isActionStopped = true;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if error is an ActionStoppedError\n * @param {Error} error - request.url() : request.url;\n    const method =\n      typeof request.method === 'function' ? request.method() : request.method;\n    return `${method}:${url}`;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Handle request start\n   */\n  function onRequest(request) {\n    const key = getRequestKey(request);\n    const url = typeof request.url === 'function' ? request.url() : request.url;\n\n    // Ignore data URLs and blob URLs\n    if (url.startsWith('data:') || url.startsWith('blob:')) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    pendingRequests.set(key, request);\n    requestStartTimes.set(key, Date.now());\n\n    // Clear idle timer since we have a new request\n    if (idleTimer) {\n      clearTimeout(idleTimer);\n      idleTimer = null;\n    }",

Now I have a comprehensive understanding. Let me create a detailed summary document.\n\n## Comprehensive Overview of Browser Commander Repository\n\n### 1. Project Structure\n\nThe browser-commander is a **universal browser automation library** with implementations in multiple languages. The repository has the following structure:\n\n```\nbrowser-commander/\n├── js/                          # JavaScript/TypeScript implementation\n│   ├── src/                     # Source code\n│   │   ├── index.js            # Main entry point\n│   │   ├── factory.js          # makeBrowserCommander factory\n│   │   ├── exports.js          # Public API exports\n│   │   ├── bindings.js         # Bound function wrapper\n│   │   ├── browser/            # Browser lifecycle (launcher, navigation)\n│   │   ├── core/               # Core infrastructure\n│   │   ├── elements/           # DOM element operations\n│   │   ├── interactions/       # User interactions (click, fill, scroll)\n│   │   ├── utilities/          # Helper functions\n│   │   └── high-level/         # High-level automation patterns\n│   ├── tests/                  # Comprehensive test suite (unit + e2e)\n│   ├── examples/               # Example usage\n│   ├── package.json            # Node.js package config\n│   └── README.md\n│\n├── rust/                        # Rust implementation\n│   ├── src/                     # Source code with parallel structure\n│   │   ├── lib.rs             # Library entry point\n│   │   ├── main.rs            # Binary entry point\n│   │   ├── browser/           # Browser lifecycle\n│   │   ├── core/              # Core types and traits\n│   │   ├── elements/          # Element operations\n│   │   ├── interactions/      # User interactions\n│   │   ├── utilities/         # Helper functions\n│   │   └── high_level/        # High-level patterns\n│   ├── Cargo.toml             # Rust package config\n│   ├── changelog.d/           # Changelog fragments\n│   ├── scripts/               # Build and release scripts\n│   └── README.md\n│\n├── .github/workflows/          # CI/CD pipelines\n│   ├── js.yml                 # JavaScript CI/CD\n│   └── rust.yml               # Rust CI/CD\n│\n├── scripts/                    # Shared build/release scripts\n├── docs/                       # Documentation\n└── README.md\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. Core Concept: Page State Machine\n\nBrowser Commander models browser automation as a **state machine with two states**:\n\n```\n┌──────────────────┐                    ┌──────────────────┐\n│                  │   navigation start │                  │\n│  WORKING STATE   │─────────────────>  │  LOADING STATE   │\n│  (action runs)   │                    │  (wait only)     │\n│                  │  <──────────────── │                  │\n└──────────────────┘   page ready       └──────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Key guarantees when navigation is detected:**\n1. Action is signaled to stop (AbortController.abort())\n2. Wait for action to finish (up to 10 seconds for graceful cleanup)\n3. Only then start waiting for page load\n4. Network idle detection: 30 seconds of zero pending HTTP requests\n\n---\n\n### 3. JavaScript Implementation\n\n#### Package Configuration (`package.json`)\n- **Version**: 0.5.3\n- **Type**: ESM (ES modules)\n- **Node requirement**: >= 20.0.0\n- **Key dependencies**:\n  - Peer deps (optional): `playwright` (>=1.40.0), `puppeteer` (>=21.0.0)\n  - Runtime: `log-lazy` for logging\n- **Dev dependencies**: ESLint, Prettier, Husky, Changesets, etc.\n\n#### Core Modules\n\n**Browser Management:**\n- `browser/launcher.js` - Launch browsers with Playwright/Puppeteer\n- `browser/navigation.js` - Navigate, wait for page ready, handle navigation events\n\n**Core Infrastructure:**\n- `core/logger.js` - Logging utilities with verbose mode support\n- `core/engine-detection.js` - Detect Playwright vs Puppeteer\n- `core/engine-adapter.js` - Unified API wrapper for both engines\n- `core/network-tracker.js` - Track HTTP requests, detect network idle (30s timeout)\n- `core/navigation-manager.js` - Monitor URL changes, manage abort signals\n- `core/page-trigger-manager.js` - Manage stoppable page triggers with lifecycle\n- `core/page-session.js` - Per-page context management\n- `core/navigation-safety.js` - Handle navigation errors gracefully\n- `core/constants.js` - Chrome args, timing constants\n- `core/preferences.js` - Chrome preferences (disable translate, etc.)\n\n**Element Operations:**\n- `elements/selectors.js` - querySelector, findByText, normalizeSelector\n- `elements/locators.js` - Create Playwright locators\n- `elements/visibility.js` - Check element visibility/enabled state\n- `elements/content.js` - Get text content, attributes, input values\n\n**Interactions:**\n- `interactions/click.js` - clickElement, clickButton with verification\n- `interactions/fill.js` - fillTextArea with verification\n- `interactions/scroll.js` - scrollIntoView with verification\n\n**Utilities:**\n- `utilities/wait.js` - wait, evaluate, safeEvaluate\n- `utilities/url.js` - getUrl, unfocusAddressBar\n\n**High-level Patterns:**\n- `high-level/universal-logic.js` - Reusable patterns (waitForUrlCondition, installClickListener, etc.)\n\n#### API Patterns\n- **Options object pattern** - All functions accept options objects for maintainability\n- **Page Trigger Pattern** - Declarative handlers with automatic lifecycle management\n- **Safe Iteration** - `ctx.forEach()` checks for navigation between items\n- **Engine Abstraction** - Unified API works with both Playwright and Puppeteer\n\n---\n\n### 4. Rust Implementation\n\n#### Cargo.toml Configuration\n- **Version**: 0.4.0\n- **Edition**: 2021\n- **Key dependencies**:\n  - `tokio` (1.0+) - Async runtime\n  - `chromiumoxide` (0.7+) - CDP-based browser automation\n  - `fantoccini` (0.21+) - WebDriver client\n  - `serde` + `serde_json` - Serialization\n  - `tracing` + `tracing-subscriber` - Logging\n  - `thiserror`, `anyhow` - Error handling\n  - `regex`, `url` - Parsing\n  - `futures`, `async-trait`, `tokio-util` - Async utilities\n\n#### Module Structure (mirrors JavaScript)\n- `browser/` - Browser launch, navigation operations\n- `core/` - Constants, engine adapter, logger, navigation\n- `elements/` - Selectors, visibility, content operations\n- `interactions/` - Click, scroll, fill operations\n- `utilities/` - URL handling, wait operations\n- `high_level/` - High-level DRY utilities\n\n#### Key Types\n- `LaunchOptions` - Browser launch configuration\n- `LaunchResult` - Result of browser launch\n- `NavigationOptions` - Navigation configuration\n- `WaitUntil` - When to consider page \"ready\"\n- `ClickOptions`, `FillOptions`, `ScrollOptions` - Operation configurations\n- `EngineAdapter` - Unified browser engine interface\n- `Logger`, `LoggerOptions` - Logging support\n\n#### Prelude Module\nConvenience imports for common functionality:\n```rust\nuse browser_commander::prelude::*;\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. Testing Infrastructure\n\n#### JavaScript Tests\nLocated in `js/tests/`:\n- **Unit tests** (`tests/unit/`) - Comprehensive testing of all modules\n  - Core: engine-detection, navigation-safety, logger, engine-adapter, network-tracker, page-trigger-manager, constants\n  - Browser: navigation, launcher\n  - Elements: selectors, locators, visibility, content\n  - Interactions: click, fill, scroll\n  - Utilities: wait, url\n  - High-level: universal-logic\n  - Factory and bindings tests\n\n- **E2E tests** (`tests/e2e/`) - Integration tests with real browsers\n  - `playwright.e2e.test.js`\n  - `puppeteer.e2e.test.js`\n\n- **Test runners**:\n  - `npm run test:unit` - Unit tests only\n  - `npm run test:e2e` - E2E tests with real browsers\n  - `npm run test:all` - Both unit and E2E\n  - `npm run test:coverage` - Coverage reporting\n\n#### Rust Tests\n- Tests written in-module with `#[cfg(test)]` blocks\n- `cargo test --all-features` - Run all tests\n- `cargo test --doc` - Documentation tests\n\n---\n\n### 6. CI/CD Workflows\n\n#### JavaScript Workflow (`js.yml`)\n**Triggers**: Push to main, PRs, workflow_dispatch\n\n**Jobs**:\n1. **detect-changes** - Determine which checks to run\n2. **changeset-check** - Verify PR has changeset entry (Changesets for versioning)\n3. **lint** - ESLint, Prettier, code duplication check\n4. **test** - Matrix test across Ubuntu/macOS/Windows with Node 20.x\n5. **release** - Automatic release on main with npm OIDC trusted publishing\n6. **instant-release** - Manual instant release via workflow_dispatch\n7. **changeset-pr** - Create changeset PR for review\n\n**Release process**: Uses Changesets for version management and automated npm publishing\n\n#### Rust Workflow (`rust.yml`)\n**Triggers**: Push to main, PRs, workflow_dispatch\n\n**Jobs**:\n1. **detect-changes** - Determine which checks to run\n2. **changelog** - Check for changelog fragments (optional warning)\n3. **lint** - `cargo fmt`, Clippy, file size check\n4. **test** - Matrix test across Ubuntu/macOS/Windows\n5. **build** - Release build and package check\n6. **auto-release** - Automatic release based on changelog fragments\n7. **manual-release** - Manual release via workflow_dispatch\n\n**Release process**: Uses changelog fragments for version management and GitHub releases\n\n---\n\n### 7. API Features Overview\n\n#### URL Condition Helpers\nMultiple ways to define URL matching conditions:\n- **Exact match**: `'https://example.com/page'`\n- **Wildcard**: `'*checkout*'`, `'/api/*'`, `'*.json'`\n- **Express-style patterns**: `'/vacancy/:id'`, `'https://hh.ru/vacancy/:vacancyId'`\n- **RegExp**: `/\\/product\\/\\d+/`\n- **Custom function**: `(url, ctx) => boolean`\n- **Combinators**: `allConditions()`, `anyCondition()`, `notCondition()`\n\n#### Action Context\nWhen action runs, it receives context with:\n- `url` - Current page URL\n- `triggerName` - Trigger name for debugging\n- `isStopped()` - Check if action should stop\n- `checkStopped()` - Throw ActionStoppedError if stopped\n- `abortSignal` - Use with fetch() for cancellation\n- `wait(ms)` - Safe wait (throws if stopped)\n- `forEach(items, fn)` - Safe iteration checking stopped between items\n- `onCleanup(fn)` - Register cleanup callback\n- `commander` - Wrapped commander (throws on stop)\n- `rawCommander` - Raw commander (use carefully)\n\n#### Core Commands Available\n- `launchBrowser(options)` - Launch browser\n- `goto(url, options)` - Navigate to URL\n- `clickButton(selector, options)` - Click button with verification\n- `fillTextArea(selector, text, options)` - Fill input with verification\n- `scrollIntoView(selector, options)` - Scroll element into view\n- `isVisible(selector)` - Check visibility\n- `isEnabled(selector)` - Check if enabled\n- `textContent(selector)` - Get text content\n- `getAttribute(selector, attr)` - Get attribute value\n- `count(selector)` - Count matching elements\n- `wait(ms)` - Wait for duration\n- `evaluate(code)` - Evaluate JavaScript\n- `getUrl()` - Get current URL\n- `waitForUrlCondition(condition)` - Wait for URL to match condition\n- `destroy()` - Cleanup and stop\n\n---\n\n### 8. What Needs to Be Replicated in Python (Issue #21)\n\nAccording to GitHub Issue #21, a **Python implementation** needs to be created with:\n\n**Core Requirements:**\n1. **Same feature set** as JavaScript and Rust implementations:\n   - Browser automation API with unified interface\n   - Page trigger system with stoppable actions\n   - Network idle detection (30 seconds)\n   - Navigation safety handling\n   - Support for multiple browser engines\n   - Element operations (click, fill, scroll, etc.)\n   - URL condition matching system\n\n2. **Code structure** mirroring existing implementations:\n   - Parallel module organization\n   - Same public API surface\n   - Same design patterns (options objects, engine abstraction)\n\n3. **CI/CD Pipeline**:\n   - Based on `python-ai-driven-development-pipeline-template`\n   - Create `python.yml` workflow\n   - Integrate into multi-language repository\n   - Support for linting, testing, changelog management, and PyPI publishing\n\n4. **Testing**:\n   - Unit tests (similar to JS test suite)\n   - E2E tests with real browsers\n   - Multi-version Python support (3.9-3.13)\n\n5. **Packaging**:\n   - PyPI distribution\n   - Poetry or similar dependency management\n   - OIDC trusted publishing to PyPI\n\n---\n\n### 9. Key Design Principles\n\n1. **Modularity** - Each module has single responsibility\n2. **Abstraction** - Unified API hides engine differences\n3. **Stability** - Options object pattern prevents breaking changes\n4. **Separation of Concerns** - Navigation, network tracking, triggers are separate\n5. **Composition** - Complex operations built from simple primitives\n6. **Safe Navigation** - Guaranteed action cleanup before page loading\n7. **Network Awareness** - Waits for true page readiness, not just DOM loading\n\n---\n\n### 10. Project Structure\n\nThe browser-commander is a **universal browser automation library** with implementations in multiple languages. The repository has the following structure:\n\n```\nbrowser-commander/\n├── js/                          # JavaScript/TypeScript implementation\n│   ├── src/                     # Source code\n│   │   ├── index.js            # Main entry point\n│   │   ├── factory.js          # makeBrowserCommander factory\n│   │   ├── exports.js          # Public API exports\n│   │   ├── bindings.js         # Bound function wrapper\n│   │   ├── browser/            # Browser lifecycle (launcher, navigation)\n│   │   ├── core/               # Core infrastructure\n│   │   ├── elements/           # DOM element operations\n│   │   ├── interactions/       # User interactions (click, fill, scroll)\n│   │   ├── utilities/          # Helper functions\n│   │   └── high-level/         # High-level automation patterns\n│   ├── tests/                  # Comprehensive test suite (unit + e2e)\n│   ├── examples/               # Example usage\n│   ├── package.json            # Node.js package config\n│   └── README.md\n│\n├── rust/                        # Rust implementation\n│   ├── src/                     # Source code with parallel structure\n│   │   ├── lib.rs             # Library entry point\n│   │   ├── main.rs            # Binary entry point\n│   │   ├── browser/           # Browser lifecycle\n│   │   ├── core/              # Core types and traits\n│   │   ├── elements/          # Element operations\n│   │   ├── interactions/      # User interactions\n│   │   ├── utilities/         # Helper functions\n│   │   └── high_level/        # High-level patterns\n│   ├── Cargo.toml             # JavaScript CI/CD\n│   └── rust.yml               # Rust CI/CD\n│\n├── scripts/                    # Shared build/release scripts\n├── docs/                       # Documentation\n└── README.md\n```\n\n---\n\n### 2. Core Concept: Page State Machine\n\nBrowser Commander models browser automation as a **state machine with two states**:\n\n```\n┌──────────────────┐                    ┌──────────────────┐\n│                  │   navigation start │                  │\n│  WORKING STATE   │─────────────────>  │  LOADING STATE   │\n│  (action runs)   │                    │  (wait only)     │\n│                  │  <──────────────── │                  │\n└──────────────────┘   page ready       └──────────────────┘\n```\n\n**Key guarantees when navigation is detected:**\n1. Action is signaled to stop (AbortController.abort())\n2. Wait for action to finish (up to 10 seconds for graceful cleanup)\n3. Only then start waiting for page load\n4. Network idle detection: 30 seconds of zero pending HTTP requests\n\n---\n\n### 3. JavaScript Implementation\n\n#### Package Configuration (`package.json`)\n- **Version**: 0.5.3\n- **Type**: ESM (ES modules)\n- **Node requirement**: >= 20.0.0\n- **Key dependencies**:\n  - Peer deps (optional): `playwright` (>=1.40.0), `puppeteer` (>=21.0.0)\n  - Runtime: `log-lazy` for logging\n- **Dev dependencies**: ESLint, Prettier, Husky, Changesets, etc.\n\n#### Core Modules\n\n**Browser Management:**\n- `browser/launcher.js` - Launch browsers with Playwright/Puppeteer\n- `browser/navigation.js` - Navigate, wait for page ready, handle navigation events\n\n**Core Infrastructure:**\n- `core/logger.js` - Logging utilities with verbose mode support\n- `core/engine-detection.js` - Detect Playwright vs Puppeteer\n- `core/engine-adapter.js` - Unified API wrapper for both engines\n- `core/network-tracker.js` - Track HTTP requests, detect network idle (30s timeout)\n- `core/navigation-manager.js` - Monitor URL changes, manage abort signals\n- `core/page-trigger-manager.js` - Manage stoppable page triggers with lifecycle\n- `core/page-session.js` - Per-page context management\n- `core/navigation-safety.js` - Handle navigation errors gracefully\n- `core/constants.js` - Chrome args, timing constants\n- `core/preferences.js` - Chrome preferences (disable translate, etc.)\n\n**Element Operations:**\n- `elements/selectors.js` - querySelector, findByText, normalizeSelector\n- `elements/locators.js` - Create Playwright locators\n- `elements/visibility.js` - Check element visibility/enabled state\n- `elements/content.js` - Get text content, attributes, input values\n\n**Interactions:**\n- `interactions/click.js` - clickElement, clickButton with verification\n- `interactions/fill.js` - fillTextArea with verification\n- `interactions/scroll.js` - scrollIntoView with verification\n\n**Utilities:**\n- `utilities/wait.js` - wait, evaluate, safeEvaluate\n- `utilities/url.js` - getUrl, unfocusAddressBar\n\n**High-level Patterns:**\n- `high-level/universal-logic.js` - Reusable patterns (waitForUrlCondition, installClickListener, etc.)\n\n#### API Patterns\n- **Options object pattern** - All functions accept options objects for maintainability\n- **Page Trigger Pattern** - Declarative handlers with automatic lifecycle management\n- **Safe Iteration** - `ctx.forEach()` checks for navigation between items\n- **Engine Abstraction** - Unified API works with both Playwright and Puppeteer\n\n---\n\n### 4. Rust Implementation\n\n#### Cargo.toml Configuration\n- **Version**: 0.4.0\n- **Edition**: 2021\n- **Key dependencies**:\n  - `tokio` (1.0+) - Async runtime\n  - `chromiumoxide` (0.7+) - CDP-based browser automation\n  - `fantoccini` (0.21+) - WebDriver client\n  - `serde` + `serde_json` - Serialization\n  - `tracing` + `tracing-subscriber` - Logging\n  - `thiserror`, `anyhow` - Error handling\n  - `regex`, `url` - Parsing\n  - `futures`, `async-trait`, `tokio-util` - Async utilities\n\n#### Module Structure (mirrors JavaScript)\n- `browser/` - Browser launch, navigation operations\n- `core/` - Constants, engine adapter, logger, navigation\n- `elements/` - Selectors, visibility, content operations\n- `interactions/` - Click, scroll, fill operations\n- `utilities/` - URL handling, wait operations\n- `high_level/` - High-level DRY utilities\n\n#### Key Types\n- `LaunchOptions` - Browser launch configuration\n- `LaunchResult` - Result of browser launch\n- `NavigationOptions` - Navigation configuration\n- `WaitUntil` - When to consider page \"ready\"\n- `ClickOptions`, `FillOptions`, `ScrollOptions` - Operation configurations\n- `EngineAdapter` - Unified browser engine interface\n- `Logger`, `LoggerOptions` - Logging support\n\n#### Prelude Module\nConvenience imports for common functionality:\n```rust\nuse browser_commander::prelude::*;\n```\n\n---\n\n### 5. Testing Infrastructure\n\n#### JavaScript Tests\nLocated in `js/tests/`:\n- **Unit tests** (`tests/unit/`) - Comprehensive testing of all modules\n  - Core: engine-detection, navigation-safety, logger, engine-adapter, network-tracker, page-trigger-manager, constants\n  - Browser: navigation, launcher\n  - Elements: selectors, locators, visibility, content\n  - Interactions: click, fill, scroll\n  - Utilities: wait, url\n  - High-level: universal-logic\n  - Factory and bindings tests\n\n- **E2E tests** (`tests/e2e/`) - Integration tests with real browsers\n  - `playwright.e2e.test.js`\n  - `puppeteer.e2e.test.js`\n\n- **Test runners**:\n  - `npm run test:unit` - Unit tests only\n  - `npm run test:e2e` - E2E tests with real browsers\n  - `npm run test:all` - Both unit and E2E\n  - `npm run test:coverage` - Coverage reporting\n\n#### Rust Tests\n- Tests written in-module with `#[cfg(test)]` blocks\n- `cargo test --all-features` - Run all tests\n- `cargo test --doc` - Documentation tests\n\n---\n\n### 6. The CI workflow handles \"linting, formatting, mypy type checking\" plus testing and coverage reporting. The release workflow orchestrates \"lint, test, and build before any release\" plus PyPI publishing and GitHub release creation.\n\n**Configuration:**\nThe `pyproject.toml` serves as the central configuration file for dependencies, build settings, and tool configurations. Additional files include `.ruff.toml` for linting rules and `.pre-commit-config.yaml` for automated checks.\n\n**Scripts:**\nSupporting utilities in the scripts directory handle file size validation, version management, and release automation tasks.\n\n## Notable Design Features\n\nThe template emphasizes strict quality gates—\"All releases require passing CI checks (lint + test + build). No release will ever happen without passing tests,\" ensuring production stability through automated enforcement.",
