patch\n          - minor\n          - major\n      description:\n        description: 'Release description (optional)'\n        required: false\n        type: string\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\njobs:\n  # === DETECT CHANGES - determines which jobs should run ===\n  detect-changes:\n    name: Detect Changes\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    if: github.event_name != 'workflow_dispatch'\n    outputs:\n      py-changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.py-changed }}\n      tests-changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.tests-changed }}\n      package-changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.package-changed }}\n      docs-changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.docs-changed }}\n      workflow-changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.workflow-changed }}\n      any-code-changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.any-code-changed }}\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Detect changes\n        id: changes\n        env:\n          GITHUB_EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}\n          GITHUB_BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}\n          GITHUB_HEAD_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\n        run: python scripts/detect_code_changes.py\n\n  # REQUIRED CI CHECKS - All must pass before release\n  # These jobs ensure code quality and tests pass before any release\n\n  # === LINT AND FORMAT CHECK ===\n  # Lint runs independently of changelog check - it's a fast check that should always run\n  # See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1024 for why this dependency was removed\n  lint:\n    name: Lint and Format Check\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    if: |\n      github.event_name == 'push' ||\n      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.py-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.tests-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.docs-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.package-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true'\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install -e \".[dev]\"\n\n      - name: Run Ruff linting\n        run: ruff check .\n\n      - name: Check Ruff formatting\n        run: ruff format --check .\n\n      - name: Run mypy\n        run: mypy src\n\n      - name: Check file size limit\n        run: python scripts/check_file_size.py\n\n  # === TEST ===\n  # Test on latest Python version only\n  test:\n    name: Test (Python 3.13)\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    if: |\n      github.event_name == 'push' ||\n      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.py-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.tests-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.package-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true'\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install -e \".[dev]\"\n\n      - name: Run tests\n        run: pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-report=xml --cov-report=term\n\n      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov\n        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4\n        with:\n          file: ./coverage.xml\n          fail_ci_if_error: false\n\n  # === BUILD PACKAGE ===\n  # Build package - runs if lint and test pass, or were skipped (docs-only PR)\n  build:\n    name: Build Package\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes, lint, test]\n    # Run if: push/dispatch event, OR lint/test succeeded, OR lint/test were skipped (docs-only PR)\n    if: |\n      always() && (\n        github.event_name == 'push' ||\n        github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||\n        (\n          (needs.lint.result == 'success' || needs.lint.result == 'skipped') &&\n          (needs.test.result == 'success' || needs.test.result == 'skipped')\n        )\n      )\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install build dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install build twine\n\n      - name: Build package\n        run: python -m build\n\n      - name: Check package\n        run: twine check dist/*\n\n      - name: Upload artifacts\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4\n        with:\n          name: dist\n          path: dist/\n\n  # === CHANGELOG CHECK - only runs on PRs with code changes ===\n  # Docs-only PRs (./docs folder, markdown files) don't require changelog fragments\n  changelog:\n    name: Changelog Fragment Check\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true'\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Set up Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: \"3.13\"\n\n      - name: Install scriv\n        run: pip install \"scriv[toml]\"\n\n      - name: Check for changelog fragments\n        run: |\n          # Get list of fragment files (excluding README and template)\n          FRAGMENTS=$(find changelog.d -name \"*.md\" ! -name \"README.md\" ! -name \"*.j2\" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)\n\n          # Get changed files in PR\n          CHANGED_FILES=$(git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD)\n\n          # Check if any source files changed (excluding docs and config)\n          SOURCE_CHANGED=$(echo \"$CHANGED_FILES\" | grep -E \"^(src/|tests/|scripts/)\" | wc -l)\n\n          if [ \"$SOURCE_CHANGED\" -gt 0 ] && [ \"$FRAGMENTS\" -eq 0 ]; then\n            echo \"::warning::No changelog fragment found. Please run 'scriv create' and document your changes.\"\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"To create a changelog fragment:\"\n            echo \"  pip install 'scriv[toml]'\"\n            echo \"  scriv create\"\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"This is similar to adding a changeset in JavaScript projects.\"\n            echo \"See changelog.d/README.md for more information.\"\n            # Note: This is a warning, not a failure, to allow flexibility\n            # Change 'exit 0' to 'exit 1' to make it required\n            exit 0\n          fi\n\n          echo \"✓ Changelog check passed\"\n\n  # RELEASE JOBS - Only run after all CI checks pass\n\n  # Automatic release on push to main (if version changed)\n  auto-release:\n    name: Auto Release\n    needs: [lint, test, build]\n    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install build twine\n\n      - name: Check if version changed\n        id: version_check\n        run: |\n          # Get current version from pyproject.toml\n          CURRENT_VERSION=$(grep -Po '(?<=^version = \")[^\"]*' pyproject.toml)\n          echo \"current_version=$CURRENT_VERSION\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n\n          # Check if tag exists\n          if git rev-parse \"v$CURRENT_VERSION\" >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n            echo \"Tag v$CURRENT_VERSION already exists, skipping release\"\n            echo \"should_release=false\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          else\n            echo \"New version detected: $CURRENT_VERSION\"\n            echo \"should_release=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          fi\n\n      - name: Download artifacts\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4\n        with:\n          name: dist\n          path: dist/\n\n      - name: Publish to PyPI\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          python scripts/create_github_release.py \\\n            --version \"${{ steps.version_check.outputs.current_version }}\" \\\n            --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\"\n\n  # Manual release via workflow_dispatch - only after CI passes\n  manual-release:\n    name: Manual Release\n    needs: [lint, test, build]\n    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install build twine \"scriv[toml]\"\n\n      - name: Configure git\n        run: |\n          git config user.name \"github-actions[bot]\"\n          git config user.email \"github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com\"\n\n      - name: Collect changelog fragments\n        run: |\n          # Check if there are any fragments to collect\n          FRAGMENTS=$(find changelog.d -name \"*.md\" ! -name \"README.md\" ! -name \"*.j2\" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)\n          if [ \"$FRAGMENTS\" -gt 0 ]; then\n            echo \"Found $FRAGMENTS changelog fragment(s), collecting...\"\n            scriv collect --version \"${{ github.event.inputs.bump_type }}\"\n          else\n            echo \"No changelog fragments found, skipping collection\"\n          fi\n\n      - name: Version and commit\n        id: version\n        run: |\n          python scripts/version_and_commit.py \\\n            --bump-type \"${{ github.event.inputs.bump_type }}\" \\\n            --description \"${{ github.event.inputs.description }}\"\n\n      - name: Build package\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        run: python -m build\n\n      - name: Check package\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        run: twine check dist/*\n\n      - name: Publish to PyPI\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          python scripts/create_github_release.py \\\n            --version \"${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}\" \\\n            --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\"",

only runs on PRs with code changes ===\n  # Docs-only PRs (./docs folder, markdown files) don't require changelog fragments\n  changelog:\n    name: Changelog Fragment Check\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true'\n    steps:\n      - name: Check for changelog fragments\n        run: |\n          # Get list of fragment files (excluding README and template)\n          FRAGMENTS=$(find changelog.d -name \"*.md\" ! -name \"README.md\" ! -name \"*.j2\" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)\n\n          # Get changed files in PR\n          CHANGED_FILES=$(git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD)\n\n          # Check if any source files changed (excluding docs and config)\n          SOURCE_CHANGED=$(echo \"$CHANGED_FILES\" | grep -E \"^(src/|tests/|scripts/)\" | wc -l)\n\n          if [ \"$SOURCE_CHANGED\" -gt 0 ] && [ \"$FRAGMENTS\" -eq 0 ]; then\n            echo \"::warning::No changelog fragment found. Please run 'scriv create' and document your changes.\"\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"To create a changelog fragment:\"\n            echo \"  pip install 'scriv[toml]'\"\n            echo \"  scriv create\"\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"This is similar to adding a changeset in JavaScript projects.\"\n            echo \"See changelog.d/README.md for more information.\"\n            # Note: This is a warning, not a failure, to allow flexibility\n            # Change 'exit 0' to 'exit 1' to make it required\n            exit 0\n          fi\n\n          echo \"✓ Changelog check passed\"\n\n  # RELEASE JOBS - Only run after all CI checks pass\n\n  # Automatic release on push to main (if version changed)\n  auto-release:\n    name: Auto Release\n    needs: [lint, test, build]\n    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install build twine\n\n      - name: Check if version changed\n        id: version_check\n        run: |\n          # Get current version from pyproject.toml\n          CURRENT_VERSION=$(grep -Po '(?<=^version = \")[^\"]*' pyproject.toml)\n          echo \"current_version=$CURRENT_VERSION\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n\n          # Check if tag exists\n          if git rev-parse \"v$CURRENT_VERSION\" >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n            echo \"Tag v$CURRENT_VERSION already exists, skipping release\"\n            echo \"should_release=false\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          else\n            echo \"New version detected: $CURRENT_VERSION\"\n            echo \"should_release=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          fi\n\n      - name: Download artifacts\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4\n        with:\n          name: dist\n          path: dist/\n\n      - name: Publish to PyPI\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          python scripts/create_github_release.py \\\n            --version \"${{ steps.version_check.outputs.current_version }}\" \\\n            --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\"\n\n  # Manual release via workflow_dispatch - only after CI passes\n  manual-release:\n    name: Manual Release\n    needs: [lint, test, build]\n    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install build twine \"scriv[toml]\"\n\n      - name: Configure git\n        run: |\n          git config user.name \"github-actions[bot]\"\n          git config user.email \"github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com\"\n\n      - name: Collect changelog fragments\n        run: |\n          # Check if there are any fragments to collect\n          FRAGMENTS=$(find changelog.d -name \"*.md\" ! -name \"README.md\" ! -name \"*.j2\" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)\n          if [ \"$FRAGMENTS\" -gt 0 ]; then\n            echo \"Found $FRAGMENTS changelog fragment(s), collecting...\"\n            scriv collect --version \"${{ github.event.inputs.bump_type }}\"\n          else\n            echo \"No changelog fragments found, skipping collection\"\n          fi\n\n      - name: Version and commit\n        id: version\n        run: |\n          python scripts/version_and_commit.py \\\n            --bump-type \"${{ github.event.inputs.bump_type }}\" \\\n            --description \"${{ github.event.inputs.description }}\"\n\n      - name: Build package\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        run: python -m build\n\n      - name: Check package\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        run: twine check dist/*\n\n      - name: Publish to PyPI\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          python scripts/create_github_release.py \\\n            --version \"${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}\" \\\n            --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\"",

        "content": "     1→/**\n     2→ * Browser Commander - Factory Function\n     3→ * This module provides the makeBrowserCommander factory function that creates\n     4→ * a browser commander instance with all bound methods.\n     5→ */\n     6→\n     7→import { createLogger } from './core/logger.js';\n     8→import { detectEngine } from './core/engine-detection.js';\n     9→import { createNetworkTracker } from './core/network-tracker.js';\n    10→import { createNavigationManager } from './core/navigation-manager.js';\n    11→import { createPageSessionFactory } from './core/page-session.js';\n    12→import {\n    13→  createPageTriggerManager,\n    14→  ActionStoppedError,\n    15→  isActionStoppedError,\n    16→  makeUrlCondition,\n    17→  allConditions,\n    18→  anyCondition,\n    19→  notCondition,\n    20→} from './core/page-trigger-manager.js';\n    21→import { createBoundFunctions } from './bindings.js';\n    22→\n    23→/**\n    24→ * Create a browser commander instance for a specific page\n    25→ * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n    26→ * @param {Object} options.page - Playwright or Puppeteer page object\n    27→ * @param {boolean} options.verbose - Enable verbose logging\n    28→ * @param {boolean} options.enableNetworkTracking - Enable network request tracking (default: true)\n    29→ * @param {boolean} options.enableNavigationManager - Enable navigation manager (default: true)\n    30→ * @returns {Object} - Browser commander API\n    31→ */\n    32→export function makeBrowserCommander(options = {}) {\n    33→  const {\n    34→    page,\n    35→    verbose = false,\n    36→    enableNetworkTracking = true,\n    37→    enableNavigationManager = true,\n    38→  } = options;\n    39→\n    40→  if (!page) {\n    41→    throw new Error('page is required in options');\n    42→  }\n    43→\n    44→  const engine = detectEngine(page);\n    45→  const log = createLogger({ verbose });\n    46→\n    47→  // Create NetworkTracker if enabled\n    48→  // Use 30 second idle timeout to ensure page is fully loaded\n    49→  let networkTracker = null;\n    50→  if (enableNetworkTracking) {\n    51→    networkTracker = createNetworkTracker({\n    52→      page,\n    53→      engine,\n    54→      log,\n    55→      idleTimeout: 30000, // Wait 30 seconds without requests before considering network idle\n    56→    });\n    57→    networkTracker.startTracking();\n    58→  }\n    59→\n    60→  // Create NavigationManager if enabled\n    61→  let navigationManager = null;\n    62→  let sessionFactory = null;\n    63→\n    64→  // PageTriggerManager (will be initialized after commander is created)\n    65→  let pageTriggerManager = null;\n    66→\n    67→  if (enableNavigationManager) {\n    68→    navigationManager = createNavigationManager({\n    69→      page,\n    70→      engine,\n    71→      log,\n    72→      networkTracker,\n    73→    });\n    74→    navigationManager.startListening();\n    75→\n    76→    // Create PageSession factory\n    77→    sessionFactory = createPageSessionFactory({\n    78→      navigationManager,\n    79→      networkTracker,\n    80→      log,\n    81→    });\n    82→\n    83→    // Create PageTriggerManager\n    84→    pageTriggerManager = createPageTriggerManager({\n    85→      navigationManager,\n    86→      log,\n    87→    });\n    88→  }\n    89→\n    90→  // Create all bound functions\n    91→  const boundFunctions = createBoundFunctions({\n    92→    page,\n    93→    engine,\n    94→    log,\n    95→    verbose,\n    96→    navigationManager,\n    97→    networkTracker,\n    98→  });\n    99→\n   100→  // Cleanup function\n   101→  const destroy = async () => {\n   102→    if (pageTriggerManager) {\n   103→      await pageTriggerManager.destroy();\n   104→    }\n   105→    if (networkTracker) {\n   106→      networkTracker.stopTracking();\n   107→    }\n   108→    if (navigationManager) {\n   109→      navigationManager.stopListening();\n   110→    }\n   111→    if (sessionFactory) {\n   112→      await sessionFactory.endAllSessions();\n   113→    }\n   114→  };\n isActionStoppedError,\n  makeUrlCondition,\n  allConditions,\n  anyCondition,\n  notCondition,\n} from './core/page-trigger-manager.js';\nimport { createBoundFunctions } from './bindings.js';\n\n/**\n * Create a browser commander instance for a specific page\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Playwright or Puppeteer page object\n * @param {boolean} options.verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @param {boolean} options.enableNetworkTracking - Enable network request tracking (default: true)\n * @param {boolean} options.enableNavigationManager - Enable navigation manager (default: true)\n * @returns {Object} - Browser commander API\n */\nexport function makeBrowserCommander(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    verbose = false,\n    enableNetworkTracking = true,\n    enableNavigationManager = true,\n  } = options;\n\n  if (!page) {\n    throw new Error('page is required in options');\n  }\n\n  const engine = detectEngine(page);\n  const log = createLogger({ verbose });\n\n  // Create NetworkTracker if enabled\n  // Use 30 second idle timeout to ensure page is fully loaded\n  let networkTracker = null;\n  if (enableNetworkTracking) {\n    networkTracker = createNetworkTracker({\n      page,\n      engine,\n      log,\n      idleTimeout: 30000, // Wait 30 seconds without requests before considering network idle\n    });\n    networkTracker.startTracking();\n  }\n\n  // Create NavigationManager if enabled\n  let navigationManager = null;\n  let sessionFactory = null;\n\n  // PageTriggerManager (will be initialized after commander is created)\n  let pageTriggerManager = null;\n\n  if (enableNavigationManager) {\n    navigationManager = createNavigationManager({\n      page,\n      engine,\n      log,\n      networkTracker,\n    });\n    navigationManager.startListening();\n\n    // Create PageSession factory\n    sessionFactory = createPageSessionFactory({\n      navigationManager,\n      networkTracker,\n      log,\n    });\n\n    // Create PageTriggerManager\n    pageTriggerManager = createPageTriggerManager({\n      navigationManager,\n      log,\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Create all bound functions\n  const boundFunctions = createBoundFunctions({\n    page,\n    engine,\n    log,\n    verbose,\n    navigationManager,\n    networkTracker,\n  });\n\n  // Cleanup function\n  const destroy = async () => {\n    if (pageTriggerManager) {\n      await pageTriggerManager.destroy();\n    }\n    if (networkTracker) {\n      networkTracker.stopTracking();\n    }\n    if (navigationManager) {\n      navigationManager.stopListening();\n    }\n    if (sessionFactory) {\n      await sessionFactory.endAllSessions();\n    }\n  };\n\n  // Build commander object\n  const commander = {\n    // Core properties\n    engine,\n    page,\n    log,\n\n    // Navigation management components\n    networkTracker,\n    navigationManager,\n    sessionFactory,\n    pageTriggerManager,\n\n    // All bound functions\n    ...boundFunctions,\n\n    // Lifecycle\n    destroy,\n\n    // (opts) => sessionFactory.createSession(opts)\n      : null,\n    getActiveSessions: sessionFactory\n      ? () => sessionFactory.getActiveSessions()\n      : () => [],\n\n    // Subscribe to navigation events (legacy API)\n    onNavigationStart: navigationManager\n      ? (fn) => navigationManager.on('onNavigationStart', fn)\n      : () => {},\n    onNavigationComplete: navigationManager\n      ? (fn) => navigationManager.on('onNavigationComplete', fn)\n      : () => {},\n    onUrlChange: navigationManager\n      ? (fn) => navigationManager.on('onUrlChange', fn)\n      : () => {},\n    onPageReady: navigationManager\n      ? (fn) => navigationManager.on('onPageReady', fn)\n      : () => {},\n\n    // Abort handling - check these to stop operations when navigation occurs\n    shouldAbort: navigationManager\n      ? () => navigationManager.shouldAbort()\n      : () => false,\n    getAbortSignal: navigationManager\n      ? () => navigationManager.getAbortSignal()\n      : () => null,\n\n    // Page Trigger API\n    // Register a trigger: commander.pageTrigger({ condition, action, name })\n    // condition receives context: { url, commander }\n    // action receives context: { url, commander, checkStopped, forEach, wait, onCleanup, ... }\n    pageTrigger: pageTriggerManager\n      ? from './browser/navigation.js';\nimport {\n  createPlaywrightLocator,\n  getLocatorOrElement,\n  waitForLocatorOrElement,\n  waitForVisible,\n  locator,\n} from './elements/locators.js';\nimport {\n  querySelector,\n  querySelectorAll,\n  findByText,\n  normalizeSelector,\n  waitForSelector,\n  withTextSelectorSupport,\n} from './elements/selectors.js';\nimport { isVisible, isEnabled, count } from './elements/visibility.js';\nimport {\n  textContent,\n  inputValue,\n  getAttribute,\n  getInputValue,\n  logElementInfo,\n} from './elements/content.js';\nimport {\n  scrollIntoView,\n  needsScrolling,\n  scrollIntoViewIfNeeded,\n} from './interactions/scroll.js';\nimport { clickElement, clickButton } from './interactions/click.js';\nimport {\n  checkIfElementEmpty,\n  performFill,\n  fillTextArea,\n} from './interactions/fill.js';\nimport {\n  waitForUrlCondition,\n  installClickListener,\n  checkAndClearFlag,\n  findToggleButton,\n} from './high-level/universal-logic.js';\n\n/**\n * Create bound functions for a browser commander instance\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @param {boolean} options.verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (optional)\n * @param {Object} options.networkTracker - NetworkTracker instance (optional)\n * @returns {Object} - Object containing all bound functions\n */\nexport function createBoundFunctions(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    log,\n    verbose = false,\n    navigationManager,\n    networkTracker,\n  } = options;\n\n  // Create bound helper functions that inject page, engine, log\n  // Wait function now automatically gets abort signal from navigation manager\n  const waitBound = (opts) => {\n    const abortSignal = navigationManager\n      ? navigationManager.getAbortSignal()\n      : null;\n    return wait({ ...opts, log, abortSignal: opts.abortSignal || abortSignal });\n  };\n  const evaluateBound = (opts) => evaluate({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const safeEvaluateBound = (opts) => safeEvaluate({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const getUrlBound = () => getUrl({ page });\n  const unfocusAddressBarBound = (opts = {}) =>\n    unfocusAddressBar({ ...opts, page });\n\n  // Bound navigation - with NavigationManager integration\n  const waitForUrlStabilizationBound = (opts) =>\n    waitForUrlStabilization({\n      ...opts,\n      page,\n      log,\n      wait: waitBound,\n      navigationManager,\n    });\n  const gotoBound = (opts) =>\n    goto({\n      ...opts,\n      page,\n      waitForUrlStabilization: waitForUrlStabilizationBound,\n      navigationManager,\n    });\n  const waitForNavigationBound = (opts) =>\n    waitForNavigation({\n      ...opts,\n      page,\n      navigationManager,\n    });\n  const waitForPageReadyBound = (opts) =>\n    waitForPageReady({\n      ...opts,\n      page,\n      navigationManager,\n      networkTracker,\n      log,\n      wait: waitBound,\n    });\n  const waitAfterActionBound = (opts) =>\n    waitAfterAction({\n      ...opts,\n      page,\n      navigationManager,\n      networkTracker,\n      log,\n      wait: waitBound,\n    });\n\n  // Bound locators\n  const createPlaywrightLocatorBound = (opts) =>\n    createPlaywrightLocator({ ...opts, page });\n  const getLocatorOrElementBound = (opts) =>\n    getLocatorOrElement({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const waitForLocatorOrElementBound = (opts) =>\n    waitForLocatorOrElement({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const waitForVisibleBound = (opts) => waitForVisible({ ...opts, engine });\n  const locatorBound = (opts) => locator({ ...opts, page, engine });\n\n  // Bound selectors\n  const querySelectorBound = (opts) => querySelector({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const querySelectorAllBound = (opts) =>\n    querySelectorAll({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const findByTextBound = (opts) => findByText({ ...opts, engine });\n  const normalizeSelectorBound = (opts) =>\n    normalizeSelector({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const waitForSelectorBound = (opts) =>\n    waitForSelector({ ...opts, page, engine });\n\n  // Bound visibility\n  const isVisibleBound = (opts) => isVisible({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const isEnabledBound = (opts) => isEnabled({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const countBound = (opts) => count({ ...opts, page, engine });\n\n  // Bound content\n  const textContentBound = (opts) => textContent({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const inputValueBound = (opts) => inputValue({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const getAttributeBound = (opts) => getAttribute({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const getInputValueBound = (opts) => getInputValue({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const logElementInfoBound = (opts) =>\n    logElementInfo({ ...opts, page, engine, log });\n\n  // Bound scroll\n  const scrollIntoViewBound = (opts) =>\n    scrollIntoView({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const needsScrollingBound = (opts) =>\n    needsScrolling({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const scrollIntoViewIfNeededBound = (opts) =>\n    scrollIntoViewIfNeeded({ ...opts, page, engine, wait: waitBound, log });\n\n  // Bound click - now navigation-aware\n  const clickElementBound = (opts) => clickElement({ ...opts, engine, log });\n  const clickButtonBound = (opts) =>\n    clickButton({\n      ...opts,\n      page,\n      engine,\n      wait: waitBound,\n      log,\n      verbose,\n      navigationManager,\n      networkTracker,\n    });\n\n  // Bound fill\n  const checkIfElementEmptyBound = (opts) =>\n    checkIfElementEmpty({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const performFillBound = (opts) => performFill({ ...opts, page, engine });\n  const fillTextAreaBound = (opts) =>\n    fillTextArea({ ...opts, page, engine, wait: waitBound, log });\n\n  // Bound high-level\n  const waitForUrlConditionBound = (opts) =>\n    waitForUrlCondition({\n      ...opts,\n      getUrl: getUrlBound,\n      wait: waitBound,\n      evaluate: evaluateBound,\n    });\n  const installClickListenerBound = (opts) =>\n    installClickListener({ ...opts, evaluate: evaluateBound });\n  const checkAndClearFlagBound = (opts) =>\n    checkAndClearFlag({ ...opts, evaluate: evaluateBound });\n  const findToggleButtonBound = (opts) =>\n    findToggleButton({\n      ...opts,\n      count: countBound,\n      findByText: findByTextBound,\n    });\n\n  // Wrap functions with text selector support\n  const fillTextAreaWrapped = withTextSelectorSupport(\n    fillTextAreaBound,\n    engine,\n    page\n  );\n  const clickButtonWrapped = withTextSelectorSupport(\n    clickButtonBound,\n    engine,\n    page\n  );\n  const getAttributeWrapped = withTextSelectorSupport(\n    getAttributeBound,\n    engine,\n    page\n  );\n  const isVisibleWrapped = withTextSelectorSupport(\n    isVisibleBound,\n    engine,\n    page\n  );\n  const isEnabledWrapped = withTextSelectorSupport(\n    isEnabledBound,\n    engine,\n    page\n  );\n  const textContentWrapped = withTextSelectorSupport(\n    textContentBound,\n    engine,\n    page\n  );\n  const inputValueWrapped = withTextSelectorSupport(\n    inputValueBound,\n    engine,\n    page\n  );\n\n  return {\n    // Helper functions (now public)\n    createPlaywrightLocator: createPlaywrightLocatorBound,\n    getLocatorOrElement: getLocatorOrElementBound,\n    waitForLocatorOrElement: waitForLocatorOrElementBound,\n    scrollIntoView: scrollIntoViewBound,\n    scrollIntoViewIfNeeded: scrollIntoViewIfNeededBound,\n    needsScrolling: needsScrollingBound,\n    checkIfElementEmpty: checkIfElementEmptyBound,\n    performFill: performFillBound,\n    logElementInfo: logElementInfoBound,\n    normalizeSelector: normalizeSelectorBound,\n    withTextSelectorSupport: (fn) => withTextSelectorSupport(fn, engine, page),\n    waitForVisible: waitForVisibleBound,\n    clickElement: clickElementBound,\n    getInputValue: getInputValueBound,\n    unfocusAddressBar: unfocusAddressBarBound,\n\n    // Main API functions\n    wait: waitBound,\n querySelector: querySelectorBound,\n    querySelectorAll: querySelectorAllBound,\n    waitForUrlStabilization: waitForUrlStabilizationBound,\n    goto: gotoBound,\n    getUrl: getUrlBound,\n 1; // Convert to 0-based\n   220→      return this.page.locator(baseSelector).nth(index);\n   221→    }\n   222→    return this.page.locator(selector);\n   223→  }\n   224→\n   225→  async querySelector(selector) {\n   226→    const locator = this.createLocator(selector).first();\n   227→    const count = await locator.count();\n   228→    return count > 0 ? locator : null;\n   229→  }\n   230→\n   231→  async querySelectorAll(selector) {\n   232→    const locator = this.createLocator(selector);\n   233→    const count = await locator.count();\n   234→    const elements = [];\n   235→    for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {\n   236→      elements.push(locator.nth(i));\n   237→    }\n   238→    return elements;\n   239→  }\n   240→\n   241→  async waitForSelector(selector, options = {}) {\n   242→    const { visible = true, timeout = 5000 } = options;\n   243→    const locator = this.createLocator(selector);\n   244→    await locator.waitFor({ state: visible ? 'visible' : 'attached', timeout });\n   245→  }\n   246→\n   247→  async waitForVisible(locatorOrElement, timeout = TIMING.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT) {\n   248→    const firstLocator = args);\n   267→  }\n   268→\n   269→  async getTextContent(locatorOrElement) {\n   270→    return await locatorOrElement.textContent();\n   271→  }\n   272→\n   273→  async ============================================================================\n   302→  // Page-level Operations\n   303→  // ============================================================================\n   304→\n   305→  async evaluateOnPage(fn, args = []) {\n   306→    // Playwright only accepts a single argument (can be array/object)\n   307→    // To match Puppeteer's behavior where args are spread, we wrap the function\n   308→    // and pass all args as a single array, then apply them in the browser context\n   309→    if (args.length === 0) {\n   310→      return await this.page.evaluate(fn);\n   311→    } else if (args.length === 1) {\n   312→      return await this.page.evaluate(fn, args[0]);\n   313→    } else {\n   314→      // Multiple args - wrap function to accept array and spread them\n   315→      // This makes Playwright behave like Puppeteer's spread behavior\n   316→      // We pass the function string and args array, then reconstruct and call in browser\n   317→      const fnString = fn.toString();\n   318→      return await this.page.evaluate(\n   319→        ({ fnStr, argsArray }) => {\n   320→          // Reconstruct the function in browser context and call with spread args\n   321→          const reconstructedFn = new Function(`return (${fnStr})`)();\n   322→          return reconstructedFn(...argsArray);\n   323→        },\n   324→        { fnStr: fnString, argsArray: args }\n   325→      );\n   326→    }\n   327→  }\n   328→\n   329→  getMainFrame() {\n   330→    return this.page.mainFrame();\n   331→  }\n   332→}\n   333→\n   334→/**\n   335→ * Puppeteer adapter implementation\n   336→ */\n   337→export class PuppeteerAdapter extends EngineAdapter {\n   338→  getEngineName() {\n   339→    return 'puppeteer';\n   340→  }\n   341→\n   342→  // ============================================================================\n   343→  // Element Selection and Locators\n   344→  // ============================================================================\n   345→\n   346→  createLocator(selector) {\n   347→    // Puppeteer doesn't have locators - just returns selector\n   348→    // The actual element will be queried when needed\n   349→    return selector;\n   350→  }\n   351→\n   352→  async querySelector(selector) {\n   353→    return await waitForVisible(locatorOrElement, timeout = TIMING.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT) {\n   366→    // For Puppeteer, locatorOrElement is already an ElementHandle\n   367→    // We can't wait on it directly, so we just return it\n   368→    // The caller should have already used waitForSelector\n   369→    return locatorOrElement;\n   370→  }\n   371→\n   372→  async count(selector) {\n   373→    const elements = Evaluation and Properties\n   379→  // ============================================================================\n   380→\n   381→  async evaluateOnElement(locatorOrElement, fn, args) {\n   382→ locatorOrElement);\n   391→  }\n   392→\n   393→  async getInputValue(locatorOrElement) {\n   394→    return await this.page.evaluate((el) => el.value, locatorOrElement);\n   395→  }\n   396→\n   397→ 411→  }\n   412→\n   413→  async type(locatorOrElement, text) {\n   414→    // Puppeteer requires focus before typing\n   415→    await locatorOrElement.focus();\n   416→    await this.page.evaluate(\n   422→      (el, value) => {\n   423→        el.value = value;\n   424→        el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true }));\n   425→        el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true }));\n   426→      },\n   427→      locatorOrElement,\n   428→      text\n   429→    );\n   430→  }\n   431→\n   432→  async focus(locatorOrElement) {\n   433→    await ============================================================================\n   439→\n   440→  async evaluateOnPage(fn, args = []) {\n   441→    // Puppeteer accepts spread arguments\n   442→ Stack trace: ${errorDetails.stackTrace}`\n   466→    );\n   467→  }\n   468→\n   469→  if (engine === 'playwright') {\n   470→    return new PlaywrightAdapter(page);\n   471→  } else if (engine === 'puppeteer') {\n   472→    return new PuppeteerAdapter(page);\n   473→  } else {\n   474→    throw new Error(\n   475→      `Unsupported engine: ${engine}. 'visible' : 'attached', timeout });\n  }\n\n  async waitForVisible(locatorOrElement, timeout = TIMING.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT) {\n    const firstLocator = locatorOrElement.first();\n    await firstLocator.waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout });\n    return firstLocator;\n  }\n\n  async count(selector) {\n    return await this.page.locator(selector).count();\n  }\n\n  // ============================================================================\n  // Element Evaluation and Properties\n  // ============================================================================\n\n  async evaluateOnElement(locatorOrElement, fn, args) {\n    // Playwright only accepts a single argument\n    if (args === undefined) {\n      return await locatorOrElement.evaluate(fn);\n    }\n    return await locatorOrElement.evaluate(fn, args);\n  }\n\n  async getTextContent(locatorOrElement) {\n    return await locatorOrElement.textContent();\n  }\n\n  async getInputValue(locatorOrElement) {\n    return await locatorOrElement.inputValue();\n  }\n\n  async getAttribute(locatorOrElement, attribute) {\n    return await locatorOrElement.getAttribute(attribute);\n  }\n\n  // ============================================================================\n  // Element Interactions\n  // ============================================================================\n\n  async click(locatorOrElement, options = {}) {\n    await locatorOrElement.click(options);\n  }\n\n  async type(locatorOrElement, text) {\n    await locatorOrElement.type(text);\n  }\n\n  async fill(locatorOrElement, text) {\n    await locatorOrElement.fill(text);\n  }\n\n  async focus(locatorOrElement) {\n    await locatorOrElement.focus();\n  }\n\n  // ============================================================================\n  // Page-level Operations\n  // ============================================================================\n\n  async evaluateOnPage(fn, args = []) {\n    // Playwright only accepts a single argument (can be array/object)\n    // To match Puppeteer's behavior where args are spread, we wrap the function\n    // and pass all args as a single array, then apply them in the browser context\n    if (args.length === 0) {\n      return await this.page.evaluate(fn);\n    } else if (args.length === 1) {\n      return await this.page.evaluate(fn, args[0]);\n    } else {\n      // Multiple args - wrap function to accept array and spread them\n      // This makes Playwright behave like Puppeteer's spread behavior\n      // We pass the function string and args array, then reconstruct and call in browser\n      const fnString = fn.toString();\n      return await this.page.evaluate(\n        ({ fnStr, argsArray }) => {\n          // Reconstruct the function in browser context and call with spread args\n          const reconstructedFn = new Function(`return (${fnStr})`)();\n          return reconstructedFn(...argsArray);\n        },\n        { fnStr: fnString, argsArray: args }\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  getMainFrame() {\n    return this.page.mainFrame();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Puppeteer adapter implementation\n */\nexport class PuppeteerAdapter extends EngineAdapter {\n  getEngineName() {\n    return 'puppeteer';\n  }\n\n  // ============================================================================\n  // Element Selection and Locators\n  // ============================================================================\n\n  createLocator(selector) {\n    // Puppeteer doesn't have locators - just returns selector\n    // The actual element will be queried when needed\n    return selector;\n  }\n\n  async querySelector(selector) {\n    return await this.page.$(selector);\n  }\n\n  async querySelectorAll(selector) {\n    return await this.page.$$(selector);\n  }\n\n  async waitForSelector(selector, options = {}) {\n    const { visible = true, timeout = 5000 } = options;\n    await this.page.waitForSelector(selector, { visible, timeout });\n  }\n\n  async waitForVisible(locatorOrElement, timeout = TIMING.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT) {\n    // For Puppeteer, locatorOrElement is already an ElementHandle\n    // We can't wait on it directly, so we just return it\n    // The caller should have already used waitForSelector\n    return locatorOrElement;\n  }\n\n  async count(selector) {\n    const elements = await this.page.$$(selector);\n    return elements.length;\n  }\n\n  // ============================================================================\n  // Element Evaluation and Properties\n  // ============================================================================\n\n  async evaluateOnElement(locatorOrElement, fn, args) {\n    // Puppeteer accepts the element as first arg, then spread args\n    if (args === undefined) {\n      return await this.page.evaluate(fn, locatorOrElement);\n    }\n    return await this.page.evaluate(fn, locatorOrElement, args);\n  }\n\n  async getTextContent(locatorOrElement) {\n    return await this.page.evaluate((el) => el.textContent, locatorOrElement);\n  }\n\n  async getInputValue(locatorOrElement) {\n    return await this.page.evaluate((el) => el.value, locatorOrElement);\n  }\n\n  async getAttribute(locatorOrElement, attribute) {\n    return await this.page.evaluate(\n      (el, attr) => el.getAttribute(attr),\n      locatorOrElement,\n      attribute\n    );\n  }\n\n  // ============================================================================\n  // Element Interactions\n  // ============================================================================\n\n  async click(locatorOrElement, options = {}) {\n    await locatorOrElement.click(options);\n  }\n\n  async type(locatorOrElement, text) {\n    // Puppeteer requires focus before typing\n    await locatorOrElement.focus();\n    await this.page.keyboard.type(text);\n  }\n\n  async fill(locatorOrElement, text) {\n    // Puppeteer doesn't have fill() - use evaluate to set value\n    await this.page.evaluate(\n      (el, value) => {\n        el.value = value;\n        el.dispatchEvent(new Event('input', { bubbles: true }));\n        el.dispatchEvent(new Event('change', { bubbles: true }));\n      },\n      locatorOrElement,\n      text\n    );\n  }\n\n  async focus(locatorOrElement) {\n    await locatorOrElement.focus();\n  }\n\n  // ============================================================================\n  // Page-level Operations\n  // ============================================================================\n\n  async evaluateOnPage(fn, args = []) {\n    // Puppeteer accepts spread arguments\n    return await this.page.evaluate(fn, ...args);\n  }\n\n  getMainFrame() {\n    return this.page.mainFrame();\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Factory function to create appropriate adapter\n * @param {Object} page - Playwright or Puppeteer page object\n * @param {string} engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @returns {EngineAdapter} - Appropriate adapter instance\n */\nexport function createEngineAdapter(page, engine) {\n  if (!page) {\n    const errorDetails = {\n      page,\n      pageType: typeof page,\n      engine,\n      stackTrace: new Error().stack,\n    };\n    throw new Error(\n      `page is required in createEngineAdapter. Received: page=${page} (type: ${typeof page}), engine=${engine}. This may indicate that the page object was not properly passed through the function call chain. Stack trace: ${errorDetails.stackTrace}`\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (engine === 'playwright') {\n    return new PlaywrightAdapter(page);\n  } else if (engine === 'puppeteer') {\n    return new PuppeteerAdapter(page);\n  } else {\n    throw new Error(\n      `Unsupported engine: ${engine}. we're now idle\n    checkIdle();\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Check if network is idle and trigger idle event\n   */\n  function checkIdle() {\n    if (pendingRequests.size === 0 && !idleTimer) {\n      // Start idle timer\n      idleTimer = setTimeout(() => {\n        if (pendingRequests.size === 0) {\n          log.debug(() => '🌐 Network idle detected');\n          listeners.onNetworkIdle.forEach((fn) => fn());\n        }\n        idleTimer = null;\n      }, idleTimeout);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Start tracking network requests\n   */\n  function startTracking() {\n    if (isTracking) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    isTracking = true;\n    navigationId++;\n\n    // Clear any existing state\n    pendingRequests.clear();\n    requestStartTimes.clear();\n    if (idleTimer) {\n      clearTimeout(idleTimer);\n      idleTimer = null;\n    }\n\n    // Setup event listeners based on engine\n    if (engine === 'playwright') {\n      page.on('request', onRequest);\n      page.on('requestfinished', onRequestEnd);\n      page.on('requestfailed', onRequestEnd);\n    } else {\n      // Puppeteer\n      page.on('request', onRequest);\n      page.on('requestfinished', onRequestEnd);\n      page.on('requestfailed', onRequestEnd);\n    }\n\n    log.debug(() => '🔌 Network tracking started');\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Stop tracking network requests\n   */\n  function stopTracking() {\n    if (!isTracking) {\n      return;\n    }\n\n    isTracking = false;\n\n    // Remove event listeners\n    if (engine === 'playwright') {\n      page.off('request', onRequest);\n      page.off('requestfinished', onRequestEnd);\n      page.off('requestfailed', onRequestEnd);\n    } else {\n      page.off('request', onRequest);\n      page.off('requestfinished', onRequestEnd);\n      page.off('requestfailed', onRequestEnd);\n    }\n\n    // Clear state\n    pendingRequests.clear();\n    requestStartTimes.clear();\n    if (idleTimer) {\n      clearTimeout(idleTimer);\n      idleTimer = null;\n    }\n\n    log.debug(() => '🔌 Network tracking stopped');\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Wait for network to become idle\n   * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n   * @param {number} options.timeout - Maximum time to wait (default: 30000ms)\n   * @param {number} options.idleTime - Time network must be idle (default: idleTimeout)\n   * @returns {Promise<boolean>} - True if idle, false if timeout\n   */\n  async function waitForNetworkIdle(opts = {}) {\n    const { timeout = 30000, idleTime = idleTimeout } = opts;\n\n    const startTime = Date.now();\n    const currentNavId = navigationId;\n\n    // If already idle, wait for idle time\n    if (pendingRequests.size === 0) {\n      await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, idleTime));\n\n      // Check if still idle and no navigation happened\n      if (pendingRequests.size === 0 && navigationId === currentNavId) {\n        return true;\n      }\n    }\n\n    return new Promise((resolve) => {\n      let resolved = false;\n      let checkTimer = null;\n      let timeoutTimer = null;\n\n      const cleanup = () => {\n        if (checkTimer) {\n          clearInterval(checkTimer);\n        }\n        if (timeoutTimer) {\n          clearTimeout(timeoutTimer);\n        }\n        resolved = true;\n      };\n\n      // Timeout handler\n      timeoutTimer = setTimeout(() => {\n        if (!resolved) {\n          cleanup();\n          log.debug(\n            () =>\n              `⚠️  Network idle timeout after ${timeout}ms (${pendingRequests.size} pending)`\n          );\n\n          // Log stuck requests\n          if (pendingRequests.size > 0) {\n            const stuckRequests = [];\n            for (const [key, req] of pendingRequests) {\n              const startTime = requestStartTimes.get(key);\n              const duration = Date.now() - startTime;\n              const url = typeof req.url === 'function' ? req.url() : req.url;\n              stuckRequests.push(\n                `  ${url.substring(0, 60)}... (${duration}ms)`\n              );\n            }\n            log.debug(() => `⚠️  Stuck requests:\\n${stuckRequests.join('\\n')}`);\n          }\n\n          resolve(false);\n        }\n      }, timeout);\n\n      // Check periodically for idle state\n      checkTimer = setInterval(async () => {\n        if (resolved) {\n          return;\n        }\n\n        // Check for navigation change (abort wait)\n        if (navigationId !== currentNavId) {\n          cleanup();\n          resolve(false);\n          return;\n        }\n\n        // Check for timed out requests and remove them\n        const now = Date.now();\n        for (const [key, startTime] of requestStartTimes) {\n          if (now - startTime > requestTimeout) {\n            log.debug(() => `⚠️  Request timed out, removing: ${key}`);\n            pendingRequests.delete(key);\n            requestStartTimes.delete(key);\n          }\n        }\n\n        // Check if idle\n        if (pendingRequests.size === 0) {\n          // Wait for idle time to confirm\n          await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, idleTime));\n\n          if (\n            !resolved &&\n            pendingRequests.size === 0 &&\n            navigationId === currentNavId\n          ) {\n            cleanup();\n            resolve(true);\n          }\n        }\n      }, 100);\n    });\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get current pending request count\n   */\n  function getPendingCount() {\n    return pendingRequests.size;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Get list of pending request URLs\n   */\n  function getPendingUrls() {\n    const urls = [];\n    for (const [key, req] of pendingRequests) {\n      const url = typeof req.url === 'function' ? req.url() : req.url;\n      urls.push(url);\n    }\n    return urls;\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Add event listener\n   */\n  function on(event, callback) {\n    if (listeners[event]) {\n      listeners[event].push(callback);\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Remove event listener\n   */\n  function off(event, callback) {\n    if (listeners[event]) {\n      const index = listeners[event].indexOf(callback);\n      if (index !== -1) {\n        listeners[event].splice(index, 1);\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  /**\n   * Reset tracking (clear all pending requests)\n   * Called on navigation to start fresh\n   */\n  function reset() {\n    navigationId++;\n    pendingRequests.clear();\n    requestStartTimes.clear();\n    if (idleTimer) {\n      clearTimeout(idleTimer);\n      idleTimer = null;\n    }\n    log.debug(() => '🔄 Network tracker reset');\n  }\n\n  return {\n    startTracking,\n    stopTracking,\n    waitForNetworkIdle,\n    getPendingCount,\n    getPendingUrls,\n    reset,\n    on,\n    off,\n  };\n}\n","numLines":374,"startLine":1,"totalLines":374}}}

6→ */\n     7→\n     8→import { TIMING } from '../core/constants.js';\n     9→import { isNavigationError } from '../core/navigation-safety.js';\n    10→import { isActionStoppedError } from '../core/page-trigger-manager.js';\n    11→\n    12→/**\n    13→ * Default verification function for navigation operations.\n    14→ * Verifies that navigation completed by checking:\n    15→ * - URL matches expected pattern (if provided)\n    16→ * - Page is in a ready state\n    17→ *\n    18→ * @param {Object} options - Verification options\n    19→ * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n    20→ * @param {string} options.expectedUrl - Expected URL or URL pattern (optional)\n    21→ * @param {string} options.startUrl - URL before navigation\n    22→ * @returns {Promise<{verified: boolean, actualUrl: string, reason: string}>}\n    23→ */\n    24→export async function defaultNavigationVerification(options = {}) {\n    25→  const { page, expectedUrl, startUrl } = options;\n    26→\n    27→  try {\n    28→    const actualUrl = page.url();\n    29→\n    30→    // If expected URL is provided, verify it matches\n    31→    if (expectedUrl) {\n    32→      // Check for exact match or pattern match\n    33→      if (actualUrl === expectedUrl) {\n    34→        return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'exact URL match' };\n    35→      }\n    36→      // Check if expected URL is contained in actual URL (for patterns)\n    37→      if (\n    38→        actualUrl.includes(expectedUrl) ||\n    39→        actualUrl.startsWith(expectedUrl)\n    40→      ) {\n    41→        return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'URL pattern match' };\n    42→      }\n    43→      // Check if it's a regex pattern\n    44→      if (expectedUrl instanceof RegExp && expectedUrl.test(actualUrl)) {\n    45→        return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'URL regex match' };\n    46→      }\n    47→\n    48→      return {\n    49→        verified: false,\n    50→        actualUrl,\n    51→        reason: `URL mismatch: expected \"${expectedUrl}\", got \"${actualUrl}\"`,\n    52→      };\n    53→    }\n    54→\n    55→    // No expected URL - just verify URL changed from start\n    56→    if (startUrl && actualUrl !== startUrl) {\n    57→      return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'URL changed from start' };\n    58→    }\n    59→\n    60→    // If no start URL and no expected URL, assume success\n    61→    return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'navigation completed' };\n    62→  } catch (error) {\n    63→    if (isNavigationError(error) || isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n    64→      return {\n    65→        verified: false,\n    66→        actualUrl: '',\n    67→        reason: 'error during verification',\n    68→        navigationError: true,\n    69→      };\n    70→    }\n    71→    throw error;\n    72→  }\n    73→}\n    74→\n    75→/**\n    76→ * Verify navigation operation with retry logic\n    77→ * @param {Object} options - Verification options\n    78→ * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n    79→ * @param {string} options.expectedUrl - Expected URL (optional)\n    80→ * @param {string} options.startUrl - URL before navigation\n    81→ * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional)\n    82→ * @param {number} options.timeout - Verification timeout in ms (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT)\n    83→ * @param {number} options.retryInterval - Interval between retries (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_RETRY_INTERVAL)\n    84→ * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n    85→ * @returns {Promise<{verified: boolean, actualUrl: string, reason: string, attempts: number}>}\n    86→ */\n    87→export async function verifyNavigation(options = {}) {\n    88→  const {\n    89→    page,\n    90→    expectedUrl,\n    91→    startUrl,\n    92→    verifyFn = defaultNavigationVerification,\n    93→    timeout = TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT,\n    94→    retryInterval = TIMING.VERIFICATION_RETRY_INTERVAL,\n    95→    log = { debug: () => {} },\n    96→  } = options;\n    97→\n    98→  const startTime = Date.now();\n    99→  let attempts = 0;\n   100→  let lastResult = { verified: false, actualUrl: '', reason: '' };\n   101→\n   102→  while (Date.now() - 115→      return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n   116→    }\n   117→\n   118→    if (lastResult.navigationError) {\n   119→      log.debug(() => '⚠️  Navigation/stop detected during verification');\n   120→      return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n   121→    }\n   122→\n   123→    // Wait before next retry\n   124→    await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, retryInterval));\n   125→  }\n   126→\n   127→  log.debug(\n   128→    () =>\n   129→      `❌ Navigation verification failed after ${attempts} attempts: ${lastResult.reason}`\n   130→  );\n   131→  return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n   132→}\n   133→\n   134→/**\n   135→ * Wait for URL to stabilize (no redirects happening)\n   136→ * This is a legacy polling-based approach for backwards compatibility.\n   137→ * When navigationManager is available, use waitForPageReady instead.\n   138→ *\n   139→ * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n   140→ * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n   141→ * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n   142→ * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n   143→ * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (optional)\n   144→ * @param {number} options.stableChecks - Number of consecutive stable checks required (default: 3)\n   145→ * @param {number} options.checkInterval - Interval between stability checks in ms (default: 1000)\n   146→ * @param {number} options.timeout - Maximum time to wait for stabilization in ms (default: 30000)\n   147→ * @param {string} options.reason - Reason for [2026-01-13T18:05:37.134Z] [INFO] age - Browser page object\n   388→ * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (required for full functionality)\n   389→ * @param {Object} options.networkTracker - startTime;\n   423→    if (networkIdle) {\n   424→      log.debug(() => `✅ Page ready after ${elapsed}ms (${reason})`);\n   425→    } else {\n   426→      log.debug(() => `⚠️  Page ready timeout after ${elapsed}ms (${reason})`);\n   427→    }\n   428→\n   429→    return networkIdle;\n   430→  }\n   431→\n   432→  // Minimal fallback: just wait a bit for DOM to settle\n   433→  log.debug(() => `⏳ Waiting for page ready - minimal mode (${reason})...`);\n   434→  await wait({ ms: 1000, reason: 'page settle time' });\n   435→  return true;\n   436→}\n   437→\n   438→/**\n   439→ * Wait for any ongoing navigation and network requests to complete.\n   440→ * Use this after actions that might trigger navigation (like clicks).\n   441→ *\n   442→ * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n   443→ * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n   444→ * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance\n   445→ * @param {Object} options.networkTracker - NetworkTracker instance\n   446→ * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n   447→ * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n   448→ * @param {number} options.navigationCheckDelay - Time to wait for potential navigation to start (default: 500ms)\n   449→ * @param {number} options.timeout - Maximum time to wait (default: 30000ms)\n   450→ * @param {string} options.reason - Reason for waiting (for logging)\n   451→ * @returns {Promise<{navigated: boolean, ready: boolean}>}\n   452→ */\n   453→export async function waitAfterAction(options = {}) {\n   454→  const {\n   455→    page,\n   456→    navigationManager,\n   457→    networkTracker,\n   458→    log,\n   459→    wait,\n   460→    navigationCheckDelay = 500,\n   461→    timeout = 30000,\n   462→    reason = 'after action',\n   463→  } = options;\n   464→\n   465→  const startUrl = page.url();\n   466→  const startTime = Date.now();\n   467→\n   468→  log.debug(() => `⏳ Waiting after action (${reason})...`);\n   469→\n   470→  // Wait briefly for potential navigation to start\n   471→  await wait({ ms: navigationCheckDelay, reason: 'checking for navigation' });\n   472→\n   473→  // Check if navigation is in progress or URL changed\n   474→  const currentUrl = page.url();\n   475→  const urlChanged = currentUrl !== startUrl;\n   476→\n   477→  if (navigationManager && navigationManager.isNavigating()) {\n   478→    log.debug(() => '🔄 Navigation in progress, waiting for completion...');\n   479→    await navigationManager.waitForNavigation({\n   480→      timeout: timeout - (Date.now() - startTime),\n   481→    });\n   482→    return { navigated: true, ready: true };\n   483→  }\n   484→\n   485→  if (urlChanged) {\n   486→    log.debug(() => `🔄 URL changed: ${startUrl} → ${currentUrl}`);\n   487→\n   488→    // Wait for page to be fully ready\n   489→    await waitForPageReady({\n   490→      page,\n   491→      navigationManager,\n   492→      networkTracker,\n   493→      log,\n   494→      wait,\n   495→      timeout: timeout - (Date.now() - startTime),\n   496→      reason: 'after URL change',\n   497→    });\n   498→\n   499→    return { navigated: true, ready: true };\n   500→  }\n   501→\n   502→  // No navigation detected, just wait for network idle\n   503→  // Use shorter idle time since this is just for XHR completion, not full page load\n   504→  if (networkTracker) {\n   505→    const idle = await networkTracker.waitForNetworkIdle({\n   506→      timeout: Math.max(0, timeout - (Date.now() - startTime)),\n   507→      idleTime: 2000, // Shorter idle time for non-navigation actions\n   508→    });\n   509→    return { navigated: false, ready: idle };\n   510→  }\n   511→\n   512→  return { navigated: false, ready: true };\n   513→}\n   514→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware.
