    \"target frame detached\",\n        \"web element reference\",\n    ]\n\n    all_patterns = playwright_patterns + selenium_patterns\n\n    return any(pattern in error_message for pattern in all_patterns)\n\n\ndef is_timeout_error(error: Exception) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if an error is a timeout error.\n\n    Args:\n        error: The exception to check\n\n    Returns:\n        True if this is a timeout error\n    \"\"\"\n    error_message = str(error).lower()\n\n    timeout_patterns = [\n        \"timeout\",\n        \"timed out\",\n        \"exceeded\",\n        \"deadline\",\n    ]\n\n    return any(pattern in error_message for pattern in timeout_patterns)\n\n\ndef safe_operation(\n    default_value: Any = None,\n    log_message: str | None = None,\n):\n    \"\"\"Decorator that wraps a function to handle navigation errors gracefully.\n\n    Args:\n        default_value: Value to return if a navigation error occurs\n        log_message: Optional message to log when recovering from navigation error\n\n    Returns:\n        Decorator function\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def decorator(func):\n        async def async_wrapper(*args, **kwargs):\n            try:\n                return await func(*args, **kwargs)\n            except Exception as e:\n                if is_navigation_error(e):\n                    if log_message:\n                        print(f\"[WARN] {log_message}\")\n                    return default_value\n                raise\n\n        def sync_wrapper(*args, **kwargs):\n            try:\n                return func(*args, **kwargs)\n            except Exception as e:\n                if is_navigation_error(e):\n                    if log_message:\n                        print(f\"[WARN] {log_message}\")\n                    return default_value\n                raise\n\n        import asyncio\n\n        if asyncio.iscoroutinefunction(func):\n            return async_wrapper\n        return sync_wrapper\n\n    return           "file_path": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1768327375795/python/src/browser_commander/core/engine_adapter.py",
          "content": "\"\"\"Engine Adapter - Abstract away Playwright/Selenium differences.\n\nThis module implements the Adapter pattern to encapsulate engine-specific\nlogic in a single place, following the \"Protected Variations\" principle.\n\nBenefits:\n- Eliminates scattered `if engine == 'playwright'` checks\n- Easier to add new engines\n- Easier to test with mock adapters\n- Clearer separation of concerns\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom abc import ABC, abstractmethod\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom browser_commander.core.constants import TIMING\nfrom browser_commander.core.engine_detection import EngineType\n\n\nclass EngineAdapter(ABC):\n    \"\"\"Base class defining the engine adapter interface.\n\n    All engine-specific operations should be defined here.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, page: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize the adapter with a page/driver object.\n\n        Args:\n            page: Playwright page or Selenium WebDriver\n        \"\"\"\n        self.page = page\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    def get_engine_name(self) -> EngineType:\n        \"\"\"Get engine name.\n\n        Returns:\n            Engine type: 'playwright' or 'selenium'\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    # =========================================================================\n    # Element Selection and Locators\n    # =========================================================================\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    def create_locator(self, selector: str) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Create a locator/element from a selector.\n\n        Args:\n            selector: CSS selector\n\n        Returns:\n            Locator (Playwright) or WebElement (Selenium)\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def query_selector(self, selector: str) -> Any | None:\n        \"\"\"Query single element.\n\n        Args:\n            selector: CSS selector\n\n        Returns:\n            Locator/Element or None\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def query_selector_all(self, selector: str) -> list[Any]:\n        \"\"\"Query all elements.\n\n        Args:\n            selector: CSS selector\n\n        Returns:\n            List of locators/elements\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def wait_for_selector(\n        self,\n        selector: str,\n        visible: bool = True,\n        timeout: int = TIMING[\"DEFAULT_TIMEOUT\"],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Wait for selector to appear.\n\n        Args:\n            selector: CSS selector\n            visible: Wait for visibility\n            timeout: Timeout in milliseconds\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def wait_for_visible(\n        self,\n        locator_or_element: Any,\n        timeout: int = TIMING[\"DEFAULT_TIMEOUT\"],\n    ) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Wait for element to be visible.\n\n        Args:\n            locator_or_element: Locator or element\n            timeout: Timeout in milliseconds\n\n        Returns:\n            The locator/element\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def count(self, selector: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Count matching elements.\n\n        Args:\n            selector: CSS selector\n\n        Returns:\n            Number of matching elements\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    # =========================================================================\n    # Element Evaluation and Properties\n    # =========================================================================\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def evaluate_on_element(\n        self,\n        locator_or_element: Any,\n        script: str,\n        *args: Any,\n    ) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Evaluate JavaScript on element.\n\n        Args:\n            locator_or_element: Locator or element\n            script: JavaScript code to evaluate\n            *args: Arguments to pass to the script\n\n        Returns:\n            Result of evaluation\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def get_text_content(self, locator_or_element: Any) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Get element text content.\n\n        Args:\n            locator_or_element: Locator or element\n\n        Returns:\n            Text content or None\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def get_input_value(self, locator_or_element: Any) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get input value.\n\n        Args:\n            locator_or_element: Locator or element\n\n        Returns:\n            Input value\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def get_attribute(\n        self,\n        locator_or_element: Any,\n        attribute: str,\n    ) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Get element attribute.\n\n        Args:\n            locator_or_element: Locator or element\n            attribute: Attribute name\n\n        Returns:\n            Attribute value or None\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    # =========================================================================\n    # Element Interactions\n    # =========================================================================\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def click(\n        self,\n        locator_or_element: Any,\n        force: bool = False,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Click element.\n\n        Args:\n            locator_or_element: Locator or element\n            force: Force click without checks\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def type_text(self, locator_or_element: Any, text: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Type text into element (simulates typing).\n\n        Args:\n            locator_or_element: Locator or element\n            text: Text to type\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def fill(self, locator_or_element: Any, text: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Fill element with text (direct value assignment).\n\n        Args:\n            locator_or_element: Locator or element\n            text: Text to fill\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def focus(self, locator_or_element: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Focus element.\n\n        Args:\n            locator_or_element: Locator or element\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    # =========================================================================\n    # Page-level Operations\n    # =========================================================================\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def evaluate_on_page(self, script: str, *args: Any) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Evaluate JavaScript in page context.\n\n        Args:\n            script: JavaScript code to evaluate\n            *args: Arguments to pass to the script\n\n        Returns:\n            Result of evaluation\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    def get_url(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get current page URL.\n\n        Returns:\n            Current URL\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n    @abstractmethod\n    async def goto(\n        self,\n        url: str,\n        timeout: int = TIMING[\"NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT\"],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Navigate to URL.\n\n        Args:\n            url: URL to navigate to\n            timeout: Timeout in milliseconds\n        \"\"\"\n        ...\n\n\nclass PlaywrightAdapter(EngineAdapter):\n    \"\"\"Playwright adapter implementation.\"\"\"\n\n    def get_engine_name(self) -> EngineType:\n        \"\"\"Get engine name.\"\"\"\n        return \"playwright\"\n\n    def create_locator(self, selector: str) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Create a Playwright locator.\"\"\"\n        # Handle :nth-of-type() pseudo-selectors\n        import re\n\n        match = re.match(r\"^(.+):nth-of-type\\((\\d+)\\)$\", selector)\n        if match:\n            base_selector = match.group(1)\n            index = int(match.group(2)) - 1  # Convert to 0-based\n            return self.page.locator(base_selector).nth(index)\n        return self.page.locator(selector)\n\n    async def query_selector(self, selector: str) -> Any | None:\n        \"\"\"Query single element.\"\"\"\n        locator = self.create_locator(selector).first\n        count = await locator.count()\n        return locator if count > 0 else None\n\n    async def query_selector_all(self, selector: str) -> list[Any]:\n        \"\"\"Query all elements.\"\"\"\n        locator = self.create_locator(selector)\n        count = await locator.count()\n        return [locator.nth(i) for i in range(count)]\n\n    async def wait_for_selector(\n        self,\n        selector: str,\n        visible: bool = True,\n        timeout: int = TIMING[\"DEFAULT_TIMEOUT\"],\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Wait for selector to appear.\"\"\"\n        locator = self.create_locator(selector)\n        state = \"visible\" if visible else \"attached\"\n        await locator.wait_for(state=state, timeout=timeout)\n\n    async def wait_for_visible(\n        self,\n        locator_or_element: Any,\n        timeout: int = TIMING[\"DEFAULT_TIMEOUT\"],\n    ) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Wait for element to be visible.\"\"\"\n        first_locator = locator_or_element.first\n        await first_locator.wait_for(state=\"visible\", timeout=timeout)\n        return first_locator\n\n    async def count(self, selector: str) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Count matching elements.\"\"\"\n        return await self.page.locator(selector).count()\n\n    async def evaluate_on_element(\n        self,\n        locator_or_element: Any,\n        script: str,\n        *args: Any,\n    ) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Evaluate JavaScript on element.\"\"\"\n        if args:\n            return await locator_or_element.evaluate(script, args[0])\n        return await locator_or_element.evaluate(script)\n\n    async def get_text_content(self, locator_or_element: Any) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Get element text content.\"\"\"\n        return await locator_or_element.text_content()\n\n    async def get_input_value(self, locator_or_element: Any) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get input value.\"\"\"\n        return await locator_or_element.input_value()\n\n    async def get_attribute(\n        self,\n        locator_or_element: Any,\n        attribute: str,\n    ) -> str | None:\n        \"\"\"Get element attribute.\"\"\"\n        return await locator_or_element.get_attribute(attribute)\n\n    async def click(\n        self,\n        locator_or_element: Any,\n        force: bool = False,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Click element.\"\"\"\n        await locator_or_element.click(force=force)\n\n    async def type_text(self, locator_or_element: Any, text: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Type text into element.\"\"\"\n        await locator_or_element.type(text)\n\n    async def fill(self, locator_or_element: Any, text: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Fill element with text.\"\"\"\n        await locator_or_element.fill(text)\n\n    async def focus(self, locator_or_element: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Focus element.\"\"\"\n        await locator_or_element.focus()\n\n    async def evaluate_on_page(self, script: str, *args: Any) -> Any:\n        \"\"\"Evaluate JavaScript in page context.\"\"\"\n        if not args:\n            return await self.page.evaluate(script)\n        if len(args) == 1:\n            return await self.page.evaluate(script, args[0])\n        # Multiple args - pass as array\n        return await self.page.evaluate(script, list(args))\n\n    def           "content": "\"\"\"NetworkTracker - Track all HTTP requests and wait for network idle.\n\nThis module monitors all network requests on a page and provides\nmethods to wait until all requests are complete (network idle).\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport time\nfrom typing import Any, Callable\n\nfrom browser_commander.core.constants import TIMING\nfrom browser_commander.core.engine_detection import EngineType\nfrom browser_commander.core.logger import Logger\n\n\nclass NetworkTracker:\n    \"\"\"Track network requests and detect network idle state.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        page: Any,\n        engine: EngineType,\n        log: Logger,\n        idle_timeout: int = 500,\n        request_timeout: int = 30000,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize NetworkTracker.\n\n        Args:\n            page: Playwright page or Selenium WebDriver\n            engine: Engine type\n            log: Logger instance\n            idle_timeout: Time to wait after last request completes (ms)\n            request_timeout: Maximum time to wait for a single request (ms)\n        \"\"\"\n        self.page = page\n        self.engine = engine\n        self.log = log\n        self.idle_timeout = idle_timeout\n        self.request_timeout = request_timeout\n\n        self._pending_requests: dict[str, Any] = {}\n        self._request_start_times: dict[str, float] = {}\n        self._listeners: dict[str, list[Callable]] = {\n            \"on_request_start\": [],\n            \"on_request_end\": [],\n            \"on_network_idle\": [],\n        }\n        self._is_tracking = False\n        self._idle_timer: asyncio.TimerHandle | None = None\n        self._navigation_id = 0\n\n    def _get_request_key(self, request: Any) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get unique request key.\"\"\"\n        if self.engine == \"playwright\":\n            url = request.url\n            method = request.method\n        else:\n            # For Selenium, we don't have direct request access\n            url = str(request)\n            method = \"GET\"\n        return f\"{method}:{url}\"\n\n    def _on_request(self, request: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle request start.\"\"\"\n        key = self._get_request_key(request)\n\n        if self.engine == \"playwright\":\n            url = request.url\n        else:\n            url = str(request)\n\n        # Ignore data URLs and blob URLs\n        if url.startswith(\"data:\") or url.startswith(\"blob:\"):\n            return\n\n        self._pending_requests[key] = request\n        self._request_start_times[key] = time.time() * 1000\n\n        # Clear idle timer since we have a new request\n        if self._idle_timer:\n            self._idle_timer.cancel()\n            self._idle_timer = None\n\n        self.log.debug(\n            lambda: f\"Request started: {url[:80]}... (pending: {len(self._pending_requests)})\"\n        )\n\n        # Notify listeners\n        for fn in self._listeners[\"on_request_start\"]:\n            fn({\"url\": url, \"pending_count\": len(self._pending_requests)})\n\n    def _on_request_end(self, request: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle request completion (success or failure).\"\"\"\n        key = self._get_request_key(request)\n\n        if key not in self._pending_requests:\n            return\n\n        if self.engine == \"playwright\":\n            url = request.url\n        else:\n            url = str(request)\n\n        del self._pending_requests[key]\n        if key in self._request_start_times:\n            del self._request_start_times[key]\n\n        self.log.debug(\n            lambda: f\"Request ended: {url[:80]}... (pending: {len(self._pending_requests)})\"\n        )\n\n        # Notify listeners\n        for fn in self._listeners[\"on_request_end\"]:\n            fn({\"url\": url, \"pending_count\": len(self._pending_requests)})\n\n        # Check if we're now idle\n        self._check_idle()\n\n    def _check_idle(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Check if network is idle and trigger idle event.\"\"\"\n        if len(self._pending_requests) == 0 and not self._idle_timer:\n            # Start idle timer (we can't use asyncio timer handle here directly)\n            # For now, idle detection will be handled in wait_for_network_idle\n            self.log.debug(lambda: \"Network idle detected\")\n            for fn in self._listeners[\"on_network_idle\"]:\n                fn()\n\n    def start_tracking(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start tracking network requests.\"\"\"\n        if self._is_tracking:\n            return\n\n        self._is_tracking = True\n        self._navigation_id += 1\n\n        # Clear any existing state\n        self._pending_requests.clear()\n        self._request_start_times.clear()\n\n        if self.engine == \"playwright\":\n            # Setup Playwright event listeners\n            self.page.on(\"request\", self._on_request)\n            self.page.on(\"requestfinished\", self._on_request_end)\n            self.page.on(\"requestfailed\", self._on_request_end)\n\n        self.log.debug(lambda: \"Network tracking started\")\n\n    def stop_tracking(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop tracking network requests.\"\"\"\n        if not self._is_tracking:\n            return\n\n        self._is_tracking = False\n\n        if self.engine == \"playwright\":\n            # Remove Playwright event listeners\n            self.page.remove_listener(\"request\", self._on_request)\n            self.page.remove_listener(\"requestfinished\", self._on_request_end)\n            self.page.remove_listener(\"requestfailed\", self._on_request_end)\n\n        # Clear state\n        self._pending_requests.clear()\n        self._request_start_times.clear()\n\n        self.log.debug(lambda: \"Network tracking stopped\")\n\n    async def wait_for_network_idle(\n        self,\n        timeout: int = TIMING[\"NETWORK_IDLE_TIMEOUT\"],\n        idle_time: int | None = None,\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Wait for network to become idle.\n\n        Args:\n            timeout: Maximum time to wait (ms)\n            idle_time: Time network must be idle (ms), defaults to idle_timeout\n\n        Returns:\n            True if idle, False if timeout\n        \"\"\"\n        if idle_time is None:\n            idle_time = self.idle_timeout\n\n        start_time = time.time() * 1000\n        current_nav_id = self._navigation_id\n\n        # If already idle, wait for idle time\n        if len(self._pending_requests) == 0:\n            await asyncio.sleep(idle_time / 1000)\n            if (\n                len(self._pending_requests) == 0\n                and self._navigation_id == current_nav_id\n            ):\n                return True\n\n        # Poll until idle or timeout\n        while True:\n            elapsed = time.time() * 1000 - start_time\n            if elapsed >= timeout:\n                self.log.debug(\n                    lambda: f\"Network idle timeout after {timeout}ms \"\n                    f\"({len(self._pending_requests)} pending)\"\n                )\n                return False\n\n            # Check for navigation change\n            if self._navigation_id != current_nav_id:\n                return False\n\n            # Check for timed out requests\n            now = time.time() * 1000\n            for key, start in list(self._request_start_times.items()):\n                if now - start > self.request_timeout:\n                    self.log.debug(lambda: f\"Request timed out, removing: {key}\")\n                    if key in self._pending_requests:\n                        del self._pending_requests[key]\n                    del self._request_start_times[key]\n\n            # Check if idle\n            if len(self._pending_requests) == 0:\n                await asyncio.sleep(idle_time / 1000)\n                if (\n                    len(self._pending_requests) == 0\n                    and self._navigation_id == current_nav_id\n                ):\n                    return True\n\n            await asyncio.sleep(0.1)  # 100ms check interval\n\n    def get_pending_count(self) -> int:\n        \"\"\"Get current pending request count.\"\"\"\n        return len(self._pending_requests)\n\n    def get_pending_urls(self) -> list[str]:\n        \"\"\"Get list of pending request URLs.\"\"\"\n        urls = []\n        for key, req in self._pending_requests.items():\n            if self.engine == \"playwright\":\n                urls.append(req.url)\n            else:\n                urls.append(str(req))\n        return urls\n\n    def on(self, event: str, callback: Callable) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Add event listener.\"\"\"\n        if event in self._listeners:\n            self._listeners[event].append(callback)\n\n    def off(self, event: str, callback: Callable) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Remove event listener.\"\"\"\n        if event in self._listeners and callback in self._listeners[event]:\n            self._listeners[event].remove(callback)\n\n    def reset(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Reset tracking (clear all pending requests).\n\n        Called on navigation to start fresh.\n        \"\"\"\n        self._navigation_id += 1\n        self._pending_requests.clear()\n        self._request_start_times.clear()\n        self.log.debug(lambda: \"Network tracker reset\")\n\n\ndef create_network_tracker(\n    page: Any,\n    engine: EngineType,\n    log: Logger,\n    idle_timeout: int = 500,\n    request_timeout: int = 30000,\n) -> NetworkTracker:\n    \"\"\"Create a NetworkTracker instance for a page.\n\n    Args:\n        page: Playwright page or Selenium WebDriver\n        engine: Engine type\n        log: Logger instance\n        idle_timeout: Time to wait after last request completes (ms)\n        request_timeout: Maximum time to wait for a single request (ms)\n\n    Returns:\n        NetworkTracker instance\n    \"\"\"\n    return NetworkTracker(\n        page=page,\n        engine=engine,\n        log=log,\n        idle_timeout=idle_timeout,\n        request_timeout=request_timeout,\n    )\n"

          "content": "\"\"\"NavigationManager - Monitor URL changes and manage navigation state.\n\nThis module provides navigation lifecycle management including:\n- URL change detection\n- Navigation start/complete events\n- Page ready state tracking\n- Abort signal management for stoppable actions\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport time\nfrom typing import Any, Callable\n\nfrom browser_commander.core.constants import TIMING\nfrom browser_commander.core.engine_detection import EngineType\nfrom browser_commander.core.logger import Logger\nfrom browser_commander.core.network_tracker import NetworkTracker\n\n\nclass NavigationManager:\n    \"\"\"Manage navigation lifecycle and state.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        page: Any,\n        engine: EngineType,\n        log: Logger,\n        network_tracker: NetworkTracker | None = None,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize NavigationManager.\n\n        Args:\n            page: Playwright page or Selenium WebDriver\n            engine: Engine type\n            log: Logger instance\n            network_tracker: Optional NetworkTracker for network idle detection\n        \"\"\"\n        self.page = page\n        self.engine = engine\n        self.log = log\n        self.network_tracker = network_tracker\n\n        self._is_listening = False\n        self._is_navigating = False\n        self._last_url = \"\"\n        self._abort_controller: asyncio.Event | None = None\n        self._listeners: dict[str, list[Callable]] = {\n            \"on_navigation_start\": [],\n            \"on_navigation_complete\": [],\n            \"on_url_change\": [],\n            \"on_page_ready\": [],\n        }\n\n    def start_listening(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Start listening for navigation events.\"\"\"\n        if self._is_listening:\n            return\n\n        self._is_listening = True\n        self._last_url = self._get_current_url()\n        self._abort_controller = asyncio.Event()\n\n        if self.engine == \"playwright\":\n            # Setup Playwright navigation listeners\n            self.page.on(\"framenavigated\", self._on_frame_navigated)\n\n        self.log.debug(lambda: \"Navigation manager started listening\")\n\n    def stop_listening(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Stop listening for navigation events.\"\"\"\n        if not self._is_listening:\n            return\n\n        self._is_listening = False\n\n        if self.engine == \"playwright\":\n            self.page.remove_listener(\"framenavigated\", self._on_frame_navigated)\n\n        self.log.debug(lambda: \"Navigation manager stopped listening\")\n\n    def _get_current_url(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get current page URL.\"\"\"\n        if self.engine == \"playwright\":\n            return self.page.url\n        return self.page.current_url\n\n    def _on_frame_navigated(self, frame: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle frame navigation event (Playwright).\"\"\"\n        # Only care about main frame navigation\n        if frame != self.page.main_frame:\n            return\n\n        new_url = self._get_current_url()\n        if new_url != self._last_url:\n            self._on_url_change(new_url)\n\n    def _on_url_change(self, new_url: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle URL change.\"\"\"\n        old_url = self._last_url\n        self._last_url = new_url\n\n        self.log.debug(lambda: f\"URL changed: {old_url} -> {new_url}\")\n\n        # Signal abort to any running actions\n        if self._abort_controller:\n            self._abort_controller.set()\n            self._abort_controller = asyncio.Event()\n\n        # Set navigating state\n        self._is_navigating = True\n\n        # Notify listeners\n        for fn in self._listeners[\"on_url_change\"]:\n            fn({\"old_url\": old_url, \"new_url\": new_url})\n\n        for fn in self._listeners[\"on_navigation_start\"]:\n            fn({\"url\": new_url})\n\n    def is_navigating(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if navigation is in progress.\"\"\"\n        return self._is_navigating\n\n    def should_abort(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if actions should abort due to navigation.\"\"\"\n        return self._abort_controller is not None and self._abort_controller.is_set()\n\n    def get_abort_signal(self) -> asyncio.Event | None:\n        \"\"\"Get the current abort signal.\"\"\"\n        return self._abort_controller\n\n    async def navigate(\n        self,\n        url: str,\n        wait_until: str = \"domcontentloaded\",\n        timeout: int = TIMING[\"NAVIGATION_TIMEOUT\"],\n    ) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Navigate to URL with full wait handling.\n\n        Args:\n            url: URL to navigate to\n            wait_until: Wait condition ('load', 'domcontentloaded', 'networkidle')\n            timeout: Timeout in \"\"\"Create a URL condition from various pattern types.\n\n    Supports:\n    - Exact match: 'https://example.com/page'\n    - Wildcard: '*checkout*', '/api/*', '*.json'\n    - Express-style patterns: '/vacancy/:id', 'https://hh.ru/vacancy/:vacancyId'\n    - RegExp: re.compile(r'/product/\\\\d+')\n    - Custom function: lambda url: bool\n\n    Args:\n        pattern: URL pattern to match\n\n    Returns:\n        Function that checks if URL matches the pattern\n    \"\"\"\n    # Already a function\n    if callable(pattern):\n        return pattern\n\n    # Compiled regex\n    if isinstance(pattern, re.Pattern):\n        return lambda url: pattern.search(url) is not None\n\n    # String pattern\n    if isinstance(pattern, str):\n        # Convert wildcard pattern to regex\n        if \"*\" in pattern:\n            # Escape special regex chars except *\n            escaped = re.escape(pattern).replace(r\"\\*\", \".*\")\n            regex = re.compile(escaped)\n param_pattern = re.sub(r\":(\\w+)\", r\"(?P<\\1>[^/]+)\", pattern)\n            regex = re.compile(param_pattern)\n            return lambda url: regex.search(url) is not None\n\n        # Exact safely stopped when navigation occurs.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport re\nfrom typing import Any, Callable\n\nfrom browser_commander.core.logger import Logger\nfrom browser_commander.core.navigation_manager import NavigationManager\n\n\nclass ActionStoppedError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Exception raised when an action is stopped due to navigation.\"\"\"\n\n    pass\n\n\ndef is_action_stopped_error(error: Exception) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if an error is an ActionStoppedError.\n\n    Args:\n        error: The exception to check\n\n    Returns:\n        True if this is an ActionStoppedError\n    \"\"\"\n    return isinstance(error, ActionStoppedError)\n\n\ndef make_url_condition(pattern: str | re.Pattern | Callable) -> Callable[[str], bool]:\n    \"\"\"Create a URL condition from various pattern types.\n\n    Supports:\n    - Exact match: 'https://example.com/page'\n    - Wildcard: '*checkout*', '/api/*', '*.json'\n    - Express-style patterns: '/vacancy/:id', 'https://hh.ru/vacancy/:vacancyId'\n    - RegExp: re.compile(r'/product/\\\\d+')\n    - Custom function: lambda url: bool\n\n    Args:\n        pattern: URL pattern to match\n\n    Returns:\n        Function that checks if URL matches the pattern\n    \"\"\"\n    # Already a function\n    if callable(pattern):\n        return pattern\n\n    # Compiled regex\n    if isinstance(pattern, re.Pattern):\n        return lambda url: pattern.search(url) is not None\n\n    # String pattern\n    if isinstance(pattern, str):\n        # Convert wildcard pattern to regex\n        if \"*\" in pattern:\n            # Escape special regex chars except *\n            escaped = re.escape(pattern).replace(r\"\\*\", \".*\")\n            regex = re.compile(escaped)\n            return lambda url: regex.search(url) is not None\n\n        # Check for Express-style pattern with :param\n        if \":\" in pattern:\n            # Convert :param to regex group\n            param_pattern = re.sub(r\":(\\w+)\", r\"(?P<\\1>[^/]+)\", pattern)\n            regex = re.compile(param_pattern)\n            return lambda url: regex.search(url) is not None\n\n        # Exact match\n        return lambda url: url == pattern or url.startswith(pattern)\n\n    msg = f\"Invalid pattern type: {type(pattern)}\"\n    raise ValueError(msg)\n\n\ndef all_conditions(*conditions: Callable[[str], bool]) -> Callable[[str], bool]:\n    \"\"\"Combine conditions with AND logic.\n\n    Args:\n        *conditions: URL condition functions\n\n    Returns:\n        Function that returns True if all conditions match\n    \"\"\"\n    return lambda url: all(cond(url) for cond in conditions)\n\n\ndef any_condition(*conditions: Callable[[str], bool]) -> Callable[[str], bool]:\n    \"\"\"Combine conditions with OR logic.\n\n    Args:\n        *conditions: URL condition functions\n\n    Returns:\n        Function that returns True if any condition matches\n    \"\"\"\n    return lambda url: any(cond(url) for cond in conditions)\n\n\ndef not_condition(condition: Callable[[str], bool]) -> Callable[[str], bool]:\n    \"\"\"Negate a condition.\n\n    Args:\n        condition: URL condition function\n\n    Returns:\n        Function that returns True if condition doesn't match\n    \"\"\"\n    return lambda url: not condition(url)\n\n\nclass ActionContext:\n    \"\"\"Context passed to trigger actions for safe execution.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        url: str,\n        trigger_name: str,\n        abort_signal: asyncio.Event,\n        commander: Any,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize action context.\n\n        Args:\n            url: Current page URL\n            trigger_name: Name of the trigger\n            abort_signal: Abort signal event\n            commander: Browser commander instance\n        \"\"\"\n        self.url = url\n        self.trigger_name = trigger_name\n        self._abort_signal = abort_signal\n        self.commander = commander\n        self._cleanup_callbacks: list[Callable] = []\n\n    def is_stopped(self) -> bool:\n        \"\"\"Check if action should stop.\"\"\"\n        return self._abort_signal.is_set()\n\n    def check_stopped(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Raise ActionStoppedError if action should stop.\"\"\"\n        if self.is_stopped():\n            raise ActionStoppedError(f\"Action '{self.trigger_name}' was stopped\")\n\n    async def wait(self, ms: int) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Safe wait that respects abort signal.\n\n        Args:\n            ms: Milliseconds to wait\n\n        Raises:\n            ActionStoppedError: If action is stopped during wait\n        \"\"\"\n        try:\n            await asyncio.wait_for(\n                self._abort_signal.wait(),\n                timeout=ms / 1000,\n            )\n            # If we get here, abort was signaled\n            raise ActionStoppedError(f\"Action '{self.trigger_name}' was stopped\")\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n            # Timeout means wait completed normally\n            pass\n\n    async def for_each(\n        self,\n        items: list[Any],\n        fn: Callable[[Any, int], Any],\n    ) -> list[Any]:\n        \"\"\"Safe iteration that checks for stop between items.\n\n        Args:\n            items: Items to iterate over\n            fn: Async function to call for each item (item, index)\n\n        Returns:\n            List of results\n\n        Raises:\n            ActionStoppedError: If action is stopped during iteration\n        \"\"\"\n        results = []\n        for i, item in enumerate(items):\n            self.check_stopped()\n            result = await fn(item, i)\n            results.append(result)\n        return results\n\n    def on_cleanup(self, callback: Callable) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Register cleanup callback.\n\n        Args:\n            callback: Function to call during cleanup\n        \"\"\"\n        self._cleanup_callbacks.append(callback)\n\n    async def cleanup(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Run all cleanup callbacks.\"\"\"\n        for callback in self._cleanup_callbacks:\n            try:\n                result = callback()\n                if asyncio.iscoroutine(result):\n                    await result\n            except Exception:\n                pass  # Ignore cleanup errors\n\n\nclass PageTriggerManager:\n    \"\"\"Manage page triggers with stoppable actions.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(\n        self,\n        navigation_manager: NavigationManager,\n        log: Logger,\n    ) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize PageTriggerManager.\n\n        Args:\n            navigation_manager: NavigationManager instance\n            log: Logger instance\n        \"\"\"\n        self.navigation_manager = navigation_manager\n        self.log = log\n        self._triggers: list[dict] = []\n        self._active_actions: list[ActionContext] = []\n        self._commander: Any = None\n\n    def initialize(self, commander: Any) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Initialize with commander reference.\n\n        Args:\n            commander: Browser commander instance\n        \"\"\"\n        self._commander = commander\n\n        # Subscribe to URL changes\n        self.navigation_manager.on(\"on_url_change\", self._on_url_change)\n\n    def _on_url_change(self, event: dict) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Handle URL change event.\"\"\"\n        new_url = event[\"new_url\"]\n\n        # Stop all active actions\n        for ctx in self._active_actions:\n            ctx._abort_signal.set()\n\n        # Check triggers for new URL\n        asyncio.create_task(self._check_triggers(new_url))\n\n    async def _check_triggers(self, url: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Check and run matching triggers for URL.\n\n        Args:\n            url: Current URL to check\n        \"\"\"\n        for trigger in self._triggers:\n            condition = trigger[\"condition\"]\n            if condition(url):\n                await self._run_trigger(trigger, url)\n\n    async def _run_trigger(self, trigger: dict, url: str) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Run a trigger's action.\n\n        Args:\n            trigger: Trigger configuration\n            url: Current URL\n        \"\"\"\n        name = trigger.get(\"name\", \"unnamed\")\n        action = trigger[\"action\"]\n\n        self.log.debug(lambda: f\"Running trigger '{name}' for URL: {url}\")\n\n        abort_signal = asyncio.Event()\n        ctx = ActionContext(\n            url=url,\n            trigger_name=name,\n            abort_signal=abort_signal,\n            commander=self._commander,\n        )\n        self._active_actions.append(ctx)\n\n        try:\n            await action(ctx)\n        except ActionStoppedError:\n            self.log.debug(lambda: f\"Trigger '{name}' was stopped\")\n        except Exception as e:\n            self.log.debug(lambda: f\"Trigger '{name}' error: {e}\")\n        finally:\n            await ctx.cleanup()\n            if ctx in self._active_actions:\n                self._active_actions.remove(ctx)\n\n    def page_trigger(self, config: dict) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Register a page trigger.\n\n        Args:\n            config: Trigger configuration with:\n                - condition: URL condition (string, regex, or function)\n                - action: Async function to run when condition matches\n                - name: Optional trigger name for debugging\n        \"\"\"\n        condition = config.get(\"condition\")\n        action = config.get(\"action\")\n        name = config.get(\"name\", \"unnamed\")\n\n        if not condition or not action:\n            msg = \"page_trigger requires 'condition' and 'action'\"\n            raise ValueError(msg)\n\n        # Convert condition to callable\n        if not callable(condition):\n            condition = make_url_condition(condition)\n\n        self._triggers.append(\n            {\n                \"condition\": condition,\n                \"action\": action,\n                \"name\": name,\n            }\n        )\n\n    async def destroy(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Clean up and stop all actions.\"\"\"\n        # Stop all active actions\n        for ctx in self._active_actions:\n            ctx._abort_signal.set()\n            await ctx.cleanup()\n\n        self._active_actions.clear()\n        self._triggers.clear()\n\n        # Unsubscribe from events\n        self.navigation_manager.off(\"on_url_change\", self._on_url_change)\n",

          "content": "\"\"\"Browser launcher for browser-commander.\"\"\"\n\nimport asyncio\nimport os\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass, field\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Any\n\nfrom browser_commander.core.constants import CHROME_ARGS\nfrom browser_commander.core.engine_detection import EngineType\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass LaunchOptions:\n    \"\"\"Browser launch configuration options.\"\"\"\n\n    engine: EngineType = \"playwright\"\n    user_data_dir: str | None = None\n    headless: bool = False\n    slow_mo: int | None = None  # Default: 150 for Playwright, 0 for Selenium\n    verbose: bool = False\n    args: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass LaunchResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of browser launch.\"\"\"\n\n    browser: Any\n    page: Any\n\n\nasync def launch_browser(options: LaunchOptions | None = None) -> LaunchResult:\n    \"\"\"Launch browser with default configuration.\n\n    Args:\n        options: Launch configuration options\n\n    Returns:\n        LaunchResult with browser and page objects\n\n    Raises:\n        ValueError: If engine is invalid\n    \"\"\"\n    if options is None:\n        options = LaunchOptions()\n\n    engine = options.engine\n    user_data_dir = options.user_data_dir\n    headless = options.headless\n    slow_mo = options.slow_mo\n    verbose = options.verbose\n    extra_args = options.args\n\n    # Set default user data directory\n    if user_data_dir is None:\n        user_data_dir = str(\n            Path.home() / \".browser-commander\" / f\"{engine}-data\"\n        )\n\n    # Set default slow_mo based on engine\n    if slow_mo is None:\n        slow_mo = 150 if engine == \"playwright\" else 0\n\n    # Combine default CHROME_ARGS with custom args\n    chrome_args = CHROME_ARGS + extra_args\n\n    if engine not in (\"playwright\", \"selenium\"):\n        msg = f\"Invalid engine: {engine}. Expected 'playwright' or 'selenium'\"\n        raise ValueError(msg)\n\n    # Set environment variables to suppress warnings\n    os.environ[\"GOOGLE_API_KEY\"] = \"no\"\n    os.environ[\"GOOGLE_DEFAULT_CLIENT_ID\"] = \"no\"\n    os.environ[\"GOOGLE_DEFAULT_CLIENT_SECRET\"] = \"no\"\n\n    if verbose:\n        print(f\"Launching browser with {engine} engine...\")\n\n    browser: Any\n    page: Any\n\n    if engine == \"playwright\":\n        from playwright.async_api import async_playwright\n\n        playwright = await async_playwright().start()\n        browser = await playwright.chromium.launch_persistent_context(\n            user_data_dir,\n            headless=headless,\n            slow_mo=slow_mo,\n            chromium_sandbox=True,\n            viewport=None,\n            args=chrome_args,\n            ignore_default_args=[\"--enable-automation\"],\n        )\n        pages = browser.pages\n        page = pages[0] if pages else await browser.new_page()\n\n    else:  # selenium\n        from selenium import webdriver\n        from selenium.webdriver.chrome.options import Options\n        from selenium.webdriver.chrome.service import Service\n\n        chrome_options = Options()\n        if headless:\n            chrome_options.add_argument(\"--headless=new\")\n        for arg in chrome_args:\n            chrome_options.add_argument(arg)\n        chrome_options.add_argument(f\"--user-data-dir={user_data_dir}\")\n\n        service = Service()\n        browser = webdriver.Chrome(service=service, options=chrome_options)\n        page = browser  # In Selenium, driver is both browser and page\n\n    if verbose:\n        print(f\"Browser launched with {engine} engine\")\n\n    # Unfocus address bar automatically after browser launch\n    try:\n        await asyncio.sleep(0.5)  # Wait for browser to initialize\n        if engine == \"playwright\":\n            await page.bring_to_front()\n        if verbose:\n            print(\"Address bar unfocused automatically\")\n    except Exception as e:\n        if verbose:\n            print(f\"Could not unfocus address bar: {e}\")\n\n    return LaunchResult(browser=browser, page=page)\n"

NavigationManager for backwards compatibility.\n     6→ */\n     7→\n     8→import { TIMING } from '../core/constants.js';\n     9→import { isNavigationError } from '../core/navigation-safety.js';\n    10→import { isActionStoppedError } from '../core/page-trigger-manager.js';\n    11→\n    12→/**\n    13→ * Default verification function for navigation operations.\n    14→ * Verifies that navigation completed by checking:\n    15→ * - URL matches expected pattern (if provided)\n    16→ * - Page is in a ready state\n    17→ *\n    18→ * @param {Object} options - Verification options\n    19→ * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n    20→ * @param {string} options.expectedUrl - Expected URL or URL pattern (optional)\n    21→ * @param {string} options.startUrl - URL before navigation\n    22→ * @returns {Promise<{verified: boolean, actualUrl: string, reason: string}>}\n    23→ */\n    24→export async function defaultNavigationVerification(options = {}) {\n    25→  const { page, expectedUrl, startUrl } = options;\n    26→\n    27→  try {\n    28→    const actualUrl = page.url();\n    29→\n    30→    // If expected URL is provided, verify it matches\n    31→    if (expectedUrl) {\n    32→      // Check for exact match or pattern match\n    33→      if (actualUrl === expectedUrl) {\n    34→        return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'exact URL match' };\n    35→      }\n    36→      // Check if expected URL is contained in actual URL (for patterns)\n    37→      if (\n    38→        actualUrl.includes(expectedUrl) ||\n    39→        actualUrl.startsWith(expectedUrl)\n    40→      ) {\n    41→        return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'URL pattern match' };\n    42→      }\n    43→      // Check if it's a regex pattern\n    44→      if (expectedUrl instanceof RegExp && expectedUrl.test(actualUrl)) {\n    45→        return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'URL regex match' };\n    46→      }\n    47→\n    48→ 54→\n    55→    // No expected URL - just verify URL changed from start\n    56→    if (startUrl && actualUrl !== startUrl) {\n    57→      return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'URL changed number}>}\n    86→ */\n    87→export async function verifyNavigation(options = {}) {\n    88→  const {\n    89→    page,\n    90→    expectedUrl,\n    91→    startUrl,\n    92→    verifyFn = 115→      return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n   116→    }\n   117→\n   118→    if (lastResult.navigationError) {\n   119→      log.debug(() => '⚠️  Navigation/stop detected during verification');\n   120→      return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n   121→    }\n   122→\n   123→    // Wait before next retry\n   124→    await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, retryInterval));\n   125→  }\n   126→\n   127→  log.debug(\n   128→    () =>\n   129→      `❌ Navigation verification failed after ${attempts} attempts: ${lastResult.reason}`\n   130→  );\n   131→  return { ...lastResult, 169→  let stableCount = 0;\n   170→  let lastUrl = page.url();\n   171→  const startTime = Date.now();\n   172→\n   173→  while (stableCount < stableChecks) {\n   174→    // Check timeout\n   175→ 180→    }\n   181→\n   182→    await wait({ ms: checkInterval, reason: 'checking URL stability' });\n   183→    const currentUrl = page.url();\n   184→\n   185→    if (currentUrl === lastUrl) {\n   186→      stableCount++;\n   187→      log.debug(\n   188→        () =>\n   189→          `🔍 [VERBOSE] URL stable for ${stableCount}/${stableChecks} checks: ${currentUrl}`\n   190→      );\n   191→    } else {\n   192→      stableCount = 0;\n   193→      lastUrl = currentUrl;\n   194→      log.debug(\n   195→        () =>\n   196→          `🔍 [VERBOSE] URL changed to: ${currentUrl}, resetting stability counter`\n   197→      );\n   198→    }\n   199→  }\n   200→\n   201→  log.debug(() => `✅ URL stabilized (${reason})`);\n   202→  return true;\n   203→}\n   204→\n   205→/**\n   206→ * @param {number} options.timeout - Navigation timeout in ms (default: 240000)\n   220→ * @param {boolean} options.verify - Whether to verify the navigation (default: true)\n   221→ * @param {Function} @returns {Promise<{navigated: boolean, verified: boolean, actualUrl?: string, reason?: string}>}\n   224→ */\n   225→export async function goto(options = {}) {\n   226→  const {\n   227→    page,\n });\n   258→\n   259→      // Verify navigation if requested\n   260→      if (verify && navigated) {\n   261→        const verificationResult = await verifyNavigation({\n   262→          page,\n   263→          expectedUrl: url,\n   264→          startUrl,\n   265→          verifyFn,\n   266→          timeout: verificationTimeout,\n   267→          log,\n   268→        });\n   269→\n   270→        return {\n   271→          navigated: true,\n   272→          verified: verificationResult.verified,\n   273→          actualUrl: verificationResult.actualUrl,\n   274→          reason: (to avoid interrupting natural redirects)\n   296→    if (waitForStableUrlBefore && stabilizeFn) {\n   297→      await stabilizeFn({\n   298→        stableChecks,\n   299→        checkInterval,\n   300→        reason: 'before navigation',\n   301→      });\n   302→    }\n   303→\n   304→    // Navigate to the URL\n   305→    await page.goto(url, { waitUntil, timeout });\n   306→\n   307→    // stableChecks,\n   311→        checkInterval,\n   312→        reason: 'after navigation',\n   313→      });\n   314→    }\n   315→\n   316→    // Verify navigation if requested\n   317→    if (verify) {\n   318→      const verificationResult = await verifyNavigation({\n   319→        page,\n   320→        expectedUrl: url,\n   321→        startUrl,\n   322→        verifyFn,\n   323→        timeout: actualUrl: verificationResult.actualUrl,\n   331→        reason: verificationResult.reason,\n   332→      };\n   333→    }\n   334→\n   335→    return { navigated: true, verified: true, actualUrl: page.url() };\n   336→  } catch (error) {\n   337→    if (isNavigationError(error) || isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n   338→      console.log(\n   339→        '⚠️  Navigation was interrupted/stopped, recovering gracefully'\n   340→      );\n   341→      return {\n   342→        navigated: false,\n   343→        verified: false,\n   344→        reason: 'navigation interrupted/stopped',\n   345→ {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n   355→ * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (optional)\n   356→ * @param {number} options.timeout - Timeout in ms\n   357→ * @returns {Promise<boolean>} - True if navigation completed, false on error\n   358→ */\n   359→export async function waitForNavigation(options = {}) {\n   360→  const { page, navigationManager, timeout } = options;\n   361→\n   362→  // If NavigationManager is available, use it\n   363→  if (navigationManager) {\n   364→    return navigationManager.waitForNavigation({ timeout });\n   365→  }\n   366→\n   367→  // Legacy approach\n   368→  try {\n   369→    await page.waitForNavigation(timeout ? { timeout } : undefined);\n   370→    return true;\n   371→  } catch (error) {\n   372→    if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n   373→      console.log(\n   374→        '⚠️  waitForNavigation was interrupted, continuing gracefully'\n   375→      );\n   376→      return false;\n   377→    }\n   378→    throw error;\n   379→  }\n   380→}\n   381→\n   382→/**\n   383→ * Wait for page to be fully ready (DOM loaded + network idle + no redirects)\n   384→ * This is the recommended method for ensuring page is ready for manipulation.\n   385→ *\n   386→ * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n   387→ * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n   388→ * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (required for full functionality)\n   389→ * @param {Object} options.networkTracker - NetworkTracker instance (optional)\n   390→ * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n   391→ * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n   392→ * @param {number} options.timeout - Maximum time to wait (default: 30000ms)\n   393→ * @param {string} options.reason - Reason for waiting (for logging)\n   394→ * @returns {Promise<boolean>} - True if ready, false if timeout\n   395→ */\n   396→export async function waitForPageReady(options = {}) {\n   397→  const {\n   398→    page,\n   399→    navigationManager,\n   400→    networkTracker,\n   401→    log,\n   402→    wait,\n   403→    timeout = 30000,\n   404→    reason = 'page ready',\n   405→  } = options;\n   406→\n   407→  // If NavigationManager is available, delegate to it\n   408→  if (navigationManager) {\n   409→    return navigationManager.waitForPageReady({ timeout, reason });\n   410→  }\n   411→\n   412→  // Fallback: use network tracker directly if available\n   413→  if (networkTracker) {\n   414→    log.debug(() => `⏳ Waiting for page ready (${reason})...`);\n   415→    const startTime = Date.now();\n   416→\n   417→    // Wait for network idle\n   418→    const networkIdle = await networkTracker.waitForNetworkIdle({\n   419→      timeout,\n   420→    });\n   421→\n   422→    const elapsed = Date.now() - startTime;\n   423→    if (networkIdle) {\n   424→      log.debug(() => `✅ Page ready after ${elapsed}ms (${reason})`);\n   425→    } else {\n   426→      log.debug(() => `⚠️  Page ready timeout after ${elapsed}ms (${reason})`);\n   427→    }\n   428→\n   429→    return networkIdle;\n   430→  }\n   431→\n   432→  // Minimal fallback: just wait a bit for DOM to settle\n   433→  log.debug(() => `⏳ Waiting for page ready - minimal mode (${reason})...`);\n   434→  await wait({ ms: 1000, reason: 'page settle time' });\n   435→  return true;\n   436→}\n   437→\n   438→/**\n   439→ * Wait for any ongoing navigation and network requests to complete.\n   440→ * Use this after actions that might trigger navigation (like clicks).\n   441→ *\n   442→ * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n   443→ * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n   444→ * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance\n   445→ * @param {Object} options.networkTracker - NetworkTracker instance\n   446→ * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n   447→ * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n   448→ * @param {number} options.navigationCheckDelay - Time to wait for potential navigation to start (default: 500ms)\n   449→ * @param {number} options.timeout - Maximum time to wait (default: 30000ms)\n   450→ * @param {string} options.reason - Reason for waiting (for logging)\n   451→ * @returns {Promise<{navigated: boolean, ready: boolean}>}\n   452→ */\n   453→export async function waitAfterAction(options = {}) {\n   454→  const {\n   455→    page,\n   456→    navigationManager,\n   457→    networkTracker,\n   458→    log,\n   459→    wait,\n   460→    navigationCheckDelay = 500,\n   461→    timeout = 30000,\n   462→    reason = 'after action',\n   463→  } = options;\n   464→\n   465→  const startUrl = page.url();\n   466→  const startTime = Date.now();\n   467→\n   468→  log.debug(() => `⏳ Waiting after action (${reason})...`);\n   469→\n   470→  // Wait briefly for potential navigation to start\n   471→  await wait({ ms: navigationCheckDelay, reason: 'checking for navigation' });\n   472→\n   473→  // Check if navigation is in progress or URL changed\n   474→  const currentUrl = page.url();\n   475→  const urlChanged = currentUrl !== startUrl;\n   476→\n   477→  if (navigationManager && navigationManager.isNavigating()) {\n   478→    log.debug(() => '🔄 Navigation in progress, waiting for completion...');\n   479→    await navigationManager.waitForNavigation({\n   480→      timeout: timeout - (Date.now() - startTime),\n   481→    });\n   482→    return { navigated: true, ready: true };\n   483→  }\n   484→\n   485→  if (urlChanged) {\n   486→    log.debug(() => `🔄 URL changed: ${startUrl} → ${currentUrl}`);\n   487→\n   488→    // Wait for page to be fully ready\n   489→    await waitForPageReady({\n   490→      page,\n   491→      navigationManager,\n   492→      networkTracker,\n   493→      log,\n   494→      wait,\n   495→      timeout: timeout - (Date.now() - for network idle\n   503→  // Use shorter idle time since this is just for XHR completion, not full page load\n   504→  if (networkTracker) {\n   505→    const idle = await networkTracker.waitForNetworkIdle({\n   506→      timeout: Math.max(0, timeout - (Date.now() - startTime)),\n   507→      idleTime: 2000, // Shorter idle time for non-navigation actions\n   508→    });\n   509→    return { navigated: false, ready: idle };\n   510→  }\n   511→\n   512→  return { navigated: false, ready: true };\n   513→}\n   514→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a file,       "filePath": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1768327375795/js/src/browser/navigation.js",
      "content": "/**\n * Navigation-related browser operations\n *\n * This module provides navigation functions that can work with or without\n * the NavigationManager for backwards compatibility.\n */\n\nimport { TIMING } from '../core/constants.js';\nimport { isNavigationError } from '../core/navigation-safety.js';\nimport { isActionStoppedError } from '../core/page-trigger-manager.js';\n\n/**\n * Default verification function for navigation operations.\n * Verifies that navigation completed by checking:\n * - URL matches expected pattern (if provided)\n * - Page is in a ready state\n *\n * @param {Object} options - Verification options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.expectedUrl - Expected URL or URL pattern (optional)\n * @param {string} options.startUrl - URL before navigation\n * @returns {Promise<{verified: boolean, actualUrl: string, reason: string}>}\n */\nexport async function defaultNavigationVerification(options = {}) {\n  const { page, expectedUrl, startUrl } = options;\n\n  try {\n    const actualUrl = page.url();\n\n    // If expected URL is provided, verify it matches\n    if (expectedUrl) {\n      // Check for exact match or pattern match\n      if (actualUrl === expectedUrl) {\n        return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'exact URL match' };\n      }\n      // Check if expected URL is contained in actual URL (for patterns)\n      if (\n        actualUrl.includes(expectedUrl) ||\n        actualUrl.startsWith(expectedUrl)\n      ) {\n        return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'URL pattern match' };\n      }\n      // Check if it's a regex pattern\n      if (expectedUrl instanceof RegExp && expectedUrl.test(actualUrl)) {\n        return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'URL regex match' };\n      }\n\n      return {\n        verified: false,\n        actualUrl,\n        reason: `URL mismatch: expected \"${expectedUrl}\", got \"${actualUrl}\"`,\n      };\n    }\n\n    // No expected URL - just verify URL changed from start\n    if (startUrl && actualUrl !== startUrl) {\n      return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'URL changed from start' };\n    }\n\n    // If no start URL and no expected URL, assume success\n    return { verified: true, actualUrl, reason: 'navigation completed' };\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error) || isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n      return {\n        verified: false,\n        actualUrl: '',\n        reason: 'error during verification',\n        navigationError: true,\n      };\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Verify navigation operation with retry logic\n * @param {Object} options - Verification options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.expectedUrl - Expected URL (optional)\n * @param {string} options.startUrl - URL before navigation\n * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional)\n * @param {number} options.timeout - Verification timeout in ms (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT)\n * @param {number} options.retryInterval - Interval between retries (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_RETRY_INTERVAL)\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @returns {Promise<{verified: boolean, actualUrl: string, reason: string, attempts: number}>}\n */\nexport async function verifyNavigation(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    expectedUrl,\n    startUrl,\n    verifyFn = defaultNavigationVerification,\n    timeout = TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT,\n    retryInterval = TIMING.VERIFICATION_RETRY_INTERVAL,\n    log = { debug: () => {} },\n  } = options;\n\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n  let attempts = 0;\n  let lastResult = { verified: false, actualUrl: '', reason: '' };\n\n  while (Date.now() - startTime < timeout) {\n    attempts++;\n    lastResult = await verifyFn({\n      page,\n      expectedUrl,\n      startUrl,\n    });\n\n    if (lastResult.verified) {\n      log.debug(\n        () =>\n          `✅ Navigation verification succeeded after ${attempts} attempt(s): ${lastResult.reason}`\n      );\n      return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n    }\n\n    if (lastResult.navigationError) {\n      log.debug(() => '⚠️  Navigation/stop detected during verification');\n      return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n    }\n\n    // Wait before next retry\n    await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, retryInterval));\n  }\n\n  log.debug(\n    () =>\n      `❌ Navigation verification failed after ${attempts} attempts: ${lastResult.reason}`\n  );\n  return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n}\n\n/**\n * Wait for URL to stabilize (no redirects happening)\n * This is a legacy polling-based approach for backwards compatibility.\n * When navigationManager is available, use waitForPageReady instead.\n *\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (optional)\n * @param {number} options.stableChecks - Number of consecutive stable checks required (default: 3)\n * @param {number} options.checkInterval - Interval between stability checks in ms (default: 1000)\n * @param {number} options.timeout - Maximum time to wait for stabilization in ms (default: 30000)\n * @param {string} options.reason - Reason for stabilization (for logging)\n * @returns {Promise<boolean>} - True if stabilized, false if timeout\n */\nexport async function waitForUrlStabilization(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    log,\n    wait,\n    navigationManager,\n    stableChecks = 3,\n    checkInterval = 1000,\n    timeout = 30000,\n    reason = 'URL stabilization',\n  } = options;\n\n  // If NavigationManager is available, delegate to it\n  if (navigationManager) {\n    return navigationManager.waitForPageReady({ timeout, reason });\n  }\n\n  // Legacy polling-based approach\n  log.debug(() => `⏳ Waiting for URL to stabilize (${reason})...`);\n  let stableCount = 0;\n  let lastUrl = page.url();\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n\n  while (stableCount < stableChecks) {\n    // Check timeout\n    if (Date.now() - startTime > timeout) {\n      log.debug(\n        () => `⚠️  URL stabilization timeout after ${timeout}ms (${reason})`\n      );\n      return false;\n    }\n\n    await wait({ ms: checkInterval, reason: 'checking URL stability' });\n    const currentUrl = page.url();\n\n    if (currentUrl === lastUrl) {\n      stableCount++;\n      log.debug(\n        () =>\n          `🔍 [VERBOSE] URL stable for ${stableCount}/${stableChecks} checks: ${currentUrl}`\n      );\n    } else {\n      stableCount = 0;\n      lastUrl = currentUrl;\n      log.debug(\n        () =>\n          `🔍 [VERBOSE] URL changed to: ${currentUrl}, resetting stability counter`\n      );\n    }\n  }\n\n  log.debug(() => `✅ URL stabilized (${reason})`);\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Navigate to URL with full wait for page ready\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {Function} options.waitForUrlStabilization - URL stabilization function (legacy)\n * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (preferred)\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance (optional)\n * @param {string} options.url - URL to navigate to\n * @param {string} options.waitUntil - Wait until condition (default: 'domcontentloaded')\n * @param {boolean} options.waitForStableUrlBefore - Wait for URL to stabilize BEFORE navigation (default: true)\n * @param {boolean} options.waitForStableUrlAfter - Wait for URL to stabilize AFTER navigation (default: true)\n * @param {boolean} options.waitForNetworkIdle - Wait for all network requests to complete (default: true)\n * @param {number} options.stableChecks - Number of consecutive stable checks required (default: 3)\n * @param {number} options.checkInterval - Interval between stability checks in ms (default: 1000)\n * @param {number} options.timeout - Navigation timeout in ms (default: 240000)\n * @param {boolean} options.verify - Whether to verify the navigation (default: true)\n * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional)\n * @param {number} options.verificationTimeout - Verification timeout in ms (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT)\n * @returns {Promise<{navigated: boolean, verified: boolean, actualUrl?: string, reason?: string}>}\n */\nexport async function goto(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    waitForUrlStabilization: stabilizeFn,\n    navigationManager,\n    log = { debug: () => {} },\n    url,\n    waitUntil = 'domcontentloaded',\n    waitForStableUrlBefore = true,\n    waitForStableUrlAfter = true,\n    waitForNetworkIdle = true,\n    stableChecks = 3,\n    checkInterval = 1000,\n    timeout = 240000,\n    verify = true,\n    verifyFn,\n    verificationTimeout = TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT,\n  } = options;\n\n  if (!url) {\n    throw new Error('url is required in options');\n  }\n\n  const startUrl = page.url();\n\n  // If NavigationManager is available, use it for full navigation handling\n  if (navigationManager) {\n    try {\n      const navigated = await navigationManager.navigate({\n        url,\n        waitUntil,\n        timeout,\n      });\n\n      // Verify navigation if requested\n      if (verify && navigated) {\n        const verificationResult = await verifyNavigation({\n          page,\n          expectedUrl: url,\n          startUrl,\n          verifyFn,\n          timeout: verificationTimeout,\n          log,\n        });\n\n        return {\n          navigated: true,\n          verified: verificationResult.verified,\n          actualUrl: verificationResult.actualUrl,\n          reason: verificationResult.reason,\n        };\n      }\n\n      return { navigated, verified: navigated, actualUrl: page.url() };\n    } catch (error) {\n      if (isNavigationError(error) || isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n        // Navigation was stopped by page trigger or navigation error\n        // This is not a failure - it means another action took over\n        return {\n          navigated: false,\n          verified: false,\n          reason: 'navigation stopped/interrupted',\n        };\n      }\n      throw error;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Legacy approach without NavigationManager\n  try {\n    // Wait for URL to stabilize BEFORE navigation (to avoid interrupting natural redirects)\n    if (waitForStableUrlBefore && stabilizeFn) {\n      await stabilizeFn({\n        stableChecks,\n        checkInterval,\n        reason: 'before navigation',\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Navigate to the URL\n    await page.goto(url, { waitUntil, timeout });\n\n    // Wait for URL to stabilize AFTER navigation (to ensure all redirects are complete)\n    if (waitForStableUrlAfter && stabilizeFn) {\n      await stabilizeFn({\n        stableChecks,\n        checkInterval,\n        reason: 'after navigation',\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Verify navigation if requested\n    if (verify) {\n      const verificationResult = await verifyNavigation({\n        page,\n        expectedUrl: url,\n        startUrl,\n        verifyFn,\n        timeout: verificationTimeout,\n        log,\n      });\n\n      return {\n        navigated: true,\n        verified: verificationResult.verified,\n        actualUrl: verificationResult.actualUrl,\n        reason: verificationResult.reason,\n      };\n    }\n\n    return { navigated: true, verified: true, actualUrl: page.url() };\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error) || isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n      console.log(\n        '⚠️  Navigation was interrupted/stopped, recovering gracefully'\n      );\n      return {\n        navigated: false,\n        verified: false,\n        reason: 'navigation interrupted/stopped',\n      };\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Wait for navigation\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (optional)\n * @param {number} options.timeout - Timeout in ms\n * @returns {Promise<boolean>} - True if navigation completed, false on error\n */\nexport async function waitForNavigation(options = {}) {\n  const { page, navigationManager, timeout } = options;\n\n  // If NavigationManager is available, use it\n  if (navigationManager) {\n    return navigationManager.waitForNavigation({ timeout });\n  }\n\n  // Legacy approach\n  try {\n    await page.waitForNavigation(timeout ? { timeout } : undefined);\n    return true;\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n      console.log(\n        '⚠️  waitForNavigation was interrupted, continuing gracefully'\n      );\n      return false;\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Wait for page to be fully ready (DOM loaded + network idle + no redirects)\n * This is the recommended method for ensuring page is ready for manipulation.\n *\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (required for full functionality)\n * @param {Object} options.networkTracker - NetworkTracker instance (optional)\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n * @param {number} options.timeout - Maximum time to wait (default: 30000ms)\n * @param {string} options.reason - Reason for waiting (for logging)\n * @returns {Promise<boolean>} - True if ready, false if timeout\n */\nexport async function waitForPageReady(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    navigationManager,\n    networkTracker,\n    log,\n    wait,\n    timeout = 30000,\n    reason = 'page ready',\n  } = options;\n\n  // If NavigationManager is available, delegate to it\n  if (navigationManager) {\n    return navigationManager.waitForPageReady({ timeout, reason });\n  }\n\n  // Fallback: use network tracker directly if available\n  if (networkTracker) {\n    log.debug(() => `⏳ Waiting for page ready (${reason})...`);\n    const startTime = Date.now();\n\n    // Wait for network idle\n    const networkIdle = await networkTracker.waitForNetworkIdle({\n      timeout,\n    });\n\n    const elapsed = Date.now() - startTime;\n    if (networkIdle) {\n      log.debug(() => `✅ Page ready after ${elapsed}ms (${reason})`);\n    } else {\n      log.debug(() => `⚠️  Page ready timeout after ${elapsed}ms (${reason})`);\n    }\n\n    return networkIdle;\n  }\n\n  // Minimal fallback: just wait a bit for DOM to settle\n  log.debug(() => `⏳ Waiting for page ready - minimal mode (${reason})...`);\n  await wait({ ms: 1000, reason: 'page settle time' });\n  return true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Wait for any ongoing navigation and network requests to complete.\n * Use this after actions that might trigger navigation (like clicks).\n *\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance\n * @param {Object} options.networkTracker - NetworkTracker instance\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n * @param {number} options.navigationCheckDelay - Time to wait for potential navigation to start (default: 500ms)\n * @param {number} options.timeout - Maximum time to wait (default: 30000ms)\n * @param {string} options.reason - Reason for waiting (for logging)\n * @returns {Promise<{navigated: boolean, ready: boolean}>}\n */\nexport async function waitAfterAction(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    navigationManager,\n    networkTracker,\n    log,\n    wait,\n    navigationCheckDelay = 500,\n    timeout = 30000,\n    reason = 'after action',\n  } = options;\n\n  const startUrl = page.url();\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n\n  log.debug(() => `⏳ Waiting after action (${reason})...`);\n\n  // Wait briefly for potential navigation to start\n  await wait({ ms: navigationCheckDelay, reason: 'checking for navigation' });\n\n  // Check if navigation is in progress or URL changed\n  const currentUrl = page.url();\n  const urlChanged = currentUrl !== startUrl;\n\n  if (navigationManager && navigationManager.isNavigating()) {\n    log.debug(() => '🔄 Navigation in progress, waiting for completion...');\n    await navigationManager.waitForNavigation({\n      timeout: timeout - (Date.now() - startTime),\n    });\n    return { navigated: true, ready: true };\n  }\n\n  if (urlChanged) {\n    log.debug(() => `🔄 URL changed: ${startUrl} → ${currentUrl}`);\n\n    // Wait for page to be fully ready\n    await waitForPageReady({\n      page,\n      navigationManager,\n      networkTracker,\n      log,\n      wait,\n      timeout: timeout - (Date.now() - startTime),\n      reason: 'after URL change',\n    });\n\n    return { navigated: true, ready: true };\n  }\n\n  // No navigation detected, just wait for network idle\n  // Use shorter idle time since this is just for XHR completion, not full page load\n  if (networkTracker) {\n    const idle = await networkTracker.waitForNetworkIdle({\n      timeout: Math.max(0, timeout - (Date.now() - startTime)),\n      idleTime: 2000, // Shorter idle time for non-navigation actions\n    });\n    return { navigated: false, ready: idle };\n  }\n\n  return { navigated: false, ready: true };\n}\n",

navigated = await navigation_manager.navigate(url, wait_until, timeout)\n\n            # Verify navigation if requested\n            if verify and navigated:\n                verification_result = timeout=verification_timeout,\n                    log=log,\n                )\n\n                return GotoResult(\n                    navigated=True,\n                    verified=verification_result.verified,\n                    actual_url=verification_result.actual_url,\n                    reason=verification_result.reason,\n                )\n\n            # Get current URL for result\n            if hasattr(page, \"url\"):\n                if callable(page.url):\n                    current_url = page.url()\n                else:\n GotoResult(\n                navigated=navigated,\n                verified=navigated,\n                actual_url=current_url,\n            )\n\n        except Exception as error:\n            if is_navigation_error(error) or is_action_stopped_error(error):\n                # Navigation was stopped by page trigger or navigation error\n                return GotoResult(\n                    navigated=False,\n                    verified=False,\n                    reason=\"navigation stopped/interrupted\",\n                )\n            raise\n\n    # Legacy approach without NavigationManager\n    try:\n        # Wait for URL to stabilize BEFORE navigation\n        if wait_for_stable_url_before and wait_for_url_stabilization_fn:\n            await wait_for_url_stabilization_fn(\n                stable_checks=stable_checks,\n                check_interval=check_interval,\n                reason=\"before navigation\",\n            )\n\n        # Navigate to the URL\n        if hasattr(page, \"goto\"):\n            # Playwright\n            await page.goto(url, wait_until=wait_until, timeout=timeout)\n        elif hasattr(page, \"get\"):\n            # Selenium\n            page.get(url)\n        else:\n            raise ValueError(\"Unknown page type - cannot navigate\")\n\n        # Wait for URL to stabilize AFTER navigation\n        if wait_for_stable_url_after and wait_for_url_stabilization_fn:\n            await wait_for_url_stabilization_fn(\n                stable_checks=stable_checks,\n                check_interval=check_interval,\n                reason=\"after navigation\",\n            )\n\n        # Verify navigation if requested\n        if verify:\n            verification_result = await verify_navigation(\n                page=page,\n                expected_url=url,\n                start_url=start_url,\n                verify_fn=verify_fn,\n                timeout=verification_timeout,\n                log=log,\n            )\n\n            return GotoResult(\n                navigated=True,\n                verified=verification_result.verified,\n                actual_url=verification_result.actual_url,\n                reason=verification_result.reason,\n            )\n\n        # Get current URL for result\n        if hasattr(page, \"url\"):\n current_url = page.current_url\n        else:\n            current_url = \"\"\n\n        return GotoResult(\n            navigated=True,\n            verified=True,\n            actual_url=current_url,\n        )\n\n    except Exception as error:\n        if is_navigation_error(error) or is_action_stopped_error(error):\n            print(\"Navigation was interrupted/stopped,        raise\n\n\nasync def wait_for_navigation(\n    page: Any,\n    navigation_manager: Any | None = None,\n    timeout: int | None = None,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Wait for navigation to          else:\n                await page.wait_for_load_state(\"load\")\n        # Selenium doesn't have wait_for_navigation, so just return True\n        return True\n    except Exception as wait_for_page_ready(\n    page: Any,\n    navigation_manager: Any | None = None,\n    network_tracker: Any | None = None,\n    log: Logger | None = None,\n    wait_fn: Callable[[int, str], Any] | None waiting (for logging)\n\n    Returns:\n        True if ready, False if timeout\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a no-op logger if none provided\n    class NoOpLogger:\n        def debug(self, _: Callable[[], page ready ({reason})...\")\n        start_time = time.time()\n\n        # Wait for network idle\n        network_idle = await network_tracker.wait_for_network_idle(timeout, 2000)\n\n        elapsed = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n        if network_idle:\n            log.debug(lambda: f\"Page ready after {elapsed}ms ({reason})\")\n        else:\n            log.debug(lambda: f\"Page ready timeout after {elapsed}ms ({reason})\")\n\n        return network_idle\n\n    # Minimal fallback: just wait a bit for DOM to settle\n    log.debug(lambda: f\"Waiting for page ready - minimal mode ({reason})...\")\n    if wait_fn:\n        await wait_fn(1000, \"page settle time\")\n    else:\n        await asyncio.sleep(1)\n    return True\n\n\nasync def wait_after_action(\n    page:
