function for navigation operations.\n\n    Verifies that navigation completed by checking:\n    - URL matches expected pattern (if provided)\n    - Page is in a ready state\n\n    Args:\n        page: Browser page object\n        expected_url: Expected URL or URL pattern (optional)\n        start_url: URL before navigation\n\n    Returns:\n        NavigationVerificationResult with verification status\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Get current URL\n        if hasattr(page, \"url\"):\n            if callable(page.url):\n                actual_url = page.url()\n            else:\n                actual_url = page.url\n        elif hasattr(page, \"current_url\"):\n            actual_url = page.current_url\n        else:\n            actual_url = \"\"\n\n        # If expected URL is provided, verify it matches\n        if expected_url:\n            # Check for exact match\n            if actual_url == expected_url:\n                return NavigationVerificationResult(\n                    verified=True,\n                    actual_url=actual_url,\n                    reason=\"exact URL match\",\n                )\n\n            # Check if expected URL is contained in actual URL (for patterns)\n            if isinstance(expected_url, str):\n                if expected_url in actual_url or actual_url.startswith(expected_url):\n                    return NavigationVerificationResult(\n                        verified=True,\n                        actual_url=actual_url,\n                        reason=\"URL pattern match\",\n                    )\n\n           actual_url=verification_result.actual_url,\n                reason=verification_result.reason,\n            )\n\n        # Get current URL for result\n        if hasattr(page, \"url\"):\n current_url = page.current_url\n        else:\n            current_url = \"\"\n\n        return GotoResult(\n            navigated=True,\n            verified=True,\n            actual_url=current_url,\n        )\n\n    except Exception as error:\n        if is_navigation_error(error) or is_action_stopped_error(error):\n            print(\"Navigation was interrupted/stopped, recovering gracefully\")\n            return GotoResult(\n                navigated=False,\n                verified=False,\n                reason=\"navigation interrupted/stopped\",\n            )\n        raise\n\n\nasync def wait_for_navigation(\n    page: Any,\n    navigation_manager: Any | None = None,\n    timeout: int | None = None,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Wait for navigation to complete.\n\n    Args:\n        page: Browser page object\n        navigation_manager: NavigationManager instance (optional)\n        timeout: Timeout in ms\n\n    Returns:\n        True if navigation completed, False on error\n    \"\"\"\n    # If NavigationManager is available, use it\n    if navigation_manager:\n        return await navigation_manager.wait_for_navigation(timeout)\n\n    # Legacy approach\n    try:\n        if hasattr(page, \"wait_for_load_state\"):\n            # Playwright\n            if timeout:\n                await page.wait_for_load_state(\"load\", timeout=timeout)\n            else:\n                await page.wait_for_load_state(\"load\")\n        # Selenium doesn't have wait_for_navigation, so just return True\n        return True\n    except Exception as error:\n        if is_navigation_error(error):\n            print(\"wait_for_navigation was interrupted, continuing gracefully\")\n            return False\n        raise\n\n\nasync def wait_for_page_ready(\n    page: Any,\n    navigation_manager: Any | None = None,\n    network_tracker: Any | None = None,\n    log: Logger | None = None,\n    wait_fn: Callable[[int, str], Any] | None = None,\n    timeout: int = 30000,\n    reason: str = \"page ready\",\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Wait for page to be fully ready (DOM loaded + network idle + no redirects).\n\n    This is the recommended method for ensuring page is ready for manipulation.\n\n    Args:\n        page: Browser page object\n        navigation_manager: NavigationManager instance (required for full functionality)\n        network_tracker: NetworkTracker instance (optional)\n        log: Logger instance\n        wait_fn: Wait function\n        timeout: Maximum time to wait (default: 30000ms)\n        reason: Reason for waiting (for logging)\n\n    Returns:\n        True if ready, False if timeout\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a no-op logger if none provided\n    class NoOpLogger:\n        def debug(self, _: Callable[[], str]) -> None:\n            pass\n\n    if log is None:\n        log = NoOpLogger()  # type: ignore\n\n    # If NavigationManager is available, delegate to it\n    if navigation_manager:\n        return await navigation_manager.wait_for_page_ready(timeout, reason)\n\n    # Fallback: use network tracker directly if available\n    if network_tracker:\n        log.debug(lambda: f\"Waiting for page ready ({reason})...\")\n        start_time = time.time()\n\n        # Wait for network idle\n        network_idle = await network_tracker.wait_for_network_idle(timeout, 2000)\n\n        elapsed = int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n        if network_idle:\n            log.debug(lambda: f\"Page ready after {elapsed}ms ({reason})\")\n        else:\n            log.debug(lambda: f\"Page ready timeout after {elapsed}ms ({reason})\")\n\n        return network_idle\n\n    # Minimal fallback: just wait a bit for DOM to settle\n    log.debug(lambda: f\"Waiting for page ready - minimal mode ({reason})...\")\n    if wait_fn:\n        await wait_fn(1000, \"page settle time\")\n    else:\n        await asyncio.sleep(1)\n    return True\n\n\nasync def wait_after_action(\n    page: Any,\n    navigation_manager: Any | None = None,\n    network_tracker: Any | None = None,\n    log: Logger | None = None,\n    wait_fn: Callable[[int, str], Any] | None = None,\n    navigation_check_delay: int = 500,\n    timeout: int = 30000,\n    reason: str = \"after action\",\n) -> WaitAfterActionResult:\n    \"\"\"Wait for any ongoing navigation and network requests to complete.\n\n    Use this after actions that might trigger navigation (like clicks).\n\n    Args:\n        page: Browser page object\n        navigation_manager: NavigationManager instance\n        network_tracker: NetworkTracker instance\n        log: Logger instance\n        wait_fn: Wait function\n        navigation_check_delay: Time to wait for potential navigation to start\n        timeout: Maximum time to wait (default: 30000ms)\n        reason: Reason for waiting (for logging)\n\n    Returns:\n        WaitAfterActionResult with navigated and ready flags\n    \"\"\"\n    # Create a no-op logger if none provided\n    class NoOpLogger:\n        def debug(self, _: Callable[[], str]) -> None:\n            pass\n\n    if log is None:\n        log = NoOpLogger()  # type: ignore\n\n    # Get start URL\n    if hasattr(page, \"url\"):\n        if callable(page.url):\n            start_url = page.url()\n        else:\n            start_url = page.url\n    elif hasattr(page, \"current_url\"):\n        start_url = page.current_url\n    else:\n        start_url = \"\"\n\n    start_time = time.time()\n\n    log.debug(lambda: f\"Waiting after action ({reason})...\")\n\n    # Wait briefly for potential navigation to start\n    if wait_fn:\n        await wait_fn(navigation_check_delay, \"checking for navigation\")\n    else:\n        await asyncio.sleep(navigation_check_delay / 1000)\n\n    # Get current URL\n    if hasattr(page, \"url\"):\n        if callable(page.url):\n            current_url = page.url()\n        else:\n            current_url = page.url\n    elif hasattr(page, \"current_url\"):\n        current_url = page.current_url\n    else:\n        current_url = \"\"\n\n    url_changed = current_url != start_url\n\n    if navigation_manager and navigation_manager.is_navigating():\n        log.debug(lambda: \"Navigation in progress, waiting for completion...\")\n        remaining_timeout = timeout - int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n        await navigation_manager.wait_for_navigation(remaining_timeout)\n        return WaitAfterActionResult(navigated=True, ready=True)\n\n    if url_changed:\n        log.debug(lambda: f\"URL changed: {start_url} -> {current_url}\")\n\n        # Wait for page to be fully ready\n        remaining_timeout = timeout - int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000)\n        await wait_for_page_ready(\n            page=page,\n            navigation_manager=navigation_manager,\n            network_tracker=network_tracker,\n            log=log,\n            wait_fn=wait_fn,\n            timeout=remaining_timeout,\n            reason=\"after URL change\",\n        )\n\n        return WaitAfterActionResult(navigated=True, ready=True)\n\n    # No navigation detected, just wait for network idle\n    if network_tracker:\n        remaining_timeout = max(0, timeout - int((time.time() - start_time) * 1000))\n        idle = await network_tracker.wait_for_network_idle(\n            timeout=remaining_timeout,\n            idle_time=2000,  # Shorter idle time for non-navigation actions\n        )\n        return WaitAfterActionResult(navigated=False, ready=idle)\n\n    return WaitAfterActionResult(navigated=False, ready=True)\n",

    "originalFile": null

        "content": "     1→import { isNavigationError } from '../core/navigation-safety.js';\n     2→import { createPlaywrightLocator } from './locators.js';\n     3→\n     4→/**\n     5→ * Query single element\n     6→ * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n     7→ * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n     8→ * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n     9→ * @param {string} options.selector - CSS selector\n    10→ * @returns {Promise<Object|null>} - Element handle or null\n    11→ */\n    12→export async function querySelector(options = {}) {\n    13→  const { page, engine, selector } = options;\n    14→\n    15→  if (!selector) {\n    16→    throw new Error('selector is required in options');\n    17→  }\n    18→\n    19→  try {\n    20→    if (engine === 'playwright') {\n    21→      const locator = createPlaywrightLocator({ page, selector }).first();\n    22→      const count = await locator.count();\n    23→      return count > 0 ? locator : null;\n    24→    } else {\n    25→      return await page.$(selector);\n    26→    }\n    27→  } catch (error) {\n    28→    if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n    29→      console.log(\n    30→        '⚠️  Navigation detected during querySelector, returning null'\n    31→      );\n    32→      return null;\n    33→    }\n    34→    throw error;\n    35→  }\n    36→}\n    37→\n    38→/**\n    39→ * Query all elements\n    40→ * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n    41→ * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n    42→ * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n    43→ * @param {string} options.selector - CSS selector\n    44→ * @returns {Promise<Array>} - Array of element handles\n    45→ */\n    46→export async function querySelectorAll(options = {}) {\n    47→  const { page, engine, selector } = options;\n    48→\n    49→  if (!selector) {\n    50→    throw new Error('selector is required in options');\n    51→  }\n    52→\n    53→  try {\n    54→    if (engine === 'playwright') {\n    55→      const locator = createPlaywrightLocator({ page, selector });\n    56→      const count = await locator.count();\n    57→      const elements = [];\n    58→      for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {\n    59→        elements.push(locator.nth(i));\n    60→      }\n    61→      return elements;\n    62→    } else {\n    63→      return await page.$$(selector);\n    64→    }\n    65→  } catch (error) {\n    66→    if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n    67→      console.log(\n    68→        '⚠️  Navigation detected during querySelectorAll, returning empty array'\n    69→      );\n    70→      return [];\n    71→    }\n    72→    throw error;\n    73→  }\n    74→}\n    75→\n    76→/**\n    77→ * Find elements by text content (works across both engines)\n    78→ * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n    79→ * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n    80→ * @param {string} options.text - Text to search for\n    81→ * @param {string} options.selector - Optional base selector (e.g., 'button', 'a', 'span')\n    82→ * @param {boolean} options.exact - Exact match vs contains (default: false)\n    83→ * @returns {Promise<string>} - CSS selector that can be used with other commander methods\n    84→ */\n    85→export async function findByText(options = {}) {\n    86→  const { engine, text, selector = '*', exact = false } = options;\n    87→\n    88→  if (!text) {\n    89→    throw new Error('text is required in options');\n    90→  }\n    91→\n    92→  if (engine === 'playwright') {\n    93→    // Playwright supports :has-text() natively\n    94→    const textSelector = exact ? `:text-is(\"${text}\")` : `:has-text(\"${text}\")`;\n    95→    return `${selector}${textSelector}`;\n    96→  } else {\n    97→    // For Puppeteer, we need to use XPath or evaluate\n    98→    // Return a special selector marker that will be handled by other methods\n    99→    return {\n   100→      _isPuppeteerTextSelector: true,\n   101→      baseSelector: selector,\n   102→      text,\n   103→      exact,\n   104→    };\n   105→  }\n   106→}\n   107→\n   108→/**\n   109→ * Check if a selector is a Playwright-specific text selector\n   110→ * @param {string} selector - The selector to check\n   111→ * @returns {boolean} - True if selector contains Playwright text pseudo-selectors\n   112→ */\n   113→function isPlaywrightTextSelector(selector) {\n   114→  if (typeof selector !== 'string') {\n   115→    return false;\n   116→  }\n   117→  return selector.includes(':has-text(') || selector.includes(':text-is(');\n   118→}\n   119→\n   120→/**\n   121→ * Parse a Playwright text selector to extract base selector and text\n   122→ * @param {string} selector - Playwright text selector like 'a:has-text(\"text\")'\n   123→ * @returns {Object|null} - { baseSelector, text, exact } or null if not parseable\n   124→ */\n   125→function parsePlaywrightTextSelector(selector) {\n   126→  // Match patterns like 'a:has-text(\"text\")' or 'button:text-is(\"exact text\")'\n   127→  const hasTextMatch = selector.match(/^(.+?):has-text\\(\"(.+?)\"\\)$/);\n   128→  if (hasTextMatch) {\n   129→    return {\n   130→      baseSelector: hasTextMatch[1],\n   131→      text: hasTextMatch[2],\n   132→      exact: false,\n   133→    };\n   134→  }\n   135→\n   136→  const textIsMatch = selector.match(/^(.+?):text-is\\(\"(.+?)\"\\)$/);\n   137→  if (textIsMatch) {\n   138→    return {\n   139→      baseSelector: textIsMatch[1],\n   140→      text: textIsMatch[2],\n   141→      exact: true,\n   142→    };\n   143→  }\n   144→\n   145→  return null;\n   146→}\n   147→\n   148→/**\n   149→ * Normalize selector to handle both Puppeteer and Playwright text selectors\n   150→ * Converts engine-specific text selectors to valid CSS selectors for browser context\n   151→ *\n   152→ * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n   153→ * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n   154→ * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n   155→ * @param {string|Object} options.selector - CSS selector or text selector object\n   156→ * @returns {Promise<string|null>} - Valid CSS selector or null if not found\n   157→ */\n   158→export async function normalizeSelector(options = {}) {\n   159→  const { page, engine, selector } = options;\n   160→\n   161→  if (!selector) {\n   162→    throw new Error('selector is required in options');\n   163→  }\n   164→\n   165→  // Handle Playwright text selectors (strings containing :has-text or :text-is)\n   166→  // These are valid for Playwright's locator API but NOT for document.querySelectorAll\n   167→  if (\n   168→    typeof selector === 'string' &&\n   169→    engine === 'playwright' &&\n   170→    isPlaywrightTextSelector(selector)\n   171→  ) {\n   172→    const parsed = parsePlaywrightTextSelector(selector);\n   173→    if (!parsed) {\n   174→      // Could not parse, return as-is and hope for the best\n   175→      return selector;\n   176→    }\n   177→\n   178→    try {\n   179→      // Use page.evaluate to find matching element and generate a valid CSS selector\n   180→      const result = await page.evaluate(({ baseSelector, text, exact }) => {\n   181→        const elements = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(baseSelector));\n   182→        const matchingElement = elements.find((el) => {\n   183→          const elementText = el.textContent.trim();\n   184→          return exact ? elementText === text : elementText.includes(text);\n   185→        });\n   186→\n   187→        if (!matchingElement) {\n   188→          return null;\n   189→        }\n   190→\n   191→        // Generate a unique selector using data-qa or nth-of-type\n   192→        const dataQa = matchingElement.getAttribute('data-qa');\n   193→        if (dataQa) {\n   194→          return `[data-qa=\"${dataQa}\"]`;\n   195→        }\n   196→\n   197→        // Use nth-of-type as fallback\n   198→        const tagName = matchingElement.tagName.toLowerCase();\n   199→        const siblings = Array.from(\n   200→          matchingElement.parentElement.children\n   201→        ).filter((el) => el.tagName.toLowerCase() === tagName);\n   202→        const index = siblings.indexOf(matchingElement);\n   203→        return `${tagName}:nth-of-type(${index + 1})`;\n   204→      }, parsed);\n   205→\n   206→      return result;\n   207→    } catch (error) {\n   208→      if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n   209→        console.log(\n   210→          '⚠️  Navigation detected during normalizeSelector (Playwright), returning null'\n   211→        );\n   212→        return null;\n   213→      }\n   214→      throw error;\n   215→    }\n   216→  }\n   217→\n   218→  // Plain string selector - return as-is\n   219→  if (typeof selector === 'string') {\n   220→    return selector;\n   221→  }\n   222→\n   223→  // Handle Puppeteer text selector objects\n   224→  if (selector._isPuppeteerTextSelector) {\n   225→    try {\n   226→      // Find element by text and generate a unique selector\n   227→      const result = await page.evaluate(\n   228→        (baseSelector, text, exact) => {\n   229→          const elements = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(baseSelector));\n   230→          const matchingElement = elements.find((el) => {\n   231→            const elementText = el.textContent.trim();\n   232→            return exact ? elementText === text : elementText.includes(text);\n   233→          });\n   234→\n   235→          if (!matchingElement) {\n   236→            return null;\n   237→          }\n   238→\n   239→          // Generate a unique selector using data-qa or nth-of-type\n   240→          const dataQa = matchingElement.getAttribute('data-qa');\n   241→          if (dataQa) {\n   242→            return `[data-qa=\"${dataQa}\"]`;\n   243→          }\n   244→\n   245→          // Use nth-of-type as fallback\n   246→          const tagName = matchingElement.tagName.toLowerCase();\n   247→          const siblings = Array.from(\n   248→            matchingElement.parentElement.children\n   249→          ).filter((el) => el.tagName.toLowerCase() === tagName);\n   250→          const index = siblings.indexOf(matchingElement);\n   251→          return `${tagName}:nth-of-type(${index + 1})`;\n   252→        },\n   253→        selector.baseSelector,\n   254→        selector.text,\n   255→        selector.exact\n   256→      );\n   257→\n   258→      return result;\n   259→    } catch (error) {\n   260→      if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n   261→        console.log(\n   262→          '⚠️  Navigation detected during normalizeSelector (Puppeteer), returning null'\n   263→        );\n   264→        return options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {string} options.selector - CSS selector\n * @returns {Promise<Object|null>} - Element handle or null\n */\nexport async function querySelector(options = {}) {\n  const { page, engine, selector } = options;\n\n  if (!selector) {\n    throw new Error('selector is required in options');\n  }\n\n  try {\n    if (engine === 'playwright') {\n      const locator = createPlaywrightLocator({ page, selector }).first();\n      const count = await locator.count();\n      return count > 0 ? locator : null;\n    } else {\n      return await page.$(selector);\n    }\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n      console.log(\n        '⚠️  Navigation detected during querySelector, returning null'\n      );\n      return null;\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Query all elements\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {string} options.selector - CSS selector\n * @returns {Promise<Array>} - Array of element handles\n */\nexport async function querySelectorAll(options = {}) {\n  const { page, engine, selector } = options;\n\n  if (!selector) {\n    throw new Error('selector is required in options');\n  }\n\n  try {\n    if (engine === 'playwright') {\n      const locator = createPlaywrightLocator({ page, selector });\n      const count = await locator.count();\n      const elements = [];\n      for (let i = 0; i < count; i++) {\n        elements.push(locator.nth(i));\n detected during querySelectorAll, returning empty array'\n      );\n      return [];\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Find elements by text content (works across both engines)\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {string} options.text - Text to search for\n * @param {string} options.selector - Optional base selector (e.g., 'button', 'a', 'span')\n * @param {boolean} options.exact - Exact match vs contains (default: false)\n * @returns {Promise<string>} - CSS selector that can be used with other commander methods\n */\nexport async function findByText(options = {}) {\n  const { engine, text, selector = '*', exact = false } = options;\n\n  if (!text) {\n    throw new Error('text is required in options');\n  }\n\n  if (engine === 'playwright') {\n    // Playwright supports :has-text() natively\n    const textSelector = exact ? `:text-is(\"${text}\")` : `:has-text(\"${text}\")`;\n    return `${selector}${textSelector}`;\n  } else {\n    // For Puppeteer, we need to use XPath or evaluate\n    // Return a special selector marker that will be handled by other methods\n    return {\n      _isPuppeteerTextSelector: true,\n      baseSelector: selector,\n      text,\n      exact,\n    };\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if a selector is a Playwright-specific text selector\n * @param {string} selector - The selector to check\n * @returns {boolean} - True if selector contains Playwright text pseudo-selectors\n */\nfunction isPlaywrightTextSelector(selector) {\n  if (typeof selector !== 'string') {\n    return false;\n  }\n  return selector.includes(':has-text(') || selector.includes(':text-is(');\n}\n\n/**\n * Parse a Playwright text selector to extract base selector and text\n * @param {string} selector - Playwright text selector like 'a:has-text(\"text\")'\n * @returns {Object|null} - { baseSelector, text, exact } or null if not parseable\n */\nfunction parsePlaywrightTextSelector(selector) {\n  // Match patterns like 'a:has-text(\"text\")' or 'button:text-is(\"exact text\")'\n  const hasTextMatch = selector.match(/^(.+?):has-text\\(\"(.+?)\"\\)$/);\n  if (hasTextMatch) {\n    return {\n      baseSelector: hasTextMatch[1],\n      text: hasTextMatch[2],\n      exact: false,\n    };\n  }\n\n  const textIsMatch = selector.match(/^(.+?):text-is\\(\"(.+?)\"\\)$/);\n  if (textIsMatch) {\n    return {\n      baseSelector: textIsMatch[1],\n      text: textIsMatch[2],\n      exact: true,\n    };\n  }\n\n  return null;\n}\n\n/**\n * Normalize selector to handle both Puppeteer and Playwright text selectors\n * Converts engine-specific text selectors to valid CSS selectors for browser context\n *\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {string|Object} options.selector - CSS selector or text selector object\n * @returns {Promise<string|null>} - Valid CSS selector or null if not found\n */\nexport async function normalizeSelector(options = {}) {\n  const { page, engine, selector } = options;\n\n  if (!selector) {\n    throw new Error('selector is required in options');\n  }\n\n  // Handle Playwright text selectors (strings containing :has-text or :text-is)\n  // These are valid for Playwright's locator API but NOT for document.querySelectorAll\n  if (\n    typeof selector === 'string' &&\n    engine === 'playwright' &&\n    isPlaywrightTextSelector(selector)\n  ) {\n    const parsed = parsePlaywrightTextSelector(selector);\n    if (!parsed) {\n      // Could not parse, return as-is and hope for the best\n      return selector;\n    }\n\n    try {\n      // Use page.evaluate to find matching element and generate a valid CSS selector\n      const result = await page.evaluate(({ baseSelector, text, exact }) => {\n        const elements = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(baseSelector));\n        const matchingElement = elements.find((el) => {\n          const elementText = el.textContent.trim();\n          return exact ? elementText === text : elementText.includes(text);\n        });\n\n        if (!matchingElement) {\n          return null;\n        }\n\n        // Generate a unique selector using data-qa or nth-of-type\n        const dataQa = matchingElement.getAttribute('data-qa');\n        if (dataQa) {\n          return `[data-qa=\"${dataQa}\"]`;\n        }\n\n        // Use nth-of-type as fallback\n        const tagName = matchingElement.tagName.toLowerCase();\n        const siblings = Array.from(\n          matchingElement.parentElement.children\n        ).filter((el) => el.tagName.toLowerCase() === tagName);\n        const index = siblings.indexOf(matchingElement);\n        return `${tagName}:nth-of-type(${index + 1})`;\n      }, parsed);\n\n      return result;\n    } catch (error) {\n      if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n        console.log(\n          '⚠️  Navigation detected during normalizeSelector (Playwright), returning null'\n        );\n        return null;\n      }\n      throw error;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Plain string selector - return as-is\n  if (typeof selector === 'string') {\n    return selector;\n  }\n\n  // Handle Puppeteer text selector objects\n  if (selector._isPuppeteerTextSelector) {\n    try {\n      // Find element by text and generate a unique selector\n      const result = await page.evaluate(\n        (baseSelector, text, exact) => {\n          const elements = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(baseSelector));\n          const matchingElement = elements.find((el) => {\n            const elementText = el.textContent.trim();\n            return exact ? elementText === text : elementText.includes(text);\n          });\n\n          if (!matchingElement) {\n            return null;\n          }\n\n          // Generate a unique selector using data-qa or nth-of-type\n          const dataQa = matchingElement.getAttribute('data-qa');\n          if (dataQa) {\n            return `[data-qa=\"${dataQa}\"]`;\n          }\n\n          // Use nth-of-type as fallback\n          const tagName = matchingElement.tagName.toLowerCase();\n          const siblings = Array.from(\n            matchingElement.parentElement.children\n          ).filter((el) => el.tagName.toLowerCase() === tagName);\n          const index = siblings.indexOf(matchingElement);\n          return `${tagName}:nth-of-type(${index + 1})`;\n        },\n        selector.baseSelector,\n        selector.text,\n        selector.exact\n      );\n\n      return result;\n    } catch (error) {\n      if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n        console.log(\n          '⚠️  Navigation detected during normalizeSelector (Puppeteer), returning null'\n        );\n        return null;\n      }\n      throw error;\n    }\n  }\n\n  return selector;\n}\n\n/**\n * Enhanced wrapper for functions that need to handle text selectors\n * @param {Function} fn - The function to wrap\n * @param {string} engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Object} page - Browser page object\n * @returns {Function} - Wrapped function\n */\nexport function withTextSelectorSupport(fn, engine, page) {\n  return async (options = {}) => {\n    let { selector } = options;\n\n    // Normalize Puppeteer text selectors (object format)\n    if (\n      engine === 'puppeteer' &&\n      typeof selector === 'object' &&\n      selector._isPuppeteerTextSelector\n    ) {\n      selector = await normalizeSelector({ page, engine, selector });\n      if (!selector) {\n        throw new Error('Element with specified text not found');\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Normalize Playwright text selectors (string format with :has-text or :text-is)\n    if (\n      engine === 'playwright' &&\n      typeof selector === 'string' &&\n      isPlaywrightTextSelector(selector)\n    ) {\n      selector = await normalizeSelector({ page, engine, selector });\n      if (!selector) {\n        throw new Error('Element with specified text not found');\n      }\n    }\n\n    return fn({ ...options, selector });\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Wait for selector to appear\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param :nth-of-type() pseudo-selectors which don't work in Playwright locators\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.selector - CSS selector\n * @returns {Object} - Playwright locator\n */\nexport function createPlaywrightLocator(options = {}) {\n  const { page, selector } = options;\n\n  if (!selector) {\n    throw new Error('selector is required in options');\n  }\n  // Check if selector has :nth-of-type(n) pattern\n  const nthOfTypeMatch = selector.match(/^(.+):nth-of-type\\((\\d+)\\)$/);\n\n  if @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {string|Object} options.selector - CSS selector or element/locator\n * @returns {Promise<Object|null>} - Locator for Playwright, Element for Puppeteer (can be null)\n */\nexport async function getLocatorOrElement(options = {}) {\n  const { page, engine, selector } = options;\n\n  if (!selector) {\n    throw new Error('selector is required in options');\n  }\n  if (typeof selector !== 'string') {\n    return selector; // Already a locator/element\n  }\n\n  if (engine === 'playwright') {\n    return createPlaywrightLocator({ page, selector });\n  } else {\n    // For Puppeteer, return element (can be null if doesn't exist)\n    return await page.$(selector);\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Get locator/element and wait for it to be visible\n * Unified waiting behavior for both engines\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {string|Object} options.selector - CSS selector or existing locator/element\n * @param {number} options.timeout - Timeout in ms (default: TIMING.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT)\n * @param {boolean} options.throwOnNavigation - Whether to throw on navigation error (default: true)\n * @returns {Promise<Object|null>} - Locator for Playwright (first match), Element for Puppeteer, or null on navigation\n * @throws {Error} - If element not found or not visible within timeout (unless navigation error and throwOnNavigation is false)\n */\nexport async function waitForLocatorOrElement(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    selector,\n    timeout = TIMING.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT,\n    throwOnNavigation = true,\n  } = options;\n\n  if (!selector) {\n    throw new Error('selector is required in options');\n  }\n\n  try {\n    if (engine === 'playwright') {\n      const locator = await getLocatorOrElement({ page, engine, selector });\n      // Use .first() to handle multiple matches (Playwright strict mode)\n      const firstLocator = locator.first();\n      await firstLocator.waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout });\n      return firstLocator;\n    } else {\n      // Puppeteer: wait for selector to be visible (returns first match by default)\n      await page.waitForSelector(selector, { visible: true, timeout });\n      const element = await page.$(selector);\n }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Wait for element to be visible (works with existing locatorOrElement)\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Element or locator to wait for\n * @param {number} options.timeout - Timeout in ms (default: TIMING.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT)\n * @returns {Promise<void>}\n */\nexport async function waitForVisible(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    engine,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    timeout = TIMING.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT,\n  } = options;\n\n  if (!locatorOrElement) {\n    throw new Error('locatorOrElement is required in options');\n  }\n\n  if (engine === 'playwright') {\n    await locatorOrElement.waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout });\n  } else {\n    // For Puppeteer, element is already fetched, just verify it exists\n    if (!locatorOrElement) {\n      throw new Error('Element not found');\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Create locator (Playwright-style fluent API)\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {string} options.selector - CSS selector\n * @returns {Object} - Locator object (Playwright) or wrapper (Puppeteer)\n */\nexport function locator(options = {}) {\n  const { page, engine, selector } = options;\n\n  if (!selector) {\n    throw new Error('selector is required in options');\n  }\n\n  if (engine === 'playwright') page.type(sel, text, options);\n      },\n      async textContent() {\n        const element = await page.$(sel);\n        if (!element) {\n          return null;\n        }\n        return await page.evaluate((el) => el.textContent, element);\n      },\n      async inputValue() {\n        const element = await page.$(sel);\n        if (!element) {\n          return '';\n        }\n        return await page.evaluate((el) => el.value, element);\n      },\n      async getAttribute(name) {\n        const element = await page.$(sel);\n        if (!element) {\n          return null;\n selector\n\n    Returns:\n        Element handle or None\n    \"\"\"\n    if not selector:\n        raise ValueError(\"selector is required\")\n\n    try:\n        if engine == \"playwright\":\n            locator = create_playwright_locator(page, selector).first()\n            count = await locator.count()\n            return locator if count > 0 else None\n        else:\n            from selenium.common.exceptions import NoSuchElementException\n            from selenium.webdriver.common.by import By\n\n            try:\n                return page.find_element(By.CSS_SELECTOR, selector)\n            except NoSuchElementException:\n                return None\n    except Exception as error:\n        if is_navigation_error(error):\n            print(\"Navigation detected during query_selector, returning None\")\n            return None\n        raise\n\n\nasync def query_selector_all(\n    page: Any,\n    engine: EngineType,\n    selector: str,\n) -> list[Any]:\n    \"\"\"Query all elements.\n\n    Args:\n        page: Browser page object\n        engine: Engine type ('playwright' or 'selenium')\n        selector: CSS selector\n\n    Returns:\n        Array of element handles\n    \"\"\"\n    if not selector:\n        raise ValueError(\"selector is required\")\n\n    try:\n        if engine == \"playwright\":\n            locator = create_playwright_locator(page, selector)\n            count = await locator.count()\n            elements = []\n            for i in range(count):\n                elements.append(locator.nth(i))\n        engine: Engine type ('playwright' or 'selenium')\n        text: Text to search for\n        selector: Optional base selector (e.g., 'button', 'a', 'span')\n        exact: Exact match vs contains (default: False)\n\n    Returns:\n        CSS selector string (for Playwright) or SeleniumTextSelector object\n    \"\"\"\n    if not text:\n        raise ValueError(\"text is required\")\n\n    if engine == \"playwright\":\n        # Playwright supports :has-text() natively\n        text_selector = f':text-is(\"{text}\")' if exact else f':has-text(\"{text}\")'\n        return f\"{selector}{text_selector}\"\n    else:\n        # For Selenium, return a special selector object\n        return SeleniumTextSelector(\n            base_selector=selector,\n            text=text,\n            exact=exact,\n        )\n\n\ndef is_playwright_text_selector(selector: Any) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a selector is a Playwright-specific text selector.\n\n    Args:\n        selector: The selector to check\n\n    Returns:\n        True if selector contains Playwright text pseudo-selectors\n    \"\"\"\n    if not isinstance(selector, str):\n        return False\n    return \":has-text(\" in selector or \":text-is(\" in selector\n\n\ndef parse_playwright_text_selector(selector: str) -> dict | None:\n    \"\"\"Parse a Playwright text selector to extract base selector and text.\n\n    Args:\n        selector: Playwright text selector like 'a:has-text(\"text\")'\n\n    Returns:\n        Dictionary with base_selector, text, exact or None if not parseable\n    \"\"\"\n    # Match patterns like 'a:has-text(\"text\")' or 'button:text-is(\"exact text\")'\n    has_text_match = re.match(r'^(.+?):has-text\\(\"(.+?)\"\\)$', selector)\n    if has_text_match:\n        return {\n            \"base_selector\": has_text_match.group(1),\n            \"text\": has_text_match.group(2),\n            \"exact\": False,\n        }\n\n    text_is_match = re.match(r'^(.+?):text-is\\(\"(.+?)\"\\)$', selector)\n    if text_is_match:\n        return {\n            \"base_selector\": text_is_match.group(1),\n            \"text\": text_is_match.group(2),\n            \"exact\": True,\n        }\n\n    return None\n\n\nasync def normalize_selector(\n    page: Any,\n    engine: EngineType,\n    selector: str | SeleniumTextSelector,\n) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"Normalize selector to handle both Selenium and Playwright text selectors.\n\n    Converts engine-specific text selectors to valid CSS selectors for browser context.\n\n    Args:\n        page: Browser page object\n        engine: Engine type ('playwright' or 'selenium')\n        selector: CSS selector or text selector object\n\n    Returns:\n        Valid CSS selector or None if not found\n    \"\"\"\n    if not selector:\n        raise ValueError(\"selector is required\")\n\n    # Handle Playwright text selectors (strings containing :has-text or :text-is)\n    if (\n        isinstance(selector, str)\n        and engine == \"playwright\"\n        and is_playwright_text_selector(selector)\n    ):\n        parsed = parse_playwright_text_selector(selector)\n        if not parsed:\n            return selector\n\n        try:\n            # Use page.evaluate to find matching element\n            result = await page.evaluate(\n                \"\"\"({ baseSelector, text, exact }) => {\n                    const elements = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(baseSelector));\n                    const matchingElement = elements.find(el => {\n                        const elementText = el.textContent.trim();\n                        return exact ? elementText === text : elementText.includes(text);\n                    });\n\n                    if (!matchingElement) {\n                        return null;\n                    }\n\n                    // Generate a unique selector using data-qa or nth-of-type\n                    const dataQa = matchingElement.getAttribute('data-qa');\n                    if (dataQa) {\n                        return `[data-qa=\"${dataQa}\"]`;\n                    }\n\n                    // Use nth-of-type as fallback\n                    const tagName = matchingElement.tagName.toLowerCase();\n                    const siblings = Array.from(matchingElement.parentElement.children)\n                        .filter(el => el.tagName.toLowerCase() === tagName);\n                    const index = siblings.indexOf(matchingElement);\n                    return `${tagName}:nth-of-type(${index + 1})`;\n                }\"\"\",\n                {\n                    \"baseSelector\": parsed[\"base_selector\"],\n                    \"text\": parsed[\"text\"],\n                    \"exact\": parsed[\"exact\"],\n                },\n            )\n            return result\n        except Exception as error:\n            if is_navigation_error(error):\n                print(\n                    \"Navigation detected during normalize_selector (Playwright), \"\n                    \"returning None\"\n                )\n                return None\n            raise\n\n    # Plain string selector - return as-is\n    if isinstance(selector, str):\n        return selector\n\n    # Handle Selenium text selector objects\n    if isinstance(selector, SeleniumTextSelector):\n        try:\n            # Find element by text and generate a unique selector\n            script = \"\"\"\n                const [baseSelector, text, exact] = arguments;\n                const elements = Array.from(document.querySelectorAll(baseSelector));\n                const matchingElement = elements.find(el => {\n                    const elementText = el.textContent.trim();\n                    return exact ? elementText === text : elementText.includes(text);\n                });\n\n                if (!matchingElement) {\n                    return null;\n                }\n\n                // Generate a unique selector using data-qa or nth-of-type\n                const dataQa = matchingElement.getAttribute('data-qa');\n                if (dataQa) {\n                    return `[data-qa=\"${dataQa}\"]`;\n                }\n\n                // Use nth-of-type as fallback\n                const tagName = matchingElement.tagName.toLowerCase();\n                const siblings = Array.from(matchingElement.parentElement.children)\n                    .filter(el => el.tagName.toLowerCase() === tagName);\n                const index = siblings.indexOf(matchingElement);\n                return `${tagName}:nth-of-type(${index + 1})`;\n            \"\"\"\n            result = page.execute_script(\n                script,\n                selector.base_selector,\n                selector.text,\n                selector.exact,\n            )\n            return result\n        except Exception as error:\n            if is_navigation_error(error):\n                print(\n                    \"Navigation detected during normalize_selector (Selenium), \"\n                    \"returning None\"\n                )\n                return None\n            raise\n\n    return str(selector)\n\n\ndef with_text_selector_support(\n    fn: Callable,\n    engine: EngineType,\n    page: Any,\n) -> Callable:\n    \"\"\"Enhanced wrapper for functions that need to handle text selectors.\n\n    Args:\n        fn: The function to wrap\n        engine: Engine type ('playwright' or 'selenium')\n        page: Browser page object\n\n    Returns:\n        Wrapped function\n !== postClickState.ariaSelected) {\n    61→        return { verified: true, reason: 'aria-selected changed' };\n    62→      }\n    63→      if (preClickState.checked !== postClickState.checked) {\n    64→        return { verified: true, reason: 'checked state changed' };\n    65→      }\n    66→      if (preClickState.className !== postClickState.className) {\n    67→        return { verified: // Navigation during click is considered verified (click triggered navigation)\n   241→      return {\n   242→        clicked: false,\n   243→        verified: true,\n   244→        reason: 'navigation during click',\n   245→      };\n   246→    }\n   247→    throw error;\n   248→  }\n   249→}\n   250→\n   251→/**\n   252→ * Detect if a click caused navigation by checking URL change or NavigationManager instance (optional)\n   256→ * @param {string} options.startUrl - URL before click\n   257→ * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n   258→ * @returns {Promise<{navigated: boolean, newUrl: string}>}\n   259→ */\n   260→async function detectNavigation(options = {}) {\n   261→  const { page, navigationManager, startUrl, log } = options;\n   262→\n   263→  const currentUrl = page.url();\n   264→  const urlChanged = currentUrl !== startUrl;\n   265→\n   266→  if (navigationManager && navigationManager.isNavigating()) {\n   267→    log.debug(() => '🔄 Navigation detected via NavigationManager');\n   268→    return { navigated: true, newUrl: currentUrl };\n   269→  }\n   270→\n   271→  if (urlChanged) {\n   272→    log.debug(() => `🔄 URL changed: ${startUrl} → ${currentUrl}`);\n   273→    return { navigated: true, newUrl: currentUrl };\n   274→  }\n   275→\n   276→  return { navigated: false, newUrl: currentUrl };\n   277→}\n   278→\n   279→/**\n   280→ * {boolean} options.verbose - Enable verbose logging\n   287→ * @param {string|Object} options.selector - CSS selector, ElementHandle, or Playwright Locator\n   288→ * @param {boolean} options.scrollIntoView - Scroll into view (default: true)\n   289→ * @param {number} options.waitAfterScroll - Wait time after scroll in ms\n   290→ * @param {boolean} options.smoothScroll - Use 294→async function prepareElement(options = {}) {\n   295→  const {\n   296→    page,\n   297→    engine,\n   298→    wait,\n   299→    log,\n   300→    verbose = false,\n   301→    selector,\n   302→    scrollIntoView: shouldScroll = true,\n   303→    waitAfterScroll,\n   304→    smoothScroll = true,\n   305→    timeout,\n   306→  } = options;\n   307→\n   308→  // Get locator/element and wait for it to be visible (unified for both engines)\n   309→  const locatorOrElement = await waitForLocatorOrElement({\n   310→    page,\n   311→    engine,\n   312→    selector,\n   313→    timeout,\n   314→  });\n   315→\n   316→  // Log element info if verbose\n   317→  if (verbose) {\n   318→    await logElementInfo({ page, engine, log, locatorOrElement });\n   319→  }\n   320→\n   321→  // Scroll into view (if requested and needed)\n   322→  if (shouldScroll) {\n   323→    const behavior = smoothScroll ? 'smooth' : 'instant';\n   324→    const scrollResult = await scrollIntoViewIfNeeded({\n   325→      page,\n   326→      engine,\n   327→      wait,\n   328→      log,\n   329→      locatorOrElement,\n   330→      behavior,\n   331→      waitAfterScroll,\n   332→      verify: false, // Don't verify scroll here, we verify the overall click\n   333→    });\n   334→    // Check if scroll was aborted due to navigation/stop\n   335→    if (!scrollResult.skipped && !scrollResult.scrolled) {\n   336→      return { locatorOrElement: null, scrolled: false, navigated: true };\n   337→    }\n   338→    return { locatorOrElement, scrolled: true, navigated: false };\n   339→  } else {\n   340→    log.debug(() => `🔍 [VERBOSE] Skipping scroll (scrollIntoView: false)`);\n   341→    return { locatorOrElement, scrolled: false, navigated: false };\n   342→  }\n   343→}\n   344→\n   345→/**\n   346→ * Execute the click operation with verification\n   347→ * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n   403→ * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n   404→ * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n   405→ * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n   406→ * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (optional)\n   407→ * @param {Object} options.networkTracker - NetworkTracker instance (optional)\n   408→ * @param {string} options.startUrl - URL before click\n   409→ * @param {boolean} options.waitForNavigation - Wait for navigation to complete\n   410→ * @param {number} options.navigationCheckDelay - Time to check if navigation started\n   411→ * @param {number} options.waitAfterClick - Wait time after click in ms\n   412→ * @returns {Promise<{navigated: boolean, verified: boolean, reason: string}>}\n   413→ */\n   414→async function handleNavigationAfterClick(options = {}) {\n   415→  const {\n   416→    page,\n   417→    wait,\n   418→    log,\n   419→    navigationManager,\n   420→    networkTracker,\n   421→    startUrl,\n   422→    waitForNavigation = true,\n   423→    navigationCheckDelay = 500,\n   424→    waitAfterClick = 1000,\n   425→  } = options;\n   426→\n   427→  // Check if click caused navigation\n   428→  if (waitForNavigation) {\n   429→    // Wait briefly for navigation to potentially start\n   430→    await wait({\n 
[2026-01-13T18:14:37.046Z] [INFO]   431→      ms: navigationCheckDelay,\n   432→      reason: 'checking for navigation after click',\n   433→    });\n   434→\n   435→    // Detect if navigation occurred\n   436→    const { navigated, newUrl } = await detectNavigation({\n   437→      page,\n   438→      navigationManager,\n   439→      startUrl,\n   440→      log,\n   441→    });\n   442→\n   443→    if (navigated) {\n   444→      log.debug(() => `🔄 Click triggered navigation to: ${newUrl}`);\n   445→\n   446→      // Wait for page to be fully ready (network idle + no more redirects)\n   447→      // Note: If navigationManager detected external navigation, it's already waiting\n   448→      // We still call waitForPageReady here to ensure we don't return until page is ready\n   449→   timeout: 120000,\n   453→          reason: 'after click navigation',\n   454→        });\n   455→      } else if (networkTracker) {\n   456→        // Without navigation manager, use network tracker directly with 30s idle time\n   457→        await networkTracker.waitForNetworkIdle({\n   458→          timeout: 120000,\n   459→          // idleTime defaults to 30000ms from tracker config\n   460→        });\n   461→      } else {\n   462→        // Fallback: wait a bit for page to settle\n   463→        await wait({ ms: 2000, reason: 'page settle after navigation' });\n   464→      }\n   465→\n   466→      // Navigation is considered successful verification\n   467→      return {\n   468→        navigated: true,\n   469→        verified: true,\n   470→        reason: 'click triggered navigation',\n   471→      };\n   472→    }\n   473→  }\n   474→\n   475→  // No navigation - wait after click if specified (useful for modals)\n   476→  if (waitAfterClick > 0) {\n   477→    const waitResult = await wait({\n   478→      ms: waitAfterClick,\n   479→      reason: 'post-click settling time for modal scroll capture',\n   480→    });\n   481→\n   482→    // Check if wait was aborted due to navigation that happened during the wait\n   483→    if (waitResult && waitResult.aborted) {\n   484→      log.debug(\n   485→        () => '🔄 Navigation detected during post-click wait (wait was aborted)'\n   486→      );\n   487→\n   488→      // Re-check for navigation since it happened during the wait\n   489→      const { navigated: lateNavigated, newUrl: lateUrl } =\n   490→        await detectNavigation({\n   491→          page,\n   492→          navigationManager,\n   493→          startUrl,\n   494→          log,\n   495→        });\n   496→\n   497→      if (lateNavigated) 508→        return {\n   509→          navigated: true,\n   510→          verified: true,\n   511→          reason: 'late-detected navigation',\n   512→        };\n   513→      }\n   514→    }\n   515→  }\n   516→\n   517→  // Final check: did navigation happen while we were processing?\n   518→  // This catches cases where navigation started but wasn't detected earlier\n   519→  if (navigationManager && navigationManager.shouldAbort()) {\n   520→    log.debug(\n   521→      () => '🔄 Navigation detected via abort signal at end of click processing'\n   522→    );\n   523→\n   524→    await navigationManager.waitForPageReady({\n   525→      timeout: 120000,\n   526→      reason: 'after abort-detected click navigation',\n   527→    });\n   528→\n   529→    return {\n   530→      navigated: true,\n   531→      verified: true,\n   532→      reason: 'abort-signal navigation',\n   533→    };\n   534→  }\n   535→\n   536→  // If we have network tracking, wait for any XHR/fetch to complete\n   537→  // Use shorter idle time for non-navigation clicks (just waiting for XHR, not full page load)\n   538→  if (networkTracker) {\n   539→    await networkTracker.waitForNetworkIdle({\n   540→      timeout: 10000, // Maximum wait time\n   541→      idleTime: 2000, // Only 2 seconds of idle needed for XHR completion\n   542→    });\n   543→  }\n   544→\n   545→  return { navigated: false, verified: true, reason: 'no navigation detected' };\n   546→}\n   547→\n   548→/**\n   549→ * Click a button or element (high-level with scrolling and waits)\n   550→ * Now navigation-aware - automatically waits for page ready after navigation-causing clicks.\n   551→ *\n   552→ * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n   553→ * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n   554→ * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n   555→ * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n   556→ * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n   557→ * @param {boolean} options.verbose - Enable verbose logging\n   558→ * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (optional)\n   559→ * @param {Object} options.networkTracker - NetworkTracker instance (optional)\n   560→ * @param {string|Object} options.selector - CSS selector, ElementHandle, or Playwright Locator\n   561→ * @param {boolean} options.scrollIntoView - Scroll into view (default: true)\n   562→ * @param {number} options.waitAfterScroll - Wait time after scroll in ms (default: TIMING.DEFAULT_WAIT_AFTER_SCROLL)\n   563→ * @param {boolean} options.smoothScroll - Use smooth scroll animation (default: true)\n   564→ * @param {number} options.waitAfterClick - Wait time after click in ms (default: 1000). Gives modals time to capture scroll position before opening\n   565→ * @param {boolean} options.waitForNavigation - Wait for navigation to complete if click causes navigation (default: true)\n   566→ * @param {number} options.navigationCheckDelay - Time to check if navigation started (default: 500ms)\n   567→ * @param {number} options.timeout - Timeout in ms (default: TIMING.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT)\n   568→ * @param {boolean} options.verify - Whether to verify the click operation (default: true)\n   569→ * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional)\n   570→ * @returns {Promise<{clicked: boolean, navigated: boolean, verified: boolean, reason?: string}>}\n   571→ *   - clicked: true if click was performed\n   572→ *   - navigated: true if click caused navigation\n   573→ *   - verified: true if click was verified (navigation counts as verification)\n   574→ * @throws {Error} - If selector is missing, element not found, or click operation fails (except navigation/stop errors)\n   575→ */\n   576→export async function clickButton(options = {}) {\n   577→  const {\n   578→    page,\n   579→    engine,\n   580→    wait,\n   581→    log,\n   582→    verbose = false,\n   583→    navigationManager,\n   584→    networkTracker,\n   585→    selector,\n   586→    scrollIntoView: shouldScroll = true,\n   587→    waitAfterScroll = TIMING.DEFAULT_WAIT_AFTER_SCROLL,\n   588→    smoothScroll = true,\n   589→    waitAfterClick = 1000,\n   590→    waitForNavigation = true,\n   591→    navigationCheckDelay = 500,\n   592→    timeout = TIMING.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT,\n   593→    verify = true,\n   594→    verifyFn,\n   595→  } = options;\n   596→\n   597→  if (!selector) {\n   598→    throw new Error('clickButton: selector is required in options');\n   599→  }\n   600→\n   601→  // Record URL before click for navigation detection\n   602→  const startUrl = page.url();\n   603→\n   604→  try {\n   605→    // Step 1: Prepare element 612→      selector,\n   613→      scrollIntoView: shouldScroll,\n   614→      waitAfterScroll,\n   615→      smoothScroll,\n   616→      timeout,\n   617→    });\n   618→\n   619→    if (prepareResult.navigated) {\n   620→      return {\n   621→        clicked: false,\n   622→        navigated: true,\n   623→        verified: true,\n   624→        reason: 'navigation during scroll',\n   625→      };\n   626→    }\n   627→\n   628→    const { locatorOrElement } = prepareResult;\n   629→\n   630→    // Step 2: Execute click operation\n   631→    const clickResult = await executeClick({\n   632→      page,\n   633→      engine,\n   634→      log,\n   635→      locatorOrElement,\n   636→      noAutoScroll: !shouldScroll,\n   637→      verify,\n   638→      verifyFn,\n   639→    });\n   640→\n   641→    if (clickResult.navigated) {\n   642→      return {\n   643→        clicked: false,\n   644→        navigated: true,\n   645→        verified: true,\n   646→        reason: 'navigation during click',\n   647→      };\n   648→    }\n   649→\n   650→    // Step 3: Handle navigation detection and waiting\n   651→    const navResult = await handleNavigationAfterClick({\n   652→      page,\n   653→      wait,\n   654→      log,\n   655→      navigationManager,\n   656→      networkTracker,\n   657→      startUrl,\n   658→      waitForNavigation,\n   659→      navigationCheckDelay,\n   660→      waitAfterClick,\n   661→    });\n   662→\n   663→    return {\n   664→      clicked: true,\n   665→      navigated: navResult.navigated,\n   666→      verified: clickResult.verified && navResult.verified,\n   667→      reason: navResult.navigated ? navResult.reason : clickResult.reason,\n   668→    };\n   669→  } catch (error) {\n   670→    if (isNavigationError(error) || isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n   671→      console.log(\n   672→        '⚠️  Navigation/stop detected during clickButton, recovering gracefully'\n   673→      );\n   674→      // Navigation/stop during click is considered successful\n   675→      return {\n   676→        clicked: false,\n   677→        navigated: true,\n   678→        verified: true,\n   679→        reason: 'navigation/stop error',\n   680→      };\n   681→    }\n   682→    throw error;\n   683→  }\n   684→}\n   685→\n\n<system-reminder>\nWhenever you read a { TIMING } from '../core/constants.js';\nimport { isNavigationError } from '../core/navigation-safety.js';\nimport { isActionStoppedError } from '../core/page-trigger-manager.js';\nimport { waitForLocatorOrElement } from '../elements/locators.js';\nimport { scrollIntoViewIfNeeded } from './scroll.js';\nimport { logElementInfo } from '../elements/content.js';\nimport { createEngineAdapter } from '../core/engine-adapter.js';\n\n/**\n * Default verification function for click operations.\n * Verifies that the click had an effect by checking for common patterns:\n * - Element state changes (disabled, aria-pressed, etc.)\n * - Element class changes\n * - Element visibility changes\n *\n * Note: Navigation-triggering clicks are considered \"verified\" if navigation starts.\n *\n * @param {Object} options - Verification options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Element that was clicked\n * @param {Object} options.preClickState - State captured before click (optional)\n * @returns {Promise<{verified: boolean, reason: string}>}\n */\nexport async function defaultClickVerification(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    preClickState = {},\n    adapter: providedAdapter,\n  } = options;\n\n  try {\n    const adapter = providedAdapter || createEngineAdapter(page, engine);\n\n    // Get current element state\n    const getElementState = async () =>\n      await adapter.evaluateOnElement(locatorOrElement, (el) => ({\n        disabled: el.disabled,\n        ariaPressed: el.getAttribute('aria-pressed'),\n        ariaExpanded: el.getAttribute('aria-expanded'),\n        ariaSelected: el.getAttribute('aria-selected'),\n        checked: el.checked,\n        className: el.className,\n        isConnected: el.isConnected,\n      }));\n\n    const postClickState = await getElementState();\n\n    // If we have pre-click state, check for changes\n    if (preClickState && Object.keys(preClickState).length > 0) {\n      // Check for state changes that indicate click was processed\n      if (preClickState.ariaPressed !== postClickState.ariaPressed) {\n        return { verified: true, reason: 'aria-pressed changed' };\n      }\n      if (preClickState.ariaExpanded !== postClickState.ariaExpanded) {\n        return { verified: true, reason: 'aria-expanded changed' };\n      }\n      if (preClickState.ariaSelected !== postClickState.ariaSelected) {\n        return { verified: true, reason: 'aria-selected changed' };\n      }\n      if (preClickState.checked !== postClickState.checked) {\n        return { verified: true, reason: 'checked state changed' };\n      }\n      if (preClickState.className !== postClickState.className) {\n        return { verified: true, reason: 'className changed' };\n      }\n    }\n\n    // If element is still connected and not disabled, assume click worked\n    // (many clicks don't change element state - they trigger actions)\n    if (postClickState.isConnected) {\n      return {\n        verified: true,\n        reason: 'element still connected (assumed success)',\n      };\n    }\n\n    // Element was removed from DOM - likely click triggered UI change\n    return { verified: true, reason: 'element removed from DOM (UI updated)' };\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error) || isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n      // Navigation/stop during verification - click likely triggered navigation\n      return {\n        verified: true,\n        reason: 'navigation detected (expected for navigation clicks)',\n        navigationError: true,\n      };\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Capture element state before click for verification\n * @param {Object} options - Options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Element to capture state from\n * @param {Object} options.adapter - Engine adapter (optional, will be created if not provided)\n * @returns {Promise<Object>} - Pre-click state object\n */\nexport async function capturePreClickState(options = {}) {\n  const { page, engine, locatorOrElement, adapter: providedAdapter } = options;\n\n  try {\n    const adapter = providedAdapter || createEngineAdapter(page, engine);\n    return await adapter.evaluateOnElement(locatorOrElement, (el) => ({\n      disabled: el.disabled,\n      ariaPressed: el.getAttribute('aria-pressed'),\n      ariaExpanded: el.getAttribute('aria-expanded'),\n      ariaSelected: el.getAttribute('aria-selected'),\n      checked: el.checked,\n      className: el.className,\n      isConnected: el.isConnected,\n    }));\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error) || isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n      return {};\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Verify click operation\n * @param {Object} options - Verification options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Element that was clicked\n * @param {Object} options.preClickState - State captured before click\n * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional)\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @returns {Promise<{verified: boolean, reason: string}>}\n */\nexport async function verifyClick(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    preClickState = {},\n    verifyFn = defaultClickVerification,\n    log = { debug: () => {} },\n  } = options;\n\n  const result = await verifyFn({\n    page,\n    engine,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    preClickState,\n  });\n\n  if (result.verified) {\n    log.debug(() => `✅ Click verification passed: ${result.reason}`);\n  } else {\n    log.debug(\n      () => `⚠️  Click verification uncertain: ${result.reason || 'unknown'}`\n    );\n  }\n\n  return result;\n}\n\n/**\n * Click an element (low-level)\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object (required for verification)\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Element or locator to click\n * @param {boolean} options.noAutoScroll - Prevent Playwright's automatic scrolling (default: false)\n * @param {boolean} options.verify - Whether to verify the click operation (default: true)\n * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional)\n * @param {Object} options.adapter - Engine adapter (optional, will be created if not provided)\n * @returns {Promise<{clicked: boolean, verified: boolean, reason?: string}>}\n */\nexport async function clickElement(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    log,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    noAutoScroll = false,\n    verify = true,\n    verifyFn,\n    adapter: providedAdapter,\n  } = options;\n\n  if (!locatorOrElement) {\n    throw new Error('locatorOrElement is required in options');\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const adapter = providedAdapter || createEngineAdapter(page, engine);\n\n    // Capture pre-click state for verification\n    let preClickState = {};\n    if (verify && page) {\n      preClickState = await capturePreClickState({\n        page,\n        engine,\n        locatorOrElement,\n        adapter,\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Click with appropriate options\n    const clickOptions =\n      engine === 'playwright' && noAutoScroll ? { force: true } : {};\n    if (engine === 'playwright' && noAutoScroll) {\n      log.debug(() => `🔍 [VERBOSE] Clicking with noAutoScroll (force: true)`);\n    }\n    await adapter.click(locatorOrElement, clickOptions);\n\n    // Verify click if requested\n    if (verify && page) {\n      const verificationResult = await verifyClick({\n        page,\n        engine,\n        locatorOrElement,\n        preClickState,\n        verifyFn,\n        log,\n      });\n\n      return {\n        clicked: true,\n        verified: verificationResult.verified,\n        reason: verificationResult.reason,\n      };\n    }\n\n    return { clicked: true, verified: true };\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error) || isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n      console.log(\n        '⚠️  Navigation/stop detected during click, recovering gracefully'\n      );\n      // Navigation during click is considered verified (click triggered navigation)\n      return {\n        clicked: false,\n        verified: true,\n        reason: 'navigation during click',\n      };\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Detect if a click caused navigation by checking URL change or navigation state\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (optional)\n * @param {string} options.startUrl - URL before click\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @returns {Promise<{navigated: boolean, newUrl: string}>}\n */\nasync function detectNavigation(options = {}) {\n  const { page, navigationManager, startUrl, log } = options;\n\n  const currentUrl = page.url();\n  const urlChanged = currentUrl !== startUrl;\n\n  if (navigationManager && navigationManager.isNavigating()) {\n    log.debug(() => '🔄 Navigation detected via NavigationManager');\n    return { navigated: true, newUrl: currentUrl };\n  }\n\n  if (urlChanged) {\n    log.debug(() => `🔄 URL changed: ${startUrl} → ${currentUrl}`);\n    return { navigated: true, newUrl: currentUrl };\n  }\n\n  return { navigated: false, newUrl: currentUrl };\n}\n\n/**\n * Prepare element for clicking - find, validate, and optionally scroll into view\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type\n * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @param {boolean} options.verbose - Enable verbose logging\n * @param {string|Object} options.selector - CSS selector, ElementHandle, or Playwright Locator\n * @param {boolean} options.scrollIntoView - Scroll into view (default: true)\n * @param {number} options.waitAfterScroll - Wait time after scroll in ms\n * @param {boolean} options.smoothScroll - Use smooth scroll animation\n * @param {number} options.timeout - Timeout in ms\n * @returns {Promise<{locatorOrElement: Object, scrolled: boolean, navigated: boolean}>}\n */\nasync function prepareElement(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    wait,\n    log,\n    verbose = false,\n    selector,\n    scrollIntoView: shouldScroll = true,\n    waitAfterScroll,\n    smoothScroll = true,\n    timeout,\n  } = options;\n\n  // Get locator/element and wait for it to be visible (unified for both engines)\n  const locatorOrElement = await waitForLocatorOrElement({\n    page,\n    engine,\n    selector,\n    timeout,\n  });\n\n  // Log element info if verbose\n  if (verbose) {\n    await logElementInfo({ page, engine, log, locatorOrElement });\n  }\n\n  // Scroll into view (if requested and needed)\n  if (shouldScroll) {\n    const behavior = smoothScroll ? 'smooth' : 'instant';\n    const scrollResult = await scrollIntoViewIfNeeded({\n      page,\n      engine,\n      wait,\n      log,\n      locatorOrElement,\n      behavior,\n      waitAfterScroll,\n      verify: false, // Don't verify scroll here, we verify the overall click\n    });\n    // Check if scroll was aborted due to navigation/stop\n    if (!scrollResult.skipped && !scrollResult.scrolled) {\n      return { locatorOrElement: null, scrolled: false, navigated: true };\n    }\n    return { locatorOrElement, scrolled: true, navigated: false };\n  } else {\n    log.debug(() => `🔍 [VERBOSE] Skipping scroll (scrollIntoView: false)`);\n    return { locatorOrElement, scrolled: false, navigated: false };\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Execute the click operation with verification\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Element or locator to click\n * @param {boolean} options.noAutoScroll - Prevent Playwright's automatic scrolling\n * @param {boolean} options.verify - Whether to verify the click operation\n * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional)\n * @returns {Promise<{clicked: boolean, verified: boolean, reason?: string, navigated: boolean}>}\n */\nasync function executeClick(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    log,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    noAutoScroll = false,\n    verify = true,\n    verifyFn,\n  } = options;\n\n  log.debug(() => `🔍 [VERBOSE] About to click element`);\n\n  const clickResult = await clickElement({\n    page,\n    engine,\n    log,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    noAutoScroll,\n    verify,\n    verifyFn,\n  });\n\n  if (!clickResult.clicked) {\n    // Navigation/stop occurred during click itself\n    return {\n      clicked: false,\n      verified: true,\n      navigated: true,\n      reason: 'navigation during click',\n    };\n  }\n\n  log.debug(() => `🔍 [VERBOSE] Click completed`);\n\n  return {\n    clicked: true,\n    verified: clickResult.verified,\n    navigated: false,\n    reason: clickResult.reason,\n  };\n}\n\n/**\n * Handle navigation detection and waiting after a click\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @param {Object} options.navigationManager - NavigationManager instance (optional)\n * @param {Object} options.networkTracker - NetworkTracker instance (optional)\n * @param {string} options.startUrl - URL before click\n * @param {boolean} options.waitForNavigation - Wait for navigation to complete\n * @param {number} options.navigationCheckDelay - Time to check if navigation started\n * @param {number} options.waitAfterClick - Wait time after click in ms\n * @returns {Promise<{navigated: boolean, verified: boolean, reason: string}>}\n */\nasync function handleNavigationAfterClick(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    wait,\n    log,\n    navigationManager,\n    networkTracker,\n    startUrl,\n    waitForNavigation = true,\n    navigationCheckDelay = 500,\n    waitAfterClick = 1000,\n  } = options;\n\n  // Check if click caused navigation\n  if (waitForNavigation) {\n    // Wait briefly for navigation to potentially start\n    await wait({\n      ms: navigationCheckDelay,\n      reason: 'checking for navigation after click',\n    });\n\n    // Detect if navigation occurred\n    const { navigated, newUrl } = await detectNavigation({\n      page,\n      navigationManager,\n      startUrl,\n      log,\n    });\n\n    if (navigated) {\n      log.debug(() => `🔄 Click triggered navigation to: ${newUrl}`);\n\n      // Wait for page to be fully ready (network idle + no more redirects)\n      // Note: If navigationManager detected external navigation, it's already waiting\n      // We still call waitForPageReady here to ensure we don't return until page is ready\n      if (navigationManager) {\n        // Use longer timeout (120s) for full page loads after click-triggered navigation\n        await navigationManager.waitForPageReady({\n          timeout: 120000,\n          reason: 'after click navigation',\n        });\n      } else if (networkTracker) {\n        // Without navigation manager, use network tracker directly with 30s idle time\n        await networkTracker.waitForNetworkIdle({\n          timeout: 120000,\n          // idleTime defaults to 30000ms from tracker config\n        });\n      } else {\n        // Fallback: wait a bit for page to settle\n        await wait({ ms: 2000, reason: 'page settle after navigation' });\n      }\n\n      // Navigation is considered successful verification\n      return {\n        navigated: true,\n        verified: true,\n        reason: 'click triggered navigation',\n      };\n    }\n  }\n\n  // No navigation - wait after click if specified (useful for modals)\n  if (waitAfterClick > 0) {\n    const waitResult = await wait({\n      ms: waitAfterClick,\n      reason: 'post-click settling time for modal scroll capture',\n    });\n\n    // Check if wait was aborted due to navigation that happened during the wait\n    if (waitResult && waitResult.aborted) {\n      log.debug(\n        () => '🔄 Navigation detected during post-click wait (wait was aborted)'\n      );\n\n      // Re-check for navigation since it happened during the wait\n      const { navigated: lateNavigated, newUrl: lateUrl } =\n        await detectNavigation({\n          page,\n          navigationManager,\n          startUrl,\n          log,\n        });\n\n      if (lateNavigated) {\n        log.debug(() => `🔄 Confirmed late navigation to: ${lateUrl}`);\n\n        // Wait for page to be fully ready\n        if (navigationManager) {\n          await navigationManager.waitForPageReady({\n            timeout: 120000,\n            reason: 'after late-detected click navigation',\n          });\n        }\n\n        return {\n          navigated: true,\n          verified: true,\n          reason: 'late-detected navigation',\n        };\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Final check: did navigation happen while we were processing?\n  // This catches cases where navigation started but wasn't detected earlier\n  if (navigationManager && navigationManager.shouldAbort()) {\n    log.debug(\n      () => '🔄 Navigation detected via abort signal at end of click processing'\n    );\n\n    await navigationManager.waitForPageReady({\n      timeout: 120000,\n      reason: 'after abort-detected click navigation',\n    });\n\n    return {\n      navigated: true,\n      verified: true,\n      reason: 'abort-signal navigation',\n    };\n  }\n\n  // If we have network tracking, wait for any XHR/fetch to complete\n  // Use shorter idle time for non-navigation clicks (just waiting for XHR, not full page load)\n  if (networkTracker) {\n    await networkTracker.waitForNetworkIdle({\n      timeout: 10000, // Maximum wait time\n      idleTime: 2000, // Only 2 seconds of idle needed for XHR completion\n    });\n  }\n\n  return { navigated: false, verified: true, reason: 'no navigation detected' };\n}\n\n/**\n * Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait
