      "content": "import { TIMING } from '../core/constants.js';\nimport { isNavigationError } from '../core/navigation-safety.js';\nimport { waitForLocatorOrElement } from '../elements/locators.js';\nimport { scrollIntoViewIfNeeded } from './scroll.js';\nimport { clickElement } from './click.js';\nimport { getInputValue } from '../elements/content.js';\nimport { createEngineAdapter } from '../core/engine-adapter.js';\n\n/**\n * Default verification function for fill operations.\n * Verifies that the filled text matches expected text.\n * @param {Object} options - Verification options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Element that was filled\n * @param {string} options.expectedText - Text that should be in the element\n * @returns {Promise<{verified: boolean, actualValue: string}>}\n */\nexport async function defaultFillVerification(options = {}) {\n  const { page, engine, locatorOrElement, expectedText } = options;\n\n  try {\n    const actualValue = await getInputValue({ page, engine, locatorOrElement });\n    // Verify that the value contains the expected text (handles cases where value may have formatting)\n    const verified =\n      actualValue === expectedText || actualValue.includes(expectedText);\n    return { verified, actualValue };\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n      return { verified: false, actualValue: '', navigationError: true };\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Verify fill operation with retry logic\n * @param {Object} options - Verification options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Element to verify\n * @param {string} options.expectedText - Expected text value\n * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional, defaults to defaultFillVerification)\n * @param {number} options.timeout - Verification timeout in ms (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT)\n * @param {number} options.retryInterval - Interval between retries (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_RETRY_INTERVAL)\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @returns {Promise<{verified: boolean, actualValue: string, attempts: number}>}\n */\nexport async function verifyFill(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    expectedText,\n    verifyFn = defaultFillVerification,\n    timeout = TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT,\n    retryInterval = TIMING.VERIFICATION_RETRY_INTERVAL,\n    log = { debug: () => {} },\n  } = options;\n\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n  let attempts = 0;\n  let lastResult = { verified: false, actualValue: '' };\n\n  while (Date.now() - startTime < timeout) {\n    attempts++;\n    lastResult = await verifyFn({\n      page,\n      engine,\n      locatorOrElement,\n      expectedText,\n    });\n\n    if (lastResult.verified) {\n      log.debug(\n        () => `✅ Fill verification succeeded after ${attempts} attempt(s)`\n      );\n      return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n    }\n\n    if (lastResult.navigationError) {\n      log.debug(() => '⚠️  Navigation detected during fill verification');\n      return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n    }\n\n    // Wait before next retry\n    await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, retryInterval));\n  }\n\n  log.debug(\n    () =>\n      `❌ Fill verification failed after ${attempts} attempts. Expected: \"${expectedText}\", Got: \"${lastResult.actualValue}\"`\n  );\n  return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if an input element is empty\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Element or locator to check\n * @param {Object} options.adapter - Engine adapter (optional, will be created if not provided)\n * @returns {Promise<boolean>} - True if empty, false if has content (returns true on navigation)\n */\nexport async function checkIfElementEmpty(options = {}) {\n  const { page, engine, locatorOrElement, adapter: providedAdapter } = options;\n\n  if (!locatorOrElement) {\n    throw new Error('locatorOrElement is required in options');\n  }\n\n  // Add defensive check for page parameter\n  if (!page && !providedAdapter) {\n    const availableKeys = Object.keys(options).join(', ');\n    throw new Error(\n      `checkIfElementEmpty: page is required in options when adapter is not provided. Available option keys: [${availableKeys}]. This indicates the 'page' parameter was not passed correctly from the calling function.`\n    );\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const adapter = providedAdapter || createEngineAdapter(page, engine);\n    const currentValue = await adapter.getInputValue(locatorOrElement);\n    return !currentValue || currentValue.trim() === '';\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n      console.log(\n        '⚠️  Navigation detected during checkIfElementEmpty, returning true'\n      );\n      return true;\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Perform fill/type operation on an element (low-level)\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Element or locator to fill\n * @param {string} options.text - Text to fill\n * @param {boolean} options.simulateTyping - Whether to simulate typing (default: true)\n * @param {boolean} options.verify - Whether to verify the fill operation (default: true)\n * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional)\n * @param {number} options.verificationTimeout - Verification timeout in ms (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT)\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance (optional)\n * @param {Object} options.adapter - Engine adapter (optional, will be created if not provided)\n * @returns {Promise<{filled: boolean, verified: boolean, actualValue?: string}>}\n */\nexport async function performFill(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    text,\n    simulateTyping = true,\n    verify = true,\n    verifyFn,\n    verificationTimeout = TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT,\n    log = { debug: () => {} },\n    adapter: providedAdapter,\n  } = options;\n\n  if (!text) {\n    throw new Error('text is required in options');\n  }\n\n  if (!locatorOrElement) {\n    throw new Error('locatorOrElement is required in options');\n  }\n\n  // Add defensive check for page parameter\n  if (!page && !providedAdapter) {\n    const availableKeys = Object.keys(options).join(', ');\n    throw new Error(\n      `performFill: page is required in options when adapter is not provided. Available option keys: [${availableKeys}]. This indicates the 'page' parameter was not passed correctly from the calling function.`\n    );\n  }\n\n  try {\n    const adapter = providedAdapter || createEngineAdapter(page, engine);\n    if (simulateTyping) {\n      await adapter.type(locatorOrElement, text);\n    } else {\n      await adapter.fill(locatorOrElement, text);\n    }\n\n    // Verify fill if requested\n    if (verify) {\n      const verificationResult = await verifyFill({\n        page,\n        engine,\n        locatorOrElement,\n        expectedText: text,\n        verifyFn,\n        timeout: verificationTimeout,\n        log,\n      });\n\n      if (!verificationResult.verified) {\n        log.debug(\n          () =>\n            `⚠️  Fill verification failed: expected \"${text}\", got \"${verificationResult.actualValue}\"`\n        );\n      }\n\n      return {\n        filled: true,\n        verified: verificationResult.verified,\n        actualValue: verificationResult.actualValue,\n      };\n    }\n\n    return { filled: true, verified: true };\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n      console.log(\n        '⚠️  Navigation detected during performFill, recovering gracefully'\n      );\n      return { filled: false, verified: false };\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Fill a textarea with text (high-level with checks and scrolling)\n * @param {Object} options - Whether to verify the fill operation (default: true)\n * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional, uses defaultFillVerification if not provided)\n * @param {number} options.verificationTimeout - Verification timeout in ms (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT)\n * @returns {Promise<{filled: boolean, verified: boolean, skipped: boolean, actualValue?: string}>}\n *   - filled: true if fill operation was attempted\n *   - verified: true if fill was verified successfully (only meaningful if filled is true)\n *   - skipped: true if element already had content and checkEmpty was true\n * @throws {Error} - If selector or text is missing, or if operation fails (except navigation)\n */\nexport async function fillTextArea(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    wait,\n    log,\n    selector,\n    text,\n    checkEmpty = true,\n    scrollIntoView: shouldScroll = true,\n    simulateTyping = true,\n    timeout = TIMING.DEFAULT_TIMEOUT,\n    verify = true,\n    verifyFn,\n    verificationTimeout = TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT,\n  } = options;\n\n  // Defensive check: Validate that page parameter is present\n  if (!page) {\n    const availableKeys = Object.keys(options).join(', ');\n    throw new Error(\n      `fillTextArea: page is required in options. Available option keys: [${availableKeys}]. This indicates the 'page' parameter was not passed correctly from the calling function (bindings layer).`\n    );\n  }\n\n  if (!selector || !text) {\n    throw new Error('fillTextArea: selector and text are required in options');\n  }\n\n  try {\n    // Get locator/element and wait for it to be visible (unified for both engines)\n    const locatorOrElement = await waitForLocatorOrElement({\n      page,\n      engine,\n      selector,\n      timeout,\n    });\n\n    // Check if empty (if requested)\n    if (checkEmpty) {\n      const isEmpty = await checkIfElementEmpty({\n        page,\n        engine,\n        locatorOrElement,\n      });\n      if (!isEmpty) {\n        const currentValue = await getInputValue({\n          page,\n          engine,\n          locatorOrElement,\n        });\n        log.debug(\n          () =>\n            `🔍 [VERBOSE] Textarea already has content, skipping: \"${currentValue.substring(0, 30)}...\"`\n        );\n        return {\n          filled: false,\n          verified: false,\n          skipped: true,\n          actualValue: currentValue,\n        };\n      }\n    }\n\n    // Scroll into view (if requested and needed)\n    if (shouldScroll) {\n      await scrollIntoViewIfNeeded({\n        page,\n        engine,\n        wait,\n        log,\n        locatorOrElement,\n        behavior: 'smooth',\n      });\n    }\n\n    // Click the element (prevent auto-scroll if scrollIntoView is disabled)\n    const clicked = await clickElement({\n      page,\n      engine,\n      log,\n      locatorOrElement,\n      noAutoScroll: !shouldScroll,\n    });\n    if (!clicked) {\n      return { filled: false, verified: false, skipped: false }; margin && rect.bottom > margin;\n  const isInHorizontalView =\n    rect.left < viewportWidth - margin && rect.right > margin;\n\n  return isInVerticalView && isInHorizontalView;\n};\n\n/**\n * Default verification function for scroll operations.\n * Verifies that the element is now visible in the viewport.\n * @param {Object} options - Verification options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Element that was scrolled to\n * @param {number} options.margin - Margin in pixels to consider element visible (default: 50)\n * @returns {Promise<{verified: boolean, inViewport: boolean}>}\n */\nexport async function defaultScrollVerification(options = {}) {\n  const { page, engine, locatorOrElement, margin = 50 } = options;\n\n  try {\n    let inViewport;\n    if (engine === 'playwright') {\n      inViewport = await locatorOrElement.evaluate(\n        isElementInViewportFn,\n        margin\n      );\n    } else {\n      inViewport = await page.evaluate(\n        isElementInViewportFn,\n        locatorOrElement,\n        margin\n      );\n    }\n    return { verified: inViewport, inViewport };\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error) || isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n      return { verified: false, inViewport: false, navigationError: true };\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Verify scroll operation with retry logic\n * @param {Object} options - Verification options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Element to verify\n * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional, defaults to defaultScrollVerification)\n * @param {number} options.timeout - Verification timeout in ms (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT)\n * @param {number} options.retryInterval - Interval between retries (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_RETRY_INTERVAL)\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @returns {Promise<{verified: boolean, inViewport: boolean, attempts: number}>}\n */\nexport async function verifyScroll(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    verifyFn = defaultScrollVerification,\n    timeout = TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT,\n    retryInterval = TIMING.VERIFICATION_RETRY_INTERVAL,\n    log = { debug: () => {} },\n  } = options;\n\n  const startTime = Date.now();\n  let attempts = 0;\n  let lastResult = { verified: false, inViewport: false };\n\n  while (Date.now() - startTime < timeout) {\n    attempts++;\n    lastResult = await verifyFn({\n      page,\n      engine,\n      locatorOrElement,\n    });\n\n    if (lastResult.verified) {\n      log.debug(\n        () => `✅ Scroll verification succeeded after ${attempts} attempt(s)`\n      );\n      return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n    }\n\n    if (lastResult.navigationError) {\n      log.debug(\n        () => '⚠️  Navigation/stop detected during scroll verification'\n      );\n      return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n    }\n\n    // Wait before next retry\n    await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, retryInterval));\n  }\n\n  log.debug(\n    () =>\n      `❌ Scroll verification failed after ${attempts} attempts - element not in viewport`\n  );\n  return { ...lastResult, attempts };\n}\n\n/**\n * Scroll element into view (low-level, does not check if scroll is needed)\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Playwright locator or Puppeteer element\n * @param {string} options.behavior - 'smooth' or 'instant' (default: 'smooth')\n * @param {boolean} options.verify - Whether to verify the scroll operation (default: true)\n * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional)\n * @param {number} options.verificationTimeout - Verification timeout in ms (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT)\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance (optional)\n * @returns {Promise<{scrolled: boolean, verified: boolean}>}\n */\nexport async function scrollIntoView(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    behavior = 'smooth',\n    verify = true,\n    verifyFn,\n    verificationTimeout = TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT,\n    log = { debug: () => {} },\n  } = options;\n\n  if (!locatorOrElement) {\n    throw new Error('locatorOrElement is required in options');\n  }\n\n  try {\n    if (engine === 'playwright') {\n      await locatorOrElement.evaluate((el, scrollBehavior) => {\n        el.scrollIntoView({\n          behavior: scrollBehavior,\n          block: 'center',\n          inline: 'center',\n        });\n      }, behavior);\n    } else {\n      await page.evaluate(\n        (el, scrollBehavior) => {\n          el.scrollIntoView({\n            behavior: scrollBehavior,\n            block: 'center',\n            inline: 'center',\n          });\n        },\n        locatorOrElement,\n        behavior\n      );\n    }\n\n    // Verify scroll if requested\n    if (verify) {\n      const verificationResult = await verifyScroll({\n        page,\n        engine,\n        locatorOrElement,\n        verifyFn,\n        timeout: verificationTimeout,\n        log,\n      });\n\n      return {\n        scrolled: true,\n        verified: verificationResult.verified,\n      };\n    }\n\n    return { scrolled: true, verified: true };\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error) || isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n      console.log(\n        '⚠️  Navigation/stop detected during scrollIntoView, skipping'\n      );\n      return { scrolled: false, verified: false };\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Check if element needs scrolling (is it more than threshold% away from viewport center)\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Playwright locator or Puppeteer element\n * @param {number} options.threshold - Percentage of viewport height to consider \"significant\" (default: 10)\n * @returns {Promise<boolean>} - True if scroll is needed, false on navigation/stop\n */\nexport async function needsScrolling(options = {}) {\n  const { page, engine, locatorOrElement, threshold = 10 } = options;\n\n  if (!locatorOrElement) {\n    throw new Error('locatorOrElement is required in options');\n  }\n\n  try {\n    if (engine === 'playwright') {\n      return await locatorOrElement.evaluate(needsScrollingFn, threshold);\n    } else {\n      return await page.evaluate(needsScrollingFn, locatorOrElement, threshold);\n    }\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error) || isActionStoppedError(error)) {\n      console.log(\n        '⚠️  Navigation/stop detected during needsScrolling, returning false'\n      );\n      return false;\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Scroll element into view only if needed (>threshold% from center)\n * Automatically waits for scroll animation if scroll was performed\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type\n * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @param {Object} options.locatorOrElement - Playwright locator or Puppeteer element\n * @param {string} options.behavior - 'smooth' or 'instant' (default: 'smooth')\n * @param {number} options.threshold - Percentage of viewport height to consider \"significant\" (default: 10)\n * @param {number} options.waitAfterScroll - Wait time after scroll in ms (default: TIMING.SCROLL_ANIMATION_WAIT for smooth, 0 for instant)\n * @param {boolean} options.verify - Whether to verify the scroll operation (default: true)\n * @param {Function} options.verifyFn - Custom verification function (optional)\n * @param {number} options.verificationTimeout - Verification timeout in ms (default: TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT)\n * @returns {Promise<{scrolled: boolean, verified: boolean, skipped: boolean}>}\n *   - scrolled: true if scroll was performed\n *   - verified: true if element is confirmed in viewport (only meaningful if scrolled is true)\n *   - skipped: true if element was already in view\n */\nexport async function scrollIntoViewIfNeeded(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    wait,\n    log,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    behavior = 'smooth',\n    threshold = 10,\n    waitAfterScroll = behavior === 'smooth' ? TIMING.SCROLL_ANIMATION_WAIT : 0,\n    verify = true,\n    verifyFn,\n    verificationTimeout = TIMING.VERIFICATION_TIMEOUT,\n  } = options;\n\n  if (!locatorOrElement) {\n    throw new Error('locatorOrElement is required in options');\n  }\n\n  // Check if scrolling is needed\n  const needsScroll = await needsScrolling({\n    page,\n    engine,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    threshold,\n  });\n\n  if (!needsScroll) {\n    log.debug(\n      () =>\n        `🔍 [VERBOSE] Element already in view (within ${threshold}% threshold), skipping scroll`\n    );\n    return { scrolled: false, verified: true, skipped: true };\n  }\n\n  // Perform scroll with verification\n  log.debug(() => `🔍 [VERBOSE] Scrolling with behavior: ${behavior}`);\n  const scrollResult = await scrollIntoView({\n    page,\n    engine,\n    locatorOrElement,\n    behavior,\n    verify,\n    verifyFn,\n    verificationTimeout,\n    log,\n  });\n\n  if (!scrollResult.scrolled) {\n    // Navigation/stop occurred during scroll\n    return { scrolled: false, verified: false, skipped: false };\n  }\n\n  // Wait for scroll animation if specified\n  if (waitAfterScroll > 0) {\n    await wait({\n      ms: waitAfterScroll,\n      reason: `${behavior} scroll animation to complete`,\n    });\n  }\n\n  if (scrollResult.verified) {\n    log.debug(() => '✅ Scroll verification passed - element is in viewport');\n  } else {\n    log.debug(\n      () =>\n        '⚠️  Scroll verification failed - element may not be fully in viewport'\n    );\n  }\n\n  return {\n    scrolled: true,\n    verified: scrollResult.verified,\n    skipped: false,\n  };\n}\n",

expected_text=text,\n                verify_fn=verify_fn,\n                timeout=verification_timeout,\n                log=log,\n            )\n\n            if not verification_result.verified and )\n\n            return FillResult(\n                filled=True,\n                verified=verification_result.verified,\n                actual_value=verification_result.actual_value,\n            )\n\n        return FillResult(filled=True, verified=True)\n\n    except Exception as error:\n        if is_navigation_error(error):\n            print(\"Navigation detected during perform_fill,    )\n            if not is_empty:\n                current_value = await get_input_value(\n                    page=page,\n                    engine=engine,\n                    locator_or_element=locator_or_element,\n                )\n                log.debug(\n                    lambda: f'Textarea already has content, skipping: '\n              actual_value=current_value,\n                )\n\n        # Scroll into view (if requested and needed)\n        if scroll_into_view:\n            await scroll_into_view_if_needed(\n   "uuid": "7c73ef1b-2274-486c-9878-f0cd7cac8932",
  "tool_use_result": {
    "type": "text",
    "file": {
      "filePath": "/tmp/gh-issue-solver-1768327375795/js/src/utilities/wait.js",
      "content": "import { isNavigationError } from '../core/navigation-safety.js';\nimport { createEngineAdapter } from '../core/engine-adapter.js';\n\n/**\n * Wait/sleep for a specified time with optional verbose logging\n * Now supports abort signals to interrupt the wait when navigation occurs\n *\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Function} options.log - Logger instance\n * @param {number} options.ms - Milliseconds to wait\n * @param {string} options.reason - Reason for waiting (for verbose logging)\n * @param {AbortSignal} options.abortSignal - Optional abort signal to interrupt wait\n * @returns {Promise<{completed: boolean, aborted: boolean}>}\n */\nexport async function wait(options = {}) {\n  const { log, ms, reason, abortSignal } = options;\n\n  if (!ms) {\n    throw new Error('ms is required in options');\n  }\n\n  if (reason) {\n    log.debug(() => `🔍 [VERBOSE] Waiting ${ms}ms: ${reason}`);\n  }\n\n  // If abort signal provided, use abortable wait\n  if (abortSignal) {\n    // Check if already aborted\n    if (abortSignal.aborted) {\n      log.debug(\n        () => `🛑 Wait skipped (already aborted): ${reason || 'no reason'}`\n      );\n      return { completed: false, aborted: true };\n    }\n\n    return new Promise((resolve) => {\n      let timeoutId = null;\n      let abortHandler = null;\n\n      const cleanup = () => {\n        if (timeoutId) {\n          clearTimeout(timeoutId);\n        }\n        if (abortHandler) {\n          abortSignal.removeEventListener('abort', abortHandler);\n        }\n      };\n\n      abortHandler = () => {\n        cleanup();\n        log.debug(() => `🛑 Wait aborted: ${reason || 'no reason'}`);\n        resolve({ completed: false, aborted: true });\n      };\n\n      abortSignal.addEventListener('abort', abortHandler);\n\n      timeoutId = setTimeout(() => {\n        cleanup();\n        if (reason) {\n          log.debug(() => `🔍 [VERBOSE] Wait complete (${ms}ms)`);\n        }\n        resolve({ completed: true, aborted: false });\n      }, ms);\n    });\n  }\n\n  // Standard non-abortable wait (backwards compatible)\n  await new Promise((r) => setTimeout(r, ms));\n\n  if (reason) {\n    log.debug(() => `🔍 [VERBOSE] Wait complete (${ms}ms)`);\n  }\n\n  return { completed: true, aborted: false };\n}\n\n/**\n * Evaluate JavaScript in page context\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Function} options.fn - Function to evaluate\n * @param {Array} options.args - Arguments to pass to function (default: [])\n * @param {Object} options.adapter - Engine adapter (optional, will be created if not provided)\n * @returns {Promise<any>} - Result of evaluation\n */\nexport async function evaluate(options = {}) {\n  const { page, engine, fn, args = [], adapter: providedAdapter } = options;\n\n  if (!fn) {\n    throw new Error('fn is required in options');\n  }\n\n  const adapter = providedAdapter || createEngineAdapter(page, engine);\n  return await adapter.evaluateOnPage(fn, args);\n}\n\n/**\n * Safe evaluate that catches navigation errors and returns default value\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Object} options.page - Browser page object\n * @param {string} options.engine - Engine type ('playwright' or 'puppeteer')\n * @param {Function} options.fn - Function to evaluate\n * @param {Array} options.args - Arguments to pass to function (default: [])\n * @param {any} options.defaultValue - Value to return on navigation error (default: null)\n * @param {string} options.operationName - Name for logging (default: 'evaluate')\n * @param {boolean} options.silent - Don't log warnings (default: false)\n * @returns {Promise<{success: boolean, value: any, navigationError: boolean}>}\n */\nexport async function safeEvaluate(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    page,\n    engine,\n    fn,\n    args = [],\n    defaultValue = null,\n    operationName = 'evaluate',\n    silent = false,\n  } = options;\n\n  try {\n    const value = await evaluate({ page, engine, fn, args });\n    return { success: true, value, navigationError: false };\n  } catch (error) {\n    if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n      if (!silent) {\n        console.log(\n          `⚠️  Navigation detected during ${operationName}, recovering gracefully`\n        );\n      }\n      return { success: false, value: defaultValue, navigationError: true };\n    }\n    throw error;\n  }\n}\n",
      "numLines": 136,
      "startLine": 1,
      "totalLines": 136
    }
  }
}

import dataclass\nfrom typing import Any, Callable\n\nfrom browser_commander.core.engine_adapter import create_engine_adapter\nfrom browser_commander.core.engine_detection import EngineType\nfrom browser_commander.core.logger import Logger\nfrom browser_commander.core.navigation_safety import is_navigation_error\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass WaitResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of wait operation.\"\"\"\n\n    completed: bool\n    aborted: bool\n\n\n@dataclass\nclass EvaluateResult:\n    \"\"\"Result of safe evaluate operation.\"\"\"\n\n    success: bool\n    value: Any\n    navigation_error: bool\n\n\nasync def wait(\n    log: Logger,\n    ms: int,\n    reason: str | None = None,\n    abort_signal: asyncio.Event | None = None,\n) -> WaitResult:\n    \"\"\"Wait/sleep for a specified time with optional verbose logging.\n\n    Now supports abort signals to interrupt the wait when navigation occurs.\n\n    Args:\n        log: Logger instance\n        ms: Milliseconds to wait\n        reason: Reason for waiting (for verbose logging)\n        abort_signal: Optional asyncio.Event to interrupt wait\n\n    Returns:\n        WaitResult with completed and aborted flags\n    \"\"\"\n    if not ms:\n        raise ValueError(\"ms is required\")\n\n    if reason:\n        log.debug(lambda: f\"Waiting {ms}ms: {reason}\")\n\n    # If abort signal provided, use abortable wait\n    if abort_signal:\n        # Check if already aborted\n        if abort_signal.is_set():\n            log.debug(lambda: f'Wait skipped (already aborted): {reason or \"no reason\"}')\n            return WaitResult(completed=False, aborted=True)\n\n        try:\n            # Wait for either the timeout or the abort signal\n            await asyncio.wait_for(\n                abort_signal.wait(),\n                timeout=ms / 1000,\n            )\n            # If we get here, abort was signaled\n            log.debug(lambda: f'Wait aborted: {reason or \"no reason\"}')\n            return WaitResult(completed=False, aborted=True)\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError:\n            # Timeout means wait completed normally\n            if reason:\n                log.debug(lambda: f\"Wait complete ({ms}ms)\")\n            return WaitResult(completed=True, aborted=False)\n\n    # Standard non-abortable wait (backwards compatible)\n    await asyncio.sleep(ms / 1000)\n\n    if reason:\n        log.debug(lambda: f\"Wait complete ({ms}ms)\")\n\n    return WaitResult(completed=True, aborted=False)\n\n\nasync def evaluate(\n    page: Any,\n    engine: EngineType,\n    fn: str,\n    args: list | None = None,\n    adapter: Any | None = None,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"Evaluate JavaScript in page context.\n\n    Args:\n        page: Browser page object\n        engine: Engine type ('playwright' or 'selenium')\n        fn: JavaScript function string to evaluate\n        args: Arguments to pass to function (default: [])\n        adapter: Engine adapter (optional, will be created if not provided)\n\n    Returns:\n        Result of evaluation\n    \"\"\"\n    if args is None:\n        args = []\n\n    if not fn:\n        raise ValueError(\"fn is required\")\n\n    if adapter is None:\n        adapter = create_engine_adapter(page, engine)\n\n    return await adapter.evaluate_on_page(fn, args)\n\n\nasync def safe_evaluate(\n    page: Any,\n    engine: EngineType,\n    fn: str,\n    args: list | None = None,\n    default_value: Any = None,\n    operation_name: str = \"evaluate\",\n    silent: bool = False,\n) -> EvaluateResult:\n    \"\"\"Safe evaluate that catches navigation errors and returns default value.\n\n    Args:\n        page: Browser page object\n        engine: Engine type ('playwright' or 'selenium')\n        fn: JavaScript function string to evaluate\n        args: Arguments to pass to function (default: [])\n        default_value: Value to return on navigation error (default: None)\n        operation_name: Name for logging (default: 'evaluate')\n        silent: Don't log warnings (default: False)\n\n    Returns:\n        EvaluateResult with success, value, and navigation_error flags\n    \"\"\"\n    if args is None:\n        args = []\n\n    try:\n        value = await evaluate(page=page, engine=engine, fn=fn, args=args)\n        return EvaluateResult(success=True, value=value, navigation_error=False)\n    except Exception as error:\n        if is_navigation_error(error):\n            if not silent:\n                print(\n                    f\"Navigation detected during {operation_name}, recovering gracefully\"\n                )\n            return EvaluateResult(\n                success=False,\n                value=default_value,\n                navigation_error=True,\n            )\n        raise\n"
        }

      "content": "/**\n * Universal high-level functions following DRY principles\n * These are pure functions that work with any browser automation engine\n */\n\nimport {\n  isNavigationError,\n  withNavigationSafety,\n} from '../core/navigation-safety.js';\n\n/**\n * Wait indefinitely for a URL condition with custom check function\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Function} options.getUrl - Function to get current URL\n * @param {Function} options.wait - Wait function\n * @param {Function} options.evaluate - Evaluate function\n * @param {string} options.targetUrl - Target URL to wait for\n * @param {string} options.description - Description for logging\n * @param {Function} options.customCheck - Optional custom check function (async)\n * @param {Function} options.pageClosedCallback - Callback to check if page closed\n * @param {number} options.pollingInterval - Polling interval in ms (default: 1000)\n * @returns {Promise<any>} - Result from customCheck or true if URL matched\n */\nexport async function waitForUrlCondition(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    getUrl,\n    wait,\n    evaluate,\n    targetUrl,\n    description,\n    customCheck,\n    pageClosedCallback = () => false,\n    pollingInterval = 1000,\n  } = options;\n\n  if (description) {\n    console.log(`⏳ ${description}...`);\n  }\n\n  while (true) {\n    if (pageClosedCallback()) {\n      return null;\n    }\n\n    try {\n      // Run custom check if provided\n      if (customCheck) {\n        const customResult = await customCheck(getUrl());\n        if (customResult !== undefined && customResult !== null) {\n          return customResult;\n        }\n      }\n\n      // Check if target URL reached\n      const currentUrl = getUrl();\n      if (currentUrl.startsWith(targetUrl)) {\n        return true;\n      }\n    } catch (error) {\n      if (pageClosedCallback()) {\n        return null;\n      }\n\n      // Handle navigation errors gracefully\n      if (isNavigationError(error)) {\n        console.log(\n          '⚠️  Navigation detected during URL check, continuing to wait...'\n        );\n      } else {\n        console.log(\n          `⚠️  Temporary error while checking URL: ${error.message.substring(0, 100)}... (retrying)`\n        );\n      }\n    }\n\n    await wait({\n      ms: pollingInterval,\n      reason: 'polling interval before next URL check',\n    });\n  }\n}\n\n/**\n * Install click detection listener on page\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Function} options.evaluate - Evaluate function\n * @param {string} options.buttonText - Text to detect\n * @param {string} options.storageKey - SessionStorage key to set\n * @returns {Promise<boolean>} - True if installed, false on navigation\n */\nexport async function installClickListener(options = {}) {\n  const { evaluate, buttonText, storageKey } = options;\n\n  const safeEvaluate = withNavigationSafety(evaluate, {\n    onNavigationError: () => {\n      console.log(\n        '⚠️  Navigation detected during installClickListener, skipping'\n      );\n      return false;\n    },\n  });\n\n  const result = await safeEvaluate({\n    fn: (text, key) => {\n      document.addEventListener(\n        'click',\n        (event) => {\n          let element = event.target;\n          while (element && element !== document.body) {\n            const elementText = element.textContent?.trim() || '';\n            if (\n              elementText === text ||\n              ((element.tagName === 'A' || element.tagName === 'BUTTON') &&\n                elementText.includes(text))\n            ) {\n              console.log(`[Click Listener] Detected click on ${text} button!`);\n              window.sessionStorage.setItem(key, 'true');\n              break;\n            }\n            element = element.parentElement;\n          }\n        },\n        true\n      );\n    },\n    args: [buttonText, storageKey],\n  });\n\n  return result === false ? false : true;\n}\n\n/**\n * Check and clear session storage flag\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Function} options.evaluate - Evaluate function\n * @param {string} options.storageKey - SessionStorage key\n * @returns {Promise<boolean>} - True if flag was set, false otherwise or on navigation\n */\nexport async function checkAndClearFlag(options = {}) {\n  const { evaluate, storageKey } = options;\n\n  const safeEvaluate = withNavigationSafety(evaluate, {\n    onNavigationError: () => {\n      console.log(\n        '⚠️  Navigation detected during checkAndClearFlag, returning false'\n      );\n      return false;\n    },\n  });\n\n  return await safeEvaluate({\n    fn: (key) => {\n      const flag = window.sessionStorage.getItem(key);\n      if (flag === 'true') {\n        window.sessionStorage.removeItem(key);\n        return true;\n      }\n      return false;\n    },\n    args: [storageKey],\n  });\n}\n\n/**\n * Find toggle button using multiple strategies\n * @param {Object} options - Configuration options\n * @param {Function} options.count - Count function\n * @param {Function} options.findByText - FindByText function\n * @param {Array<string>} options.dataQaSelectors - Data-qa selectors to try\n * @param {string} options.textToFind - Text to search for\n * @param {Array<string>} options.elementTypes - Element types to search\n * @returns {Promise<string|null>} - Selector or null\n */\nexport async function findToggleButton(options = {}) {\n  const {\n    count,\n    findByText,\n    dataQaSelectors = [],\n    textToFind,\n    elementTypes = ['button', 'a', 'span'],\n  } = options;\n\n  // Try data-qa selectors first\n  for (const sel of dataQaSelectors) {\n    const elemCount = await count({ selector: sel });\n    if (elemCount > 0) {\n      return sel;\n    }\n  }\n\n  // Fallback to text search\n  if (textToFind) {\n    for (const elementType of elementTypes) {\n      const selector = await findByText({\n        text: textToFind,\n        selector: elementType,\n      });\n      const elemCount = await count({ selector });\n      if (elemCount > 0) {\n        return selector;\n      }\n    }\n  }\n\n  return null;\n}\n",

    }

          "content": "\"\"\"Universal high-level functions following DRY principles.\n\nThese are pure functions that work with any browser automation engine.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom typing import Any, Callable\n\nfrom browser_commander.core.navigation_safety import (\n    is_navigation_error,\n    with_navigation_safety,\n)\n\n\nasync def wait_for_url_condition(\n    get_url: Callable[[], str],\n    wait_fn: Callable[[int, str], Any],\n    evaluate_fn: Callable[[str, list], Any],\n    target_url: str,\n    description: str | None = None,\n    custom_check: Callable[[str], Any] | None = None,\n    page_closed_callback: Callable[[], bool] | None = None,\n    polling_interval: int = 1000,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"Wait indefinitely for a URL condition with custom check function.\n\n    Args:\n        get_url: Function to get current URL\n        wait_fn: Wait function (ms, reason) -> Any\n        evaluate_fn: Evaluate function (fn, args) -> Any\n        target_url: Target URL to wait for\n        description: Description for logging\n        custom_check: Optional custom check function (async)\n        page_closed_callback: Callback to check if page closed\n        polling_interval: Polling interval in ms (default: 1000)\n\n    Returns:\n        Result from custom_check or True if URL matched\n    \"\"\"\n    if page_closed_callback is None:\n        page_closed_callback = lambda: False\n\n    if description:\n        print(f\"Waiting: {description}...\")\n\n    while True:\n        if page_closed_callback():\n            return None\n\n        try:\n            # Run custom check if provided\n            if custom_check:\n                custom_result = await custom_check(get_url())\n                if custom_result is not None:\n                    return custom_result\n\n            # Check if target URL reached\n            current_url = get_url()\n            if current_url.startswith(target_url):\n                return True\n\n        except Exception as error:\n            if page_closed_callback():\n                return None\n\n            # Handle navigation errors gracefully\n            if is_navigation_error(error):\n                print(\"Navigation detected during URL check, continuing to wait...\")\n            else:\n                error_msg = str(error)[:100]\n                print(\n                    f\"Temporary error while checking URL: {error_msg}... (retrying)\"\n                )\n\n        await wait_fn(polling_interval, \"polling interval before next URL check\")\n\n\nasync def install_click_listener(\n    evaluate_fn: Callable[[str, list], Any],\n    button_text: str,\n    storage_key: str,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Install click detection listener on page.\n\n    Args:\n        evaluate_fn: Evaluate function (fn, args) -> Any\n        button_text: Text to detect\n        storage_key: SessionStorage key to set\n\n    Returns:\n        True if installed, False on navigation\n    \"\"\"\n    js_code = \"\"\"\n    (text, key) => {\n        document.addEventListener('click', (event) => {\n            let element = event.target;\n            while (element && element !== document.body) {\n                const elementText = element.textContent?.trim() || '';\n                if (elementText === text ||\n                    ((element.tagName === 'A' || element.tagName === 'BUTTON') &&\n                      elementText.includes(text))) {\n                    console.log(`[Click Listener] Detected click on ${text} button!`);\n                    window.sessionStorage.setItem(key, 'true');\n                    break;\n                }\n                element = element.parentElement;\n            }\n        }, true);\n    }\n    \"\"\"\n\n    safe_evaluate = with_navigation_safety(\n        evaluate_fn,\n        on_navigation_error=lambda: print(\n            \"Navigation detected during install_click_listener, skipping\"\n        ),\n    )\n\n    try:\n        result = await safe_evaluate(js_code, [button_text, storage_key])\n        return result is not False\n    except Exception:\n        return False\n\n\nasync def check_and_clear_flag(\n    evaluate_fn: Callable[[str, list], Any],\n    storage_key: str,\n) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check and clear session storage flag.\n\n    Args:\n        evaluate_fn: Evaluate function (fn, args) -> Any\n        storage_key: SessionStorage key\n\n    Returns:\n        True if flag was set, False otherwise or on navigation\n    \"\"\"\n    js_code = \"\"\"\n    (key) => {\n        const flag = window.sessionStorage.getItem(key);\n        if (flag === 'true') {\n            window.sessionStorage.removeItem(key);\n            return true;\n        }\n        return false;\n    }\n    \"\"\"\n\n    safe_evaluate = with_navigation_safety(\n        evaluate_fn,\n        on_navigation_error=lambda: print(\n            \"Navigation detected during check_and_clear_flag, returning False\"\n        ),\n    )\n\n    try:\n        return await safe_evaluate(js_code, [storage_key])\n    except Exception:\n        return False\n\n\nasync def find_toggle_button(\n    count_fn: Callable[[str], int],\n    find_by_text_fn: Callable[[str, str], str],\n    data_qa_selectors: list[str] | None = None,\n    text_to_find: str | None = None,\n    element_types: list[str] | None = None,\n) -> str | None:\n    \"\"\"Find toggle button using multiple strategies.\n\n    Args:\n        count_fn: Count function (selector) -> int\n        find_by_text_fn: FindByText function (text, selector) -> str\n        data_qa_selectors: Data-qa selectors to try\n        text_to_find: Text to search for\n        element_types: Element types to search\n\n    Returns:\n        Selector or None\n    \"\"\"\n    if data_qa_selectors is None:\n        data_qa_selectors = []\n    if element_types is None:\n        element_types = [\"button\", \"a\", \"span\"]\n\n    # Try data-qa selectors first\n    for sel in data_qa_selectors:\n        elem_count = await count_fn(sel)\n        if elem_count > 0:\n            return sel\n\n    # Fallback to text search\n    if text_to_find:\n        for element_type in element_types:\n            selector = await find_by_text_fn(text_to_find, element_type)\n            elem_count = await count_fn(selector)\n            if elem_count > 0:\n                return selector\n\n    return None\n"
        }
      }

\"navigation_error\": result.navigation_error,\n        }\n\n    # ==================== URL Functions ====================\n    def get_url(self) -> str:\n        \"\"\"Get current URL.\"\"\"\n        from browser_commander.utilities.url import get_url\n\n        return get_url(self.page)\n\n    async def unfocus_address_bar(self) -> None:\n        \"\"\"Unfocus address bar.\"\"\"\n        from browser_commander.utilities.url import unfocus_address_bar\n\n        await unfocus_address_bar(self.page)\n\n    # ==================== Navigation Functions ====================\n    async def {\n      if (expectedError && !error.message.includes(expectedError)) {\n        throw new Error(\n          `${message}: Expected error message to include \"${expectedError}\", got \"${error.message}\"`\n        );\n      }\n    }\n  },\n  doesNotThrow: async (fn, message = '') => {\n    try {\n      await fn();\n    } catch (error) {\n      throw new Error(\n        `${message}: Expected function not to throw, but got: ${error.message}`\n      );\n    }\n  },\n};\n","numLines":530,"startLine":1,"totalLines":530}}}

patch\n          - minor\n          - major\n      description:\n        description: 'Release description (optional)'\n        required: false\n        type: string\n\nconcurrency:\n  group: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref }}\n  cancel-in-progress: true\n\njobs:\n  # === DETECT CHANGES - determines which jobs should run ===\n  detect-changes:\n    name: Detect Changes\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    if: github.event_name != 'workflow_dispatch'\n    outputs:\n      py-changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.py-changed }}\n      tests-changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.tests-changed }}\n      package-changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.package-changed }}\n      docs-changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.docs-changed }}\n      workflow-changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.workflow-changed }}\n      any-code-changed: ${{ steps.changes.outputs.any-code-changed }}\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Detect changes\n        id: changes\n        env:\n          GITHUB_EVENT_NAME: ${{ github.event_name }}\n          GITHUB_BASE_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}\n          GITHUB_HEAD_SHA: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\n        run: python scripts/detect_code_changes.py\n\n  # REQUIRED CI CHECKS - All must pass before release\n  # These jobs ensure code quality and tests pass before any release\n\n  # === LINT AND FORMAT CHECK ===\n  # Lint runs independently of changelog check - it's a fast check that should always run\n  # See: https://github.com/link-assistant/hive-mind/pull/1024 for why this dependency was removed\n  lint:\n    name: Lint and Format Check\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    if: |\n      github.event_name == 'push' ||\n      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.py-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.tests-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.docs-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.package-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true'\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install -e \".[dev]\"\n\n      - name: Run Ruff linting\n        run: ruff check .\n\n      - name: Check Ruff formatting\n        run: ruff format --check .\n\n      - name: Run mypy\n        run: mypy src\n\n      - name: Check file size limit\n        run: python scripts/check_file_size.py\n\n  # === TEST ===\n  # Test on latest Python version only\n  test:\n    name: Test (Python 3.13)\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    if: |\n      github.event_name == 'push' ||\n      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.py-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.tests-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.package-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true'\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install -e \".[dev]\"\n\n      - name: Run tests\n        run: pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-report=xml --cov-report=term\n\n      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov\n        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4\n        with:\n          file: ./coverage.xml\n          fail_ci_if_error: false\n\n  # === BUILD PACKAGE ===\n  # Build package - runs if lint and test pass, or were skipped (docs-only PR)\n  build:\n    name: Build Package\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes, lint, test]\n    # Run if: push/dispatch event, OR lint/test succeeded, OR lint/test were skipped (docs-only PR)\n    if: |\n      always() && (\n        github.event_name == 'push' ||\n        github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||\n        (\n          (needs.lint.result == 'success' || needs.lint.result == 'skipped') &&\n          (needs.test.result == 'success' || needs.test.result == 'skipped')\n        )\n      )\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install build dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install build twine\n\n      - name: Build package\n        run: python -m build\n\n      - name: Check package\n        run: twine check dist/*\n\n      - name: Upload artifacts\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4\n        with:\n          name: dist\n          path: dist/\n\n  # === CHANGELOG CHECK - only runs on PRs with code changes ===\n  # Docs-only PRs (./docs folder, markdown files) don't require changelog fragments\n  changelog:\n    name: Changelog Fragment Check\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    if: github.event_name == 'pull_request' && needs.detect-changes.outputs.any-code-changed == 'true'\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Set up Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: \"3.13\"\n\n      - name: Install scriv\n        run: pip install \"scriv[toml]\"\n\n      - name: Check for changelog fragments\n        run: |\n          # Get list of fragment files (excluding README and template)\n          FRAGMENTS=$(find changelog.d -name \"*.md\" ! -name \"README.md\" ! -name \"*.j2\" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)\n\n          # Get changed files in PR\n          CHANGED_FILES=$(git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD)\n\n          # Check if any source files changed (excluding docs and config)\n          SOURCE_CHANGED=$(echo \"$CHANGED_FILES\" | grep -E \"^(src/|tests/|scripts/)\" | wc -l)\n\n          if [ \"$SOURCE_CHANGED\" -gt 0 ] && [ \"$FRAGMENTS\" -eq 0 ]; then\n            echo \"::warning::No changelog fragment found. Please run 'scriv create' and document your changes.\"\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"To create a changelog fragment:\"\n            echo \"  pip install 'scriv[toml]'\"\n            echo \"  scriv create\"\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"This is similar to adding a changeset in JavaScript projects.\"\n            echo \"See changelog.d/README.md for more information.\"\n            # Note: This is a warning, not a failure, to allow flexibility\n            # Change 'exit 0' to 'exit 1' to make it required\n            exit 0\n          fi\n\n          echo \"✓ Changelog check passed\"\n\n  # RELEASE JOBS - Only run after all CI checks pass\n\n  # Automatic release on push to main (if version changed)\n  auto-release:\n    name: Auto Release\n    needs: [lint, test, build]\n    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install build twine\n\n      - name: Check if version changed\n        id: version_check\n        run: |\n          # Get current version from pyproject.toml\n          CURRENT_VERSION=$(grep -Po '(?<=^version = \")[^\"]*' pyproject.toml)\n          echo \"current_version=$CURRENT_VERSION\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n\n          # Check if tag exists\n          if git rev-parse \"v$CURRENT_VERSION\" >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n            echo \"Tag v$CURRENT_VERSION already exists, skipping release\"\n            echo \"should_release=false\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          else\n            echo \"New version detected: $CURRENT_VERSION\"\n            echo \"should_release=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          fi\n\n      - name: Download artifacts\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4\n        with:\n          name: dist\n          path: dist/\n\n      - name: Publish to PyPI\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          python scripts/create_github_release.py \\\n            --version \"${{ steps.version_check.outputs.current_version }}\" \\\n            --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\"\n\n  # Manual release via workflow_dispatch - only after CI passes\n  manual-release:\n    name: Manual Release\n    needs: [lint, test, build]\n    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}",

then\n            echo \"Tag v$CURRENT_VERSION already exists, skipping release\"\n            echo \"should_release=false\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          else\n            echo \"New version detected: $CURRENT_VERSION\"\n            echo \"should_release=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          fi\n\n      - name: Download artifacts\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4\n        with:\n          name: dist\n          path: dist/\n\n      - name: Publish to PyPI\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          python scripts/create_github_release.py \\\n            --version \"${{ steps.version_check.outputs.current_version }}\" \\\n            --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\"\n\n  # Manual release via workflow_dispatch - only after CI passes\n  manual-release:\n    name: Manual Release\n    needs: [lint, test, build]\n    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}",

    "isImage": false

name: Check for changelog fragments\n        run: |\n          # Get list of fragment files (excluding README and template)\n          FRAGMENTS=$(find changelog.d -name \"*.md\" ! -name \"README.md\" ! -name \"*.j2\" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)\n\n          # Get changed files in PR\n          CHANGED_FILES=$(git diff --name-only origin/${{ github.base_ref }}...HEAD)\n\n          # Check if any source files changed (excluding docs and config)\n          SOURCE_CHANGED=$(echo \"$CHANGED_FILES\" | grep -E \"^python/(src/|tests/|scripts/)\" | wc -l)\n\n          if [ \"$SOURCE_CHANGED\" -gt 0 ] && [ \"$FRAGMENTS\" -eq 0 ]; then\n            echo \"::warning::No changelog fragment found. Please run 'scriv create' and document your changes.\"\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"To create a changelog fragment:\"\n            echo \"  pip install 'scriv[toml]'\"\n            echo \"  scriv create\"\n            echo \"\"\n            echo \"This is similar to adding a changeset in JavaScript projects.\"\n            echo \"See changelog.d/README.md for more information.\"\n            # Note: This is a warning, not a failure, to allow flexibility\n            # Change 'exit 0' to 'exit 1' to make it required\n            exit 0\n          fi\n\n          echo \"✓ Changelog check passed\"\n\n  # === LINT AND FORMAT CHECK ===\n  lint:\n    name: Lint and Format Check\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes]\n    if: |\n      github.event_name == 'push' ||\n      github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.py-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.tests-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.docs-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.package-changed == 'true' ||\n      needs.detect-changes.outputs.workflow-changed == 'true'\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install -e \".[dev]\"\n\n      - name: Run Ruff linting\n        run: ruff check .\n\n      - name: Check Ruff formatting\n        run: ruff format --check .\n\n      - name: Run mypy\n        run: mypy src\n\n      - name: Check file size limit\n        run: python scripts/check_file_size.py\n\n  # === TEST ===\n  test:\n    name: Test (Python ${{ matrix.python-version }} on ${{ matrix.os }})\n    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}\n    needs: [detect-changes, changelog]\n    # Run if: push event, OR changelog succeeded, OR changelog was skipped (docs-only PR)\n    if: always() && (github.event_name == 'push' || github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' || needs.changelog.result == 'success' || needs.changelog.result == 'skipped')\n    strategy:\n      fail-fast: false\n      matrix:\n        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]\n        python-version: ['3.13']\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install -e \".[dev]\"\n\n      - name: Run tests\n        run: pytest tests/ -v --cov=src --cov-report=xml --cov-report=term\n\n      - name: Upload coverage to Codecov\n        if: matrix.os == 'ubuntu-latest' && matrix.python-version == '3.13'\n        uses: codecov/codecov-action@v4\n        with:\n          file: ./python/coverage.xml\n          fail_ci_if_error: false\n          flags: python\n\n  # === BUILD PACKAGE ===\n  build:\n    name: Build Package\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    needs: [detect-changes, lint, test]\n    # Run if: push/dispatch event, OR lint/test succeeded, OR lint/test were skipped (docs-only PR)\n    if: |\n      always() && (\n        github.event_name == 'push' ||\n        github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch' ||\n        (\n          (needs.lint.result == 'success' || needs.lint.result == 'skipped') &&\n          (needs.test.result == 'success' || needs.test.result == 'skipped')\n        )\n      )\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install build dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install build twine\n\n      - name: Build package\n        run: python -m build\n\n      - name: Check package\n        run: twine check dist/*\n\n      - name: Upload artifacts\n        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4\n        with:\n          name: python-dist\n          path: python/dist/\n\n  # === AUTO RELEASE - triggers on push to main if version changed ===\n  auto-release:\n    name: Auto Release\n    needs: [lint, test, build]\n    if: github.event_name == 'push' && github.ref == 'refs/heads/main'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install build twine\n\n      - name: Check if version changed\n        id: version_check\n        run: |\n          # Get current version from pyproject.toml\n          CURRENT_VERSION=$(grep -Po '(?<=^version = \")[^\"]*' pyproject.toml)\n          echo \"current_version=$CURRENT_VERSION\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n\n          # Check if tag exists (with python prefix to differentiate from JS/Rust)\n          if git rev-parse \"python-v$CURRENT_VERSION\" >/dev/null 2>&1; then\n            echo \"Tag python-v$CURRENT_VERSION already exists, skipping release\"\n            echo \"should_release=false\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          else\n            echo \"New version detected: $CURRENT_VERSION\"\n            echo \"should_release=true\" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT\n          fi\n\n      - name: Download artifacts\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4\n        with:\n          name: python-dist\n          path: python/dist/\n\n      - name: Publish to PyPI\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1\n        with:\n          packages-dir: python/dist/\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.version_check.outputs.should_release == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          python scripts/create_github_release.py \\\n            --version \"${{ steps.version_check.outputs.current_version }}\" \\\n            --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\" \\\n            --tag-prefix \"python-v\"\n\n  # === MANUAL RELEASE - triggered via workflow_dispatch ===\n  manual-release:\n    name: Manual Release\n    needs: [lint, test, build]\n    if: github.event_name == 'workflow_dispatch'\n    runs-on: ubuntu-latest\n    permissions:\n      contents: write\n      id-token: write\n    steps:\n      - uses: actions/checkout@v4\n        with:\n          fetch-depth: 0\n          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n\n      - name: Setup Python\n        uses: actions/setup-python@v5\n        with:\n          python-version: '3.13'\n\n      - name: Install dependencies\n        run: |\n          python -m pip install --upgrade pip\n          pip install build twine \"scriv[toml]\"\n\n      - name: Configure git\n        run: |\n          git config user.name \"github-actions[bot]\"\n          git config user.email \"github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com\"\n\n      - name: Collect changelog fragments\n        run: |\n          # Check if there are any fragments to collect\n          FRAGMENTS=$(find changelog.d -name \"*.md\" ! -name \"README.md\" ! -name \"*.j2\" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)\n          if [ \"$FRAGMENTS\" -gt 0 ]; then\n            echo \"Found $FRAGMENTS changelog fragment(s), collecting...\"\n            scriv collect --version \"${{ github.event.inputs.bump_type }}\"\n          else\n            echo \"No changelog fragments found, skipping collection\"\n          fi\n\n      - name: Version and commit\n        id: version\n        run: |\n          python scripts/version_and_commit.py \\\n            --bump-type \"${{ github.event.inputs.bump_type }}\" \\\n            --description \"${{ github.event.inputs.description }}\"\n\n      - name: Build package\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        run: python -m build\n\n      - name: Check package\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        run: twine check dist/*\n\n      - name: Publish to PyPI\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        uses: pypa/gh-action-pypi-publish@release/v1\n        with:\n          packages-dir: python/dist/\n\n      - name: Create GitHub Release\n        if: steps.version.outputs.version_committed == 'true' || steps.version.outputs.already_released == 'true'\n        env:\n          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}\n        run: |\n          python scripts/create_github_release.py \\\n            --version \"${{ steps.version.outputs.new_version }}\" \\\n            --repository \"${{ github.repository }}\" \\\n            --tag-prefix \"python-v\"\n"

        "content": "#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nDetect code changes for CI/CD pipeline.\n\nThis script detects what types of files have changed between two commits\nand outputs the results for use in GitHub Actions workflow conditions.\n\nKey behavior:\n- For PRs: compares PR head against base branch\n- For pushes: compares HEAD against HEAD^\n- Excludes certain folders and file types from \"code changes\" detection\n\nExcluded from code changes (don't require changelog fragments):\n- Markdown files (*.md) in any folder\n- changelog.d/ folder (changelog metadata)\n- docs/ folder (documentation)\n- experiments/ folder (experimental scripts)\n- examples/ folder (example scripts)\n\nUsage:\n    python scripts/detect_code_changes.py\n\nEnvironment variables (set by GitHub Actions):\n    - GITHUB_EVENT_NAME: 'pull_request' or 'push'\n    - GITHUB_BASE_SHA: Base commit SHA for PR\n    - GITHUB_HEAD_SHA: Head commit SHA for PR\n\nOutputs (written to GITHUB_OUTPUT):\n    - py-changed: 'true' if any .py files changed\n    - tests-changed: 'true' if any tests/ files changed\n    - package-changed: 'true' if pyproject.toml changed\n    - docs-changed: 'true' if any .md files changed\n    - workflow-changed: 'true' if any .github/workflows/ files changed\n    - any-code-changed: 'true' if any code files changed (excludes docs, changelogs, experiments, examples)\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport os\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\n\n\ndef exec_command(command: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"Execute a shell command and return trimmed output.\"\"\"\n    try:\n        result = subprocess.run(\n            command,\n            shell=True,\n            capture_output=True,\n            text=True,\n            check=True,\n        )\n        return result.stdout.strip()\n    except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:\n        print(f\"Error executing command: {command}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        print(f\"stderr: {e.stderr}\", file=sys.stderr)\n        return \"\"\n\n\ndef set_output(name: str, value: str) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Write output to GitHub Actions output file.\"\"\"\n    output_file = os.environ.get(\"GITHUB_OUTPUT\")\n    if output_file:\n        with open(output_file, \"a\") as f:\n            f.write(f\"{name}={value}\\n\")\n    print(f\"{name}={value}\")\n\n\ndef get_changed_files() -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Get the list of changed files between two commits.\"\"\"\n    event_name = os.environ.get(\"GITHUB_EVENT_NAME\", \"local\")\n\n    if event_name == \"pull_request\":\n        base_sha = os.environ.get(\"GITHUB_BASE_SHA\")\n        head_sha = os.environ.get(\"GITHUB_HEAD_SHA\")\n\n        if base_sha and head_sha:\n            print(f\"Comparing PR: {base_sha}...{head_sha}\")\n            try:\n                # Ensure we have the base commit\n                try:\n                    subprocess.run(\n                        f\"git cat-file -e {base_sha}\",\n                        shell=True,\n                        check=True,\n                        capture_output=True,\n                    )\n                except subprocess.CalledProcessError:\n                    print(\"Base commit not available locally, attempting fetch...\")\n                    subprocess.run(\n                        f\"git fetch origin {base_sha}\",\n                        shell=True,\n                        check=False,\n                    )\n\n                output = exec_command(f\"git diff --name-only {base_sha} {head_sha}\")\n                if output:\n                    return [f for f in output.split(\"\\n\") if f]\n            except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"Git diff failed: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    # For push events or fallback\n    print(\"Comparing HEAD^ to HEAD\")\n    try:\n        output = exec_command(\"git diff --name-only HEAD^ HEAD\")\n        if output:\n            return [f for f in output.split(\"\\n\") if f]\n    except Exception:\n        # If HEAD^ doesn't exist (first commit), list all files in HEAD\n        print(\"HEAD^ not available, listing all files in HEAD\")\n        output = exec_command(\"git ls-tree --name-only -r HEAD\")\n        if output:\n            return [f for f in output.split(\"\\n\") if f]\n\n    return []\n\n\ndef is_excluded_from_code_changes(file_path: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a file should be excluded from code changes detection.\"\"\"\n    # Exclude markdown files in any folder\n    if file_path.endswith(\".md\"):\n        return True\n\n    # Exclude specific folders from code changes\n    excluded_folders = [\"changelog.d/\", \"docs/\", \"experiments/\", \"examples/\"]\n\n    for folder in excluded_folders:\n        if file_path.startswith(folder):\n            return True\n\n    return False\n\n\ndef detect_changes() -> None:\n    \"\"\"Main function to detect changes.\"\"\"\n    print(\"Detecting file changes for CI/CD...\\n\")\n\n    changed_files = get_changed_files()\n\n    print(\"Changed files:\")\n    if not changed_files:\n        print(\"  (none)\")\n    else:\n        for file in changed_files:\n            print(f\"  {file}\")\n    print()\n\n    # Detect .py file changes\n    py_changed = any(f.endswith(\".py\") for f in changed_files)\n    set_output(\"py-changed\", \"true\" if py_changed else \"false\")\n\n    # Detect tests/ changes\n    tests_changed = any(f.startswith(\"tests/\") for f in changed_files)\n    set_output(\"tests-changed\", \"true\" if tests_changed else \"false\")\n\n    # Detect pyproject.toml changes\n    package_changed = \"pyproject.toml\" in changed_files\n    set_output(\"package-changed\", \"true\" if package_changed else \"false\")\n\n    # Detect documentation changes (any .md file)\n    docs_changed = any(f.endswith(\".md\") for f in changed_files)\n    set_output(\"docs-changed\", \"true\" if docs_changed else \"false\")\n\n    # Detect workflow changes\n    workflow_changed = any(f.startswith(\".github/workflows/\") for f in changed_files)\n    set_output(\"workflow-changed\", \"true\" if workflow_changed else \"false\")\n\n    # Detect code changes (excluding docs, changelogs, experiments, examples folders, and markdown files)\n    code_changed_files = [\n        f for f in changed_files if not is_excluded_from_code_changes(f)\n    ]\n\n    print(\"\\nFiles considered as code changes:\")\n    if not code_changed_files:\n        print(\"  (none)\")\n    else:\n        for file in code_changed_files:\n            print(f\"  {file}\")\n    print()\n\n    # Check if any code files changed (.py, .toml, .yml, .yaml, or workflow files)\n    import re\n\n    code_pattern = re.compile(r\"\\.(py|toml|yml|yaml)$|\\.github/workflows/\")\n    code_changed = any(code_pattern.search(f) for f in code_changed_files)\n    set_output(\"any-code-changed\", \"true\" if code_changed else \"false\")\n\n    print(\"\\nChange detection completed.\")\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    detect_changes()",

changed_files)\n    set_output(\"py-changed\", \"true\" if py_changed else \"false\")\n\n    # Detect tests/ changes\n    tests_changed = any(f.startswith(\"tests/\") for f in changed_files)\n python/\n    - package-changed: 'true' if pyproject.toml changed in python/\n    - docs-changed: 'true' if any .md files changed in python/\n    - workflow-changed: 'true' if python workflow file changed\n    - any-code-changed: 'true' if any code files changed in python/\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport os\nimport re\nimport subprocess\nimport sys\n\n\ndef f.write(f\"{name}={value}\\n\")\n    print(f\"{name}={value}\")\n\n\ndef get_changed_files() -> list[str]:\n    \"\"\"Get the list of changed files between two commits.\"\"\"\n    event_name =    except Exception as e:\n                print(f\"Git diff failed: {e}\", file=sys.stderr)\n\n    # For push events or fallback\n    print(\"Comparing HEAD^ to HEAD\")\n    try:\n        output = python/\n    tests_changed = any(\n        strip_python_prefix(f).startswith(\"tests/\") for f in changed_files\n    )\n    set_output(\"tests-changed\", \"true\" if tests_changed else \"false\")\n\n annotations\n\nimport sys\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nMAX_LINES = 1000\nFILE_EXTENSIONS = [\".py\"]\nEXCLUDE_PATTERNS = [\n    \"node_modules\",\n    \".venv\",\n    \"venv\",\n    \"env\",\n    \"__pycache__\",\n    \".git\",\n    \"build\",\n    \"dist\",\n    \".eggs\",\n    \"*.egg-info\",\n]\n\n\ndef should_exclude(path: Path, exclude_patterns: list[str]) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a path should be excluded.\n\n    Args:\n        path: Path to check\n        exclude_patterns: List of patterns to exclude\n\n    Returns:\n        True if path should be excluded\n    \"\"\"\n    path_str = str(path)\n    return any(pattern in path_str for pattern in exclude_patterns)\n\n\ndef find_python_files(directory: Path, exclude_patterns: list[str]) -> list[Path]:\n    \"\"\"Recursively find all Python files in a directory.\n\n    Args:\n        directory: Directory to search\n        exclude_patterns: Patterns to exclude\n\n    Returns:\n        List of file paths\n    \"\"\"\n    files = []\n    for path in directory.rglob(\"*\"):\n        if should_exclude(path, exclude_patterns):\n            continue\n        if path.is_file() and path.suffix in FILE_EXTENSIONS:\n            files.append(path)\n    return files\n\n\ndef count_lines(file_path: Path) -> int:\n    \"\"\"Count lines in a file.\n\n    Args:\n        file_path: Path to the file\n\n    Returns:\n        Number of lines\n    \"\"\"\n    return len(file_path.read_text(encoding=\"utf-8\").split(\"\\n\"))\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    \"\"\"Main function.\"\"\"\n    cwd = Path.cwd()\n    print(f\"\\nChecking Python files for maximum {MAX_LINES} lines...\\n\")\n\n    files = find_python_files(cwd, EXCLUDE_PATTERNS)\n    violations = []\n\n    for file in files:\n        line_count = count_lines(file)\n Look for version section (e.g., \"## 1.2.3\" or \"## 1.2.3 - 2024-01-15\")\n    version_pattern = rf\"^## {re.escape(version)}(\\s|$)\"\n    match = re.search(version_pattern, content, re.MULTILINE)\n\n    if not match:\n        print(\n            f\"Warning: Version {version} not found in {changelog_path}\",\n            file=sys.stderr,\n        )\n        return f\"Release {version}\"\n\n    # Extract content until next version section or end of file\n    start = match.end()\n    next_version = re.search(r\"^## \\d+\\.\\d+\\.\\d+\", content[start:], re.MULTILINE)\n\n    if next_version:\n        entry = content[start : start + next_version.start()].strip()\n    else:\n        entry = content[start:].strip()\n\n    return entry if entry else f\"Release {version}\"\n\n\ndef create_release(\n    version: str, repository: str, release_notes: str, prerelease: bool = False\n) -> None:\n    \"\"\"Create a GitHub release using gh CLI.\"\"\"\n    tag = f\"v{version}\"\n\n    print(f\"\\nCreating GitHub release for {tag}...\")\n    print(f\"Repository: {repository}\")\n    print(f\"Prerelease: {prerelease}\")\n    print(f\"\\nRelease notes:\\n{release_notes}\\n\")\n\n    cmd = [\n        \"gh\",\n        \"release\",\n        \"create\",\n        tag,\n        \"--repo\",\n        repository,\n        \"--title\",\n        tag,\n        \"--notes\",\n        release_notes,\n    ]\n\n    if prerelease:\n        cmd.append(\"--prerelease\")\n\n    run_command(cmd)\n    print(f\"\\n✅ GitHub release {tag} created successfully!\")\n\n\ndef main() -> int:\n    \"\"\"Main entry point.\"\"\"\n    parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\n        description=\"Create GitHub release from CHANGELOG.md\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--version\",\n        \"-v\",\n        required=True,\n        help=\"Version to release (e.g., 1.2.3)\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--repository\",\n        \"-r\",\n        required=True,\n        help=\"GitHub repository (owner/repo)\",\n    )\n    parser.add_argument(\n        \"--prerelease\",\n        action=\"store_true\",\n        help=\"Mark as prerelease\",\n    )\n\n    args = parser.parse_args()\n\n    # REPO\n\nExample:\n    python scripts/create_github_release.py --version 1.2.3 --repository owner/repo\n\nEnvironment variables:\n    GH_TOKEN or GITHUB_TOKEN: GitHub token for
